symptom: When it comes to DEP integration with Intune, the topic can seem a bit overwhelming or even frustrating, however with a bit of discussion and documentation you will see that it is a very simple and very reliable process that can aid you in enrolling your Apple products for Mobile Device Management with Microsoft Intune with ease.  This will provide you better control over the enrollment experience and management of the Apple devices in your environment.Articles to be referenced in this advisory:https://deploy.apple.com/https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/ios-enrollhttps://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/apple-mdm-push-certificate-gethttps://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/device-enrollment-program-enroll-iosOn this topic we will start where all good topics, start, prerequisites...The prerequisites for using DEP Enrollment with Intune are as follows:1. Install APN Certificate2. Join the Apple Device Enrollment Program3. Install the DEP TokenSo what is an APN Certificate? It stands for Apple Push Notification, which is a service provided by Apple to allow MDM solutions to manage iOS and Mac devices.This certificate can only be gained from Apple after creating a APN certificate request, but where do you create this request? Intune to the answer here :)1. Open the Intune Admin Portal2. Navigate to Device enrollment > Apple Enrollment > Apple MDM Push CertificateOnce here you will want to click on the Download the APN certificate request, what this does is create a .CSR file that is formatted for Apple.You will save this file which will be used to request a trust relationship certificate from Apple to allow Intune to talk to Apple devices and Manage them.Once you have this saved, you will need to head to the Apple website to get the APN certificate, the URL for Apple site is:https://idmsa.apple.com/IDMSWebAuth/login?appIdKey=3fbfc9ad8dfedeb78be1d37f6458e72adc3160d1ad5b323a9e5c5eb2f8e7e3e2&rv=2This is for the Apple Push Certificates Portal, in order to use this you will need to sign in with a valid Apple ID, this ID will need to be used any time you update the certificate in the future as well.I recommend creating a service account for your company that can safely be stored and utilized by your admins who manage the Intune system vs a personal ID.Once you sign in, you will create a new certificate by the simple following steps:1. Click the Create a Certificate button (it will be green and in the top right)2. Agree to the Terms of Use3. Click Browse and select the .CSR file you created in the Intune Portal and then click Upload4. You will be prompted for saving a .json file, just click Cancel as this is a Java script and not needed for Intune5. Refresh the page with the power of F56. You will then be taken back to the Certificates for Third-Party Servers list7. Select the Certificate you just created (will have the latest expiration date) by clicking the Download button next to itOnce you have the APN cert you now can close the Apple Push Certificates Portal and switch back to the Intune SilverLight (Classic) Portal.Once there its time to add the certificate you just created by the following actions:Enter the Apple ID used to create your Apple MDM push certificateRecord this ID as a reminder for when you need to renew this certificate.Browse to your Apple MDM push certificate to uploadGo to the certificate (.pem) file, choose Open, and then choose Upload. That is it, that easy you are now ready able to enroll and manage iOS Devices, but what does this do in the mechanics area?What occurs is when you enroll an iOS device via the Company Portal or the Company Portal website, is that the cert is then checked by the iOS or Mac device and installed on the device to allow Intune to be trusted as a management source.When you enroll the device, it checks the hardware and sees that it is a Apple device, it then checks the telemetry for Intune and sees that there is an APN cert available, prompts the person enrolling the device to install the cert in order to trust Intune.Once installed the device will accept configuration policies, compliance policies, profiles and more from Intune as it would from an Apple Configurator.You can provide y our end users the following articles for enrollment:https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune-user-help/enroll-your-device-in-intune-ioshttps://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune-user-help/enroll-your-device-in-intune-macosNow, what if you do not wish to have your end users go through this process, what if you wish to make it easier and allow the customer to get their new iOS device from you brand new in the box and untouched?You want them to open the device, power it on and walk through the initial setup which enrolls it with ease at that time and they are managed from the first second of use? Well then we are now cooking with DEP gas!So this leads me into the next discussion, which is the heart and soul of why we are having this advisory and why you are looking at using Intune with DEP for management integration.To first get things going with DEP, we need to first sign up for the Apple Deployment Program, by visiting: https://idmsa.apple.com/IDMSWebAuth/login.html?disable2SV=true&rv=2&appIdKey=09273cfd6b56a8ce5af52a0153d1d796d364e03a36c6e87ef21e92c77a83ef3f&path=/qforms/web/index/avs&language=US-EN&country=USOnce here you will need to click on Enroll Now, which takes you here: https://deploy.apple.com/qforms/open/register/index/avs and then you will click Enroll next to the Device Enrollment Program.You will need to complete the form that includes the following:* Organization type* Job Title* First name* Last name* Work Email* Work PhoneI would advise here that you again either create another service account or use the same account that you used for the APN certificate, then click Next.You will then be sent an email, this email is to complete your setup, so you will need to check the email that you used to register with, you will want to follow the steps in the Email.These steps currently are to sign in with the account and the temp password in the Email, then to enable two-step verification, then continue enrollment but this may change as it is controlled via Apple.Once you have done this, you will sign into the Apple Deployment Program site, fun is not over... now you need to enter a contact to verify you, once done you will click Next.You will then enter institution details, be forewarned to complete this part you will need to know the following information:* Company name* Company address* Where your devices are purchased from (you can add if not in the drop down list)* Company D-U-N-SNow the last one is usually where the questions come in, what is a D-U-N-S?The D-U-N-S Number is a unique nine-digit number that identifies business entities on a location-specific basis. Assigned and maintained by Dun & Bradstreet (D&B), the D-U-N-S Number is widely used as a standard business identifier.You must provide a D-U-N-S Number registered to your legal entity as part of the enrollment process for the Device Enrollment Program. If you do not know it, you can get it from here: https://iupdate.dnb.com/iUpdate/companylookup.htmOnce you complete the hurdles for enrollment into the Apple Deployment Program, you will return to the Intune SilverLight (Classic) Portal to get an encryption key, which is needed to set up the trust between Apple's DEP and Microsoft's Intune.You do this by the following steps:1. Open the Intune Admin portal2. Navigate to Device enrollment > Apple enrollment > Enrollment Program Tokens > AddOnce here you will click the Download Your Public Key button, this will prompt you to save a .pem (encryption key) file locally.Now you will go back to the Device Enrollment Program Portal and sign in with the account you just created during your enrollment to the program, please note this ID is crucial as it will be needed to update\renew the DEP token.In the DEP Portal you would do the following steps:1. Navigate to Device Enrollment Program->Manage Servers2. Click Add MDM Server3. Enter the MDM Server name and click Next (please note this is just a reference name for the server)4. Click Choose file on the dialog that opens, choose the .pem file you saved and click Next5. You will be presented with a server token, it will be a .p7m format, save this and choose done.You will switch back over to the Intune Admin Portal and then add the .p7m token you just saved from the DEP site by doing the following actions:1. Open the Intune Admin Portal2. Navigate to Device enrollment > Apple enrollment > Enrollment Program TokensOnce here, in the Apple Token box you will click Open and browse to the .p7m file and enter your Apple ID and click Upload, that is it for the connectivity between the two but before you can start using it, you need to create a Corporate Device Enrollment Policy. What is this policy for? In short it tells the devices how to behave during the Apple Setup Assistant when the device first starts, how this works is as follows for the mechanics:1. Apple device is turned on2. Apple device starts the Apple Setup Assistant3. During this process it checks the device against the apple registration and will see that the device has been registered with the DEP program4. This will prompt the end user to sign in with their UPN, this will be processed on the back end by Apple which will find the MDM Server information and deliver the Enrollment Policy to the device before continuing the Apple Setup Assistant.5. The Setup assistant will then incorporate the settings in the Enrollment Policy to the device to continue.So as you can see setting this policy up is a must prior to enrolling the devices via the DEP program, to configure this policy you do the following actions:1. Open the Intune Admin Portal2. Navigate to Device enrollment > Apple Enrollment > Enrollment program tokens3. Select a token, choose Profiles > Create profile > iOSYou will configure the following to be used during the enrollment \ Apple Setup Assistant:* Specify if there will be user Affinity or not for the device* Assign the device to a specific group in Intune* Configure the DEP Program Settings, which allow you to configure the following choices:Department - Appears when users tap About Configuration during activationSupport phone number - Appears when the user clicks the Need Help button during activationPreparation mode - Set during activation and cannot be changed without factory resetting the device:Unsupervised - Limited management capabilitiesSupervised - Enables more management options and disables Activation Lock by defaultLock enrollment profile to device - Set during activation and cannot be changed without a factory resetDisable - Allows the management profile to be removed from the Settings menuEnable - (Requires Preparation Mode = Supervised) Disables the iOS Settings menu option to remove the management profileSetup Assistant Options - These optional settings can be set up later in the iOS Settings menu.Passcode - Prompt for passcode during activation. Always require a passcode unless the device will be secured or have access controlled in some other manner (that is, kiosk mode that restricts the device to one app)Location Services - If enabled, Setup Assistant prompts for the service during activationRestore - If enabled, Setup Assistant prompts for iCloud backup during activationApple ID - If enabled, iOS will prompt users for an Apple ID when Intune attempts to install an app without an ID. An Apple ID is required to download iOS App Store apps, including those installed by Intune.Terms and Conditions - If enabled, Setup Assistant prompts users to accept Apple's terms and conditions during activationTouch ID - If enabled, Setup Assistant prompts for this service during activationApple Pay - If enabled, Setup Assistant prompts for this service during activationZoom - If enabled, Setup Assistant prompts for this service during activationSiri - If enabled, Setup Assistant prompts for this service during activationSend diagnostic data to Apple - If enabled, Setup Assistant prompts for this service during activationEnable additional Apple Configurator management - Set to Disallow to prevent syncing files with iTunes or management via Apple Configurator. It's a good idea to choose Disallow, export further configurations from Apple Configurator, and then deploy as a Custom iOS configuration profile via Intune instead of using this setting to allow manual deployment with or without a certificate.Disallow - Prevents the device from communicating via USB (disables pairing)Allow - Allows a device to communicate via USB connection for any PC or MacRequire certificate - Allows pairing with a Mac with a certificate imported to the enrollment profileOnce you finish setting this policy up, you will save it and then assign it to devices when you assign it you can set that you wish it to go to all devices.At this point you are ready to start assigning DEP devices for Management, excited yet? We are so close to your first out of box experience!To do this you will need to head over to the Apple Device Enrollment Program Portal, sign in with the ID you used earlier, then follow the steps:1. Navigate to Deployment Program->Device Enrollment Program->Manage Devices2. Here you can provide device information through the following options:* Serial Number* Order Number* Upload a CSV FileYou then choose the MDM Server you wish to assign to these devices, in this case the Intune server you set up earlier.Once you have this done you will want to Sync the DEP managed devices to Intune so they are showing up in the DB and ready to rock.To sync the DEP and Intune services, its pretty simple and can be done any time but please note that Apple limits that a FULL sync can be run no more than once every seven days.1. Open the Intune Admin Portal2. choose Device enrollment > Apple Enrollment > Enrollment program tokens > choose a token in the list > Devices > Sync.Assign an enrollment profile to devicesYou must assign an enrollment program profile to devices before they can enroll. NoteYou can also assign serial numbers to profiles from the Apple Serial Numbers blade.In Intune in the Azure portal, choose Device enrollment > Apple Enrollment > Enrollment program tokens > choose a token in the list.Choose Devices > choose devices in the list > Assign profile.Under Assign profile, choose a profile for the devices > Assign.Assign a default profileYou can pick a default profile to be applied to all devices enrolling with a specific token.In Intune in the Azure portal, choose Device enrollment > Apple Enrollment > Enrollment program tokens > choose a token in the list.Choose Set Default Profile, choose a profile in the drop-down list, and then choose Save. This profile will be applied to all devices that enroll with the token.Please note that until the device has enrolled the "State" will read as Not-Contacted until the device is powered on and runs the Setup Assistant to enroll.Your corporate-owned devices can now be distributed to users. When an iOS device is turned on it will be enrolled for management by Intune. The user device limit applies to DEP-managed devices.
This article describes how to add the Intune-enlightened apps that are in the the Intune Gallery. Examples and customer ready samples are included for the Azure portal (Ibiza).
Windows 10 devices are not reporting  status for Antivirus client to Intune Admin ConsoleIntune Endpoint Protection client was not removed
rootCause: When upgrading from Windows 8 to Windows 10 the Intune Endpoint Protection Client does not get removedWindows Defender is the only antivirus client on Windows 10 that will report status back to Intune Admin Console
solution:
To remove the Intune Endpoint Protection Client:1. Create a .cmd
file on a machine that is having the problem named EPUninstall.cmd

 

2. Edit the empty
.cmd file and add this content:

wmic product where
name="Microsoft Endpoint Protection Management Components" call
uninstall

wmic product where
name="Microsoft Security Client" call uninstall

wmic product where
name="Microsoft Intune Endpoint Protection Agent" call uninstall

wmic product where
name="Windows Endpoint Protection Management Components" call
uninstall

wmic product where
name="Windows Security Client" call uninstall

wmic product where
name="Windows Intune Endpoint Protection Agent" call uninstall

 

3. Save the file.

 

4. Open an elevated
(admin) command prompt.

 

5. Navigate to the
Path where the file was created. 

 

6. Run the file from
the cmd prompt.

 7. If step 6 does not
remove the install binaries under %windir%\Program Files\Microsoft Security
Client. Attempt to uninstall the listed products from step 2 if present
using the Uninstall
Troubleshooter

8. Reboot

 

9. The installed
binaries will be removed from %windir%\Program Files\Microsoft Security
Client and the Endpoint Protection icon under the taskbar will be removed
as well. The default
Windows Defender client should take over and report back status to Intune

Section 3: Deploying
the Device Configuration Profile in Microsoft Intune (Ibiza Portal)

The process below will go through the steps of deploying
your newly created Device Configuration profile within Microsoft Intune to
enable the telemetry functionality required to successfully collect information
for the Update Compliance and Upgrade Readiness Solutions offered by the
Microsoft Operations Management Suite (OMS).

1.      
Login to the Microsoft Intune Ibiza Portal: https://portal.azure.com/#blade/Microsoft_Intune_DeviceSettings/ExtensionLandingBlade/overview


2.      
Navigate to Device configuration

3.      
In the Device Configuration � Profiles Screen
Click on the Newly Created Device configuration profile.

4.      
Under the Manage Field Click Assignments



5.      
Click the Include Tab and then Select Groups to
Include


6.      
Select a Previously Created Device Group from
Azure Active Directory Premium



7.      
Click Save to Deploy

 

Section 4:

To confirm the
deployment setting were successfully placed on a device that was targeting to
the Deployment there are a few areas one can check.

 

From the
Microsoft Intune Console:

After Deployment to check the status of the
Device Configuration go back to the Device Configuration � Profiles and Select
the Profile Created to Open the Properties


2.      
Click on Monitor/Device Status



3.      
This will show a list of devices and their Deployment
Status



 

 

 

On a PC that
was targeted with the deployment:

1.      
Check
the following Registry Entry:
HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Datacollection for
AllowTelemtry and CommercialId.   These
should both match what you deployed.

 



 

In the
Microsoft Operations Management Suite (OMS)

Login
to Microsoft Operations Management Suite (OMS): https://login.mms.microsoft.com/signin.aspx?ref=ms_mms

Confirm
that you have received results from a PC. 
Note this can take up to 48 hours.



 

4.

on the screen above will give you a breakout of the telemetry data:



 

 




 

 

 

Please note the
following from: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/deployment/upgrade/upgrade-readiness-get-started#enable-data-sharing-between-your-organization-and-upgrade-analytics

 

�After data is sent from computers to Microsoft, it
generally takes 48 hours for the data to populate in Upgrade Readiness. The
compatibility update KB takes several minutes to run. If the KB does not get a
chance to finish running or if the computers are inaccessible (turned off or
sleeping for example), data will take longer to populate in Upgrade Readiness.
For this reason, you can expect most your computers to be populated in OMS in
about 1-2 weeks after deploying the KB and configuration to user computers.�

 

The task that collects the information for this is called
the Microsoft Compatibility Appraiser (Windows Compat Appraiser) and
can get found under Task Scheduler/Application Experience.  From there the
customer can run this manually to hopefully speed up the reporting back of
data.

symptom: After MDM Authority Reset from Intune to SCCM DEP pre-enrolled devices are showing in Intune vs SCCM
rootCause: APN Certificate, DEP Token and MDM Server configuration still in place for old MDM Authority and not set up correctly for the new MDM Authority.
solution: This will walk you through clean up and the re-configuration to set up clean to assure things are correct after the MDM Authority ResetPHASE 1 CLEAN UP:[DEP Portal]1. Open the DEP Portal2. Login with your Apple ID, you used to set up the DEP Portal3. Navigate to Deployment Program->Device Enrollment Program->Manage Devices4. Remove any devices that are currently imported5. Navigate to Device Enrollment Program->Manage Servers6. Remove the MDM Servers so that there are none listed for Intune or SCCM[Intune Portal]1. Open the Intune Portal2. Navigate to Admin->Mobile Device Management->iOS and Mac OS X->Device Enrollment Program3. Remove any DEP Token present in the Intune Portal4. Navigate to Admin->Mobile Device Management->iOS and Mac OS X->Upload an APN Certificate5. Remove any APN Certificates present[ConfigMgr Console]1. Open the ConfigMgr Console2. Navigate to Administration->Cloud Services->Microsoft Intune Subscriptions->Configure Platforms->iOS3. On the APN Certificate Tab, uncheck the Enable iOS and Mac OS X enrollment box4. On the Apple Device Enrollment Program tab, uncheck the Enable Device Enrollment Program box5. Click Apply and then OkPHASE 2 RECONFIGURATION:[APN Certificate]1. Open the ConfigMgr Consolea. Navigate to Administration->Cloud Services->Microsoft Intuneb. Click the Create APNs certificate request button in the ribbonc. Click Browse and set the file name to 51117_APN_Request.csr and click Saved. Click Download2. Open the Apple Push Certificate Portala. Login with your Apple IDb. Click the Create a Certificate button (it will be green and in the top right)c. Agree to the Terms of Used. Click Browse and select the .CSR file you created in the Intune Portal and then click Uploade. You will be prompted for saving a .json file, just click Cancel as this is a Java script and not needed for Intunef. Refresh the page with the power of F5g. You will then be taken back to the Certificates for Third-Party Servers listh. Select the Certificate you just created (will have the latest expiration date) by clicking the Download button next to it3. Open the ConfigMgr Consolea. Navigate to Administration->Cloud Services->Microsoft Intune Subscriptions->Configure Platforms->iOSb. On the APN Certificate Tab, check the Enable iOS and Mac OS X enrollment boxc. Click Browse next to APN Certificate and browse to the certificate you downloaded from the Apple Push Certificate Portald. Click Ok and then Apply and OK again[DEP Token]1. Open the ConfigMgr Consolea. Navigate to Administration->Cloud Services->Microsoft Intuneb. Click the Create DEP Token Request button in the ribbonc. Click Browse and set the file name to 51117_DEP_Request.pem and click Saved. Click Download2. Open the Apple Device Enrollment Program Portala. Navigate to Device Enrollment Program->Manage Serversb. Click Add MDM Serverc. Enter the MDM Server name and click Next (please note this is just a reference name for the server)d. Click Choose file on the dialog that opens, choose the .pem file you saved and click Nexte. You will be presented with a server token, it will be a .p7m format, save this and choose done3. Open the ConfigMgr Consolea. Navigate to Administration->Cloud Services->Microsoft Intune Subscriptions->Configure Platforms->iOSb. On the Apple Device Enrollment Program tab, check the Enable Device Enrollment Program boxc. Click Browse and select the .p7m file you downloaded from the DEP Portald. Click Open and then Apply and OK4. Open the Apple Device Enrollment Program Portala. Navigate to Deployment Program->Device Enrollment Program->Manage Devicesb. Import the device information from a CSV file5. Perform Sync from ConfigMgr with DEPa. Open the ConfigMgr Consoleb. Navigate to Assets and Compliance->All Corporate-owned Devices->Predeclared Devicesc. Click DEP Sync on the ribbond. Wait 10 minutese. Refresh the view

Engineering has started migrating customers who are actively using those features to the new experience in Azure, one MSU at a time over the next week as documented under the section �Secondary migration required for select capabilities� in the link below:Known issues in Microsoft Intunehttps://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/known-issuesIf customers open a support case, we can inform them that their migration will occur in the next week or so and close their case. If they have an urgent request to migrate, you can work with your TA/Lead to flight them, but generally we should just have the customer wait. When completed, customers will be notified in their Office Message Center with the following post along with some links:Please feel free to e-mail the Intune Flighting Team at intunecssflighting@microsoft.com if you have any questions/concerns. *********************************** **** ************************************When selecting DEP/VPP/DEM features in the Intune on Azure portal customers receive the following message:
rootCause: After migration to the Azure Portal customers will now need to be manually flighted for VPP/DEP/DEM for these features to light up in the Intune on Azure Portal
solution: Please follow the process below to request the flighitng for these features: 1) Ensure that the SR support topic is set to: a. Intune Insiders\Flighiting Request 2) Once you have confirmed that you have the correct tenant that needs to be flighted, ensure that the tenant�s MDM Authority is set to Intune (or coexistence). Tenants that have the MDM Authority set to SCCM do not need to be flighted for these features 3) Verify that tenant has been migrated by confirming the following Flighting Tag in VP -> Intune Account Diagnostic: AADGroupTargetingForUsersAndDevices Please note: We cannot proceed with VPP/DEP/DEM flighting if the tenant does not have the flighting Tag AADGroupTargetingForUsersAndDevices as it indicates that the tenant is not migrated to the Intune on Azure. Please inform the customer that they must wait for the tenant migration to Intune on Azure first before these features can be flighted. Share the following link with the customer to ensure that they do not have any migration blockers: https://blogs.technet.microsoft.com/intunesupport/2017/05/17/intune-migration-blockers-for-grouping-targeting 4) Send the following template to the customer and ensure that you get consent from an e-mail registered as Global Administrator for the tenant: Please note: Replace all the areas highlighted in GRAY in the template below with the appropriate values Hi <Customer Name>, Intune requires a permanent change to enable managing several features in Azure. This change will simultaneously disable managing the following features in the Intune classic portal: Corporate Device Enrollment Profiles Apple Device Enrollment Program Corporate Pre-enrolled devices by iOS serial number group Device Enrollment Managers Apple Volume Purchase Program NOTE: Once the features are disabled in the Intune classic portal it may take up to 24 hours before these features in the Azure portal are enabled. Please avoid modifying the DEP/VPP tokens and changing/creating DEP profiles, VPP users or VPP app assignments until support has notified you via email that the migration is completed. IMPORTANT NOTE: The Azure portal does not support a �default� Corporate Device Enrollment profile for Apple Device Enrollment Program (DEP) devices. This functionality, was previously discontinued to prevent unintentional profile assignment. When DEP serial numbers sync in the Azure portal, no Corporate Device Enrollment profile is assigned. You must assign a profile before activating DEP devices. NOTE: After migration you may still see the VPP token menu item disabled in the Azure portal. This is expected. To access the VPP token setup in the Azure Portal, you can navigate to the old page in the classic console and it will link you directly to the Azure token page, or you can bookmark this link https://aka.ms/iosvpptokenibizape which will grant you direct access to the page. The Licensed Apps menu item will show you the VPP apps you currently have, and that should be enabled. Do we have your authorization to proceed with this change on tenant < Enter Tenant ID (for example: intune.onmicrosoft.com) to be flighted > ? (Please reply with an e-mail address that is registered as a Global Administration in your tenant) 5) Set the SR Wait State to �Solution Delivered � Pending Customer Confirmation� <After global administrator has provided authorization/consent, please proceed with remaining steps below> 6) Create a Tech Review Task in MSSOLVE with the following information: Task Title: VPP/DEP/DEM Flighting Request Log Notes: Tenant ID: <the domain used to login to service (IE intune.onmicrosoft.com)> Context ID: <Company ID FROM VIEWPOINT> AccountID: <GUID FROM VIEWPOINT/GATEWAY TOOL> MSUID: <MSUID FROM VIEWPOINT/GATEWAY TOOL> Authorization from GA: Y/N Example: Tenantid: mytest.onmicrosoft.com ContextID: 74537d1e-19e1-4555-bfcb-c6d8bace6725 AccountID: 22abe0ab-8dcf-4058-8cfd-2a03fc07f519 MSU: MSUA05 Authorization from GA: Y/N 7) Email the template to intunecssflighting@microsoft.com and Transfer the task to the Intune Flighting Request queue in MSSolve 8) Once you receive notification that flighting request is complete, e-mail the customer indicating that the migration has been initiated: Hi <Customer Name>, The feature migration process has been initiated and it can take up to 24 hours for the process to complete. Please avoid modifying the DEP/VPP tokens and changing/creating DEP profiles, VPP users or VPP app assignments until the process is complete. A feature has finished migration once it can be accessed in the Intune on Azure portal.

rootCause: The error occurs when the Company Portal app checks our certificates on ADFS to see if the host is trustworthy. The issue is that Android handles cert chaining in a way somewhat differently from iOS and Windows Phone. In short, Android needs all certs in the chain to be present on the ADFS Server, where iOS would intelligently lookup the Cert Signer.
solution: Import the certs up the chain into the intermediate store on the ADFS Proxy Servers. So, launch the MMC and add the Certificates Snapin for the Local Computer on your ADFS Server. Find the cert your ADFS Service is using (likely issued to adfs.yourcompany.com), and view its parent certificate. Move a copy of the parent cert, (in my case, Symantec) into the Computer\Intermediate Certification Authorities\Certificates store. This part is CRUCIAL! Next, move copies of your ADFS, ADFS Decrypting, and ADFS Signing Certs into the Personal Storefor the ADFS Service. Finally, restart the ADFS servers, because restarting the service alone is not enough. From <https://foxdeploy.com/2015/12/10/solved-ios-devices-can-connect-via-intune-but-not-android/>

symptom: When validating a NDES configuration, running NDESLongURLValidator seems to hang at Step 3 (NDESLongUrlValidatorStep3of3). If you wait long enough it will eventually complete and log a timeout error similar to the following in NDESLongURLValidator.txt: NDESLongUrlValidator.exe Information: 0 : Retrieving the following URL: https://ndes.contoso.com/certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll?operation=PKIOperation&message=<FAKE CHALLENGE WILL GO HERE> NDESLongUrlValidator.exe Error: 0 : An error occurred: System.Net.WebException: The operation has timed out at System.Net.HttpWebRequest.GetResponse() at NDESLongUrlValidator.Program.PkiOperation(String ndesurl, String fakeChallenge) at NDESLongUrlValidator.Program.Main(String[] args) NDESLongUrlValidator.exe Information: 0 : Exiting NDESLongUrlValidator... Note that Step 1 and Step 2 complete successfully.
rootCause: This problem can occur if there is a Windows Application Proxy (WAP) between the client and the NDES server, and the WAP server does not have the necessary prerequisite updates installed.
solution: To resolve this problem, install the December 2014 update rollup or the update in KB3011135 on the WAP server.

symptom: This article explains how to collect SysDump logging from a Samsung device running Android OS.
solution: Open the Phone app. Type *#9900# This will open the SysDump menu. Tap DEBUG LEVEL DISABLED/LOW. Select MID. Repro the issue and note down timestamp. Open the Phone app. Type *#9900# In the SysDump menu, tap COPY TO SDCARD (INCLUDE CP RAMDUMP). The log file will be labelled honoring the format dumpState_Guid_Date.log and it will be saved to a folder called log on your device. You can access the folder either via the SAMSUNG My Files app, or, through Windows Explorer when USB connected.

symptom: In Intune Mobile Application Management Policies, there are several options to "Select which storage services corporate data can be saved to" under the "Prevent Save As" parameter. One of these options is Local Storage.If "Local Storage" is selected, users still cannot save to the local device.
rootCause: By Design.
solution: Although this option is listed in the Intune service, it actually requires the apps that are targeted to implement this feature in the Intune SDK. This is an app side fix and there are no actions from the Intune service/policies that will correct this if it is not enabled in the targeted app.

symptom: What is DataAlert? A Telecom Expense Management (TEM) application integrated into Intune to provide mobile data management capabilities for companies. The app monitors telecom plan usage on the device

Does Intune support dual-SIM or SIM card changes? Intune does not support dual-SIM or SIM card changes today. So if a customer is swapping SIMs in a managed device, Intune will pick that up, and Saaswedo will also pick it up, however both lines need to exist in Saaswedo with associated plans for MDM-managed TEM to still work. In this case the Intune system will accept traffic from Saaswedo, so Saaswedo will assume success, but the Background task will log a message that either a TEMDevice or Intune Device cannot be found.
When setting up Data Alert and Intune integration on iOS and Android devices, users receive an ADAL-6 error via the DataAlert app.
rootCause: Still under investigation.
solution: Check the following Service Principal with the AAD Service Principal Policy Diagnostic in ViewPoint. The ServicePrincipal displays a Guid.Empty value (000000-0000) for groups, which should not be. When this comes back if Groups is Guid.Empty (all 0s), then we need to run an ops procedure. Create an ICM and reference that this as a sample ops procedure needs to be run.

symptom: When deploying a wifi profile to an Android device, the PFX certificate never authenticates the profile.
rootCause: The Wifi profile is not authenticating against the PFX certificate because it does not recognize it
solution: Navigate to manage.microsoft.com, click on the Policy node and edit the configuration policy for the PFX Certificate. Validate the configuration contains Client Authentication so the Company Portal will NOT exclude the Certificate. Delete the old WiFi profile, create a new one and deploy it to the Intune AFW Group. Test by syncing the security policy and then checking compliance.

symptom: Android device enrollment fails or shows device as rooted. Common fixes: 1) Turn off Developer Options within device settings, as this can be evaluated as rooted since it changes factory settings. 2) Load factory image and perform factory reset.
Customer wants to get setup on the Intune Canary program or needs SAW Actions overview. This content has been moved to the Intune CSS Wiki.

Microsoft/Checkpoint Support Collaboration and Escalation Process: When an issue requires attention of Checkpoint Support, the Customer has three options to join Checkpoint support (telephone, web request, or live chat). The customer should be on the call with Checkpoint support. Microsoft engineer can always join the call but cannot contact Checkpoint directly unless defined as support contact within the customer account.
Archived
Apps purchased with the Apple Volume Purchase Program are not appearing in the Intune Console.
solution: Cause 1 - By DesignCause 2- Not Supported at this time.  On the Intune side is that once you uploaded the VPP token, Intune queries Apple and Apple tells that it has 2 VPP apps associated with the token/account, but when Intune tries to query Apple for the 2 VPP apps, Apple responds back saying that it does not know anything about the apps.Intune queries the public URL for Apple using this format: https://itunes.apple.com/lookup?id=<applicationID>&cc=us  and https://itunes.apple.com/lookup?id=<applicationID>&cc=us. Custom B2B apps are not publicly available and the app metadata cannot be found, thus the admin cannot see any app information. When going to the app lookup urls above for a particular app through a browser, save the response and look at the contents of the response. Both these URLs come up with an empty �set� of properties, however if you use the Product ID of an app (like Facebook) instead, you will see the response contain real values. The link for Facebook is: https://itunes.apple.com/lookup?id=284882215&cc=us.

When creating a dynamic group in the Intune on Azure portal using the dynamic membership rule below, it does not show any members even though several Corporate devices exist under All devices:(device.deviceOwnership -eq "Corporate")
rootCause: The correct value for deviceOwership in Azure is "company" not "Corporate" as can be seen using the Intune Graph API example below:https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/managedDevices/8278f275-19d1-4aa6-9fa9-2d06526221b6
solution: In order to resolve the issue, follow the steps below:1) Ensure that the device is indeed properly classified as a Corporate device in Intune by going to Intune on Azure blade -> Devices -> All Devices -> Select one of the impacted devices then "Properties" and verify that the "Device ownership" is indeed set to "Corporate"2) Update the dynamic membership rule of the respective dynamic group with the following:(device.deviceOwnership -eq "company")

symptom: When attempting to launch the Skype for Business client app, after you enter valid credentials and attempt to login, the app fails with the following error:"The requested operation was blocked by policy"This can occur in the following scenario:The user is using Skype for Business on-premises clients.The user�s mailbox is homed in Exchange Online.Conditional Access policies for Intune or Azure AD are being applied.
rootCause: This is by design. Conditional Access policies are not supported for Skype for Business on-prem clients when the user�s mailbox is homed in Exchange Online.
solution: Here are three potential workarounds to this problem:1. Disable the Azure AD and/or Intune Conditional Access policies.2. Use Skype for Business Online.3. Make sure users always launch the Outlook client before launching Skype for Business.Note that there may be other workarounds than the ones listed above. If you find another workaround, click here to add it to the article directly.

When an iPhone user with a paired Apple watch enrolls their phone into Intune, reminders for scheduled tasks no longer appear on the watch.
rootCause: This can occur if there is an iOS Device Restriction configuration profile assigned to the user and Viewing corporate documents in unmanaged apps set to Block.
solution: To work around this problem, set Viewing corporate documents in unmanaged apps to Not configured. This can be found in Azure Intune portal -> Device configuration -> <profileName> -> Properties -> Settings -> App Store, Doc Viewing, Gaming.Once the profile is updated and that device syncs (checks compliance) then the reminders for scheduled tasks should reappear on the paired watch.

symptom: https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/help/3090505 References the preferred methods to obtain IOS Console logs.During troubleshooting, an engineer finds that he needs the iOS Console (XCode) logs to further investigate an issue on iOS devices. What are Xcode logs? On MacOS/iOS, the operating system maintains a log of every process that runs from second to second. On a Mac, these logs can be viewed by running the Console app, however on iOS, we need to use Xcode to view them, as no app exists within iOS to easily read them. (You wouldn�t want to anyways). How do I get Xcode logs? Getting the logs is a fairly straightforward process. First and foremost, you will need a Mac running the latest version of MacOS and Xcode. Open Xcode and connect your iOS device to your Mac using a cable. On your iOS device, make sure to select �Trust This Computer� On the Menu Bar, select Window>Devices Select your iOS device from the list to the left. Xcode will then take some time to load all your phones information. After it�s loaded, repro the issue that you are experiencing. Once complete, select the download button in the lower right corner of the Devices window. (If you do not see this button, you may have the console section hidden, you can show it by clicking the �up� arrow found directly to the right of the �filter� box at the bottom of the devices list). Below is a screenshot from my MacBook of what things will look like: Now to read the Xcode logs, I�ve found that the easiest way is to open them up using TextAnalysisTool.NET, and then filtering by �mdmd� (the core MDM process in iOS) to start with. As I mentioned earlier, Xcode logs are essentially a console dump of every running process on the iOS device, so you�ll get a ton of noise from other apps running in the background that have nothing to do with what you�re troubleshooting. The format of the logs is [Date][Device Name][Process Name][PID][Message], so oftentimes narrowing things down to what process is being called and then filtering by that process is the most efficient way to look through them. Some things worth noting: Xcode 7 does NOT support iOS 10+. If customers are having trouble getting their iOS devices to show up in Xcode, make sure they�re running the latest version. They can update by going to the App Store and selecting their installed apps, then clicking Update Now. Reproing the issue while capturing the logs is a must, Xcode grabs the logs in real time so if the issue isn�t reproed you won�t learn anything useful from the logs. UPDATE � if you are running the latest version of OSX, MacOS Sierra, then you don�t actually need to use Xcode to pull logs from iOS devices! The Console app built into Sierra will let you select connected iOS devices and pull the logs directly. It also has some pretty slick filtering and searching capabilities, which is awesome if you�re doing a repro and want to dig into the logs before exporting them to a Windows machine to check out with another app like TextAnalysisTool.NET. This is a huge win for both us and our customers, as we all know how long it takes for Xcode to install; using the Console instead will lead to far quicker gathering of logs and resolution as the app is already built in to MacOS, no additional downloads needed! To export the logs from the Console, the best way is to clear any search queries you may have, then go to the Menu Bar and select Edit>Select All, then Edit>Copy. You can then paste them wherever you want (I like to use TextEdit).

When deploying an Intune App Protection policy to a user group for Microsoft Teams for iOS, the Intune App Protection dashboard does not report any users checked in for Microsoft Teams on iOS, and will always report 0. Other MAMWE iOS apps (Word, Excel, Outlook, etc.) work and report checked in users back to the Azure console properly.
rootCause: The Microsoft Teams app for iOS was created with an app id with lower-case characters (<Ios> instead of <IOS>). The app provider is case insensitive, but the background task which computes policy state is not.
solution: A fix to address the case sensitivity is slated for a future release in the July 2017 timeframe.

symptom: When attempting to enroll a device in Intune, the device hangs at Checking Compliance. When the hang occurs, the sync or compliance check may never complete. This is typically seen with iOS devices but Android devices can be affected as well.
rootCause: This can occur due to various conditions. See the steps below for information on how to begin troubleshooting the causes of this issue.
solution: 1. Have the customer cancel any enrollment in progress, then terminate the Company Portal app and re-open it. Sometimes the compliance check will actually complete successfully but not show up in the UI until the app is exited and restarted. When the Company Portal app is restarted, if it shows that the compliance check has completed then the problem is resolved. If it still shows 'Checking Compliance', completely uninstall the Company Portal app. Once uninstalled, reboot the phone, reinstall the Company Portal app and attempt to enroll again. If enrollment is successful and the compliance check completes, the problem is resolved. Root cause can vary, however one example where this can happen is if the device management profile has been removed from the device or is corrupted.2. This can be caused by an expired or revoke APNS certificate. Note that it could be expired even though it shows valid in Viewpoint. Have the customer check their cert via the Apple Push Certificates Portal at https://identity.apple.com/pushcert/. Verify that the APNS certificate shows as active and has not passed its expiration date.3. Get the UPN of an affected user and determine whether any Device compliance policies are deployed to that user. If so, disable the compliance policy and test to see if the problem still occurs. If the problem no longer occurs then there is a conflict somewhere in the policy. In other words, the compliance policy is requiring some condition that the client can never fulfill. Example 1:The customer may have a compliance policy targeted to the affected users with the policy setting �Require mobile devices to have a managed email profile�, however an email profile hasn't been deployed or targeted to the affected users. This can cause the targeted users to hang during a compliance check. Example 2:The customer has a compliance policy targeted to the affected users that requires a minimum OS version, yet a version number is not configured. This can cause the targeted users to hang during a compliance check. If disabling the compliance policy fixes the problem, examine the properties of the compliance policy setting and work to identify which specific policy setting is causing the enrollment failure. Once identified, the policy can be edited or removed as appropriate for a long term solution.4. Get the UPN of an affected user and determine whether any Device configuration policies are deployed to that user. If so, disable the compliance policy and test to see if the problem still occurs. If the problem no longer occurs then there is a conflict somewhere in the policy. In other words, the configuration policy is requiring some condition that the client cannot meet, or possibly a configuration that is failing or taking a long time to complete. For example, the policy can have the setting Authentication method set to Certificate, yet for some reason it is taking a very long time to retrieve the necessary certificate. In this scenario, checking compliance may take 20 to 30 minutes or more and the customer may perceive that to be a hang even though if enough time is allowed, the compliance check will complete. This can also manifest itself as a problem where some users can enroll fine while users in other specific locations experience what they believe is a hang.5. Get the UPN of an affected user and determine whether any Conditional Access (CA) policies are targeted to that user. If there are, disable the CA policy and test to see if the problem still occurs. If the problem no longer occurs then there is a conflict somewhere in that policy. For example, there may be a CA policy configured for Exchange Online, however the targeted users are hosted on Exchange on-prem and there's a compliance policy for the targeted users email. This can cause those users to hang during a compliance check. If disabling the conditional access policy fixes the problem, examine the properties of the conditional access policies as well as the compliance policies and work to identify which specific policy setting is causing the failure. Once identified, the policy can be edited or removed as appropriate for a long term solution.

symptom: OverviewAndroid Enterprise System Apps: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/apps/apps-ae-systemYou can enable a system app for Android Enterprise dedicated devices or fully managed devices.Managed Google Play web links: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/apps/apps-add-android-for-work#managed-google-play-web-linksManaged Google Play web links are installable and manageable just like other Android apps. When installed on a device, they will appear in the user's app list alongside the other apps they have installed. When tapped, they will launch in the device's browser.Managed Google Play LOB Apps: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/apps/apps-add-android-for-work#managed-google-play-private-lob-appsThis allows you to add LOB apps by submitting just the app APK and a title, directly within Intune. This method does not require you to have a Google developer account and does not require you to pay the fee to register with Google as a developer.Managed Google Play Store Apps: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/apps-add-android-for-workAll apps you install on Android Enterprise devices come from the Managed Google Play store. How you assign apps to Android Enterprise devices differs from how you assign them to standard Android Device Admin devices. You sign in to the store, browse for the apps you want, and approve them. The app then appears in the Licensed apps node of the Azure portal, and you can manage assignment of the app as you would any other app.Additionally, if you have created your own line-of-business (LOB) apps, you can assign them as follows:Sign up for a Google Developer account that lets you publish apps to a private area in the Google Play store.Synchronize the apps with Intune.To make it easier for you to configure and use Android Enterprise management, upon connecting to Google Play, Intune will automatically add four common Android Enterprise related apps to the Intune admin console. The four Android Enterprise apps are the following:Microsoft Intune - Used for Android Enterprise fully managed scenarios.Microsoft Authenticator - Helps you sign-in to your accounts if you use two-factor verification.Intune Company Portal - Used for App Protection Policies (APP) and Android Enterprise work profile scenarios.Managed Home Screen - Used for Android Enterprise dedicated/kiosk scenarios.Known IssuesManaged Google Play App audit events appear to be missinghttps://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-US/help/4506745Managed Google Play no longer supports the ability to purchase app licenseshttps://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-US/help/4499504Managed Play Apps not syncing to Intunehttps://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-US/help/4468728ConfigurationApproving the Company Portal app for Android Enterprise device managementhttps://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-US/help/4461454Configure Managed Google Play web linkshttps://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/help/4533320Enable Android system apps in Corporate-owned, fully managed user device enrollmenthttps://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-US/help/4515806How to allow private APK apps on Android Enterprisehttps://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/help/4533517Managed Google Play Update Behavior for Android Dedicated (COSU) Deviceshttps://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-US/help/4489449TroubleshootingScenario 1 Actual Issue Apps that are not deployed appear in the work profile for Android for Work devicesImpact to User Causing confusion because there are listed applications that are not being deployed Apps that are "system" apps can be enabled in the work profile by the device OEM at the time that the work profile is created. This is not controllable by the EMM Provider. Troubleshooting steps: Gather Company Portal Logs. Note which apps appear in the work profile unexpectedly. Unenroll the device from Intune, and then uninstall Company Portal. Install the "Test DPC" app from Play Store. (This is a Google app that allows creation of a work profile without an EMM). Follow the instructions in Test DPC to create a work profile on the device. Observe which apps appear in the work profile.If the same applications appear in the Test DPC App, the apps that the customer is seeing are expected and by design per the respective OEM for that device. Scenario 2Actual Issue Newly approved apps are not populating in the Apps page Impact to User Prevents Admin from deploying newly approved apps to Android Enterprise Users Currently, the sync is manual only. Therefore, the "Sync Now" option and the newly approved apps should appear. If you click the "Sync Now" option, and this successfully updates the Last Sync time, but the newly approved apps still do not appear, consult with your Android for Work SME. Scenario 3Actual Issue Application is not deployed successfully to the device Impact to User Device user cannot use the required applications Troubleshooting stepsGet the Intune Account ID, User ID, and optional device ID.Run the following query in Kusto (KB3153254):Note If you know the device ID, use the ActivityId value instead of "Col2" for the query. Also, make sure that you specify valid Date and Time values.IntuneEvent | where EventUniqueName == "AfwUserInstallApplicationWarnException" or EventUniqueName == "AfwUserInstallApplicationErrorException" | where Col1 == "<accountID>" | where Col2 == "<userId>" // | where ActivityId == "<deviceId>" | where env_time > now(-8d) | project env_time, (Col3) To determine which apps the event is for, examine the query results from step 2 for an exception, locate the env_time value for the exceptions, and then use that value in the following query.let TimeStart = datetime(2017-05-19 15:52:55.340); let TimeEnd = datetime(2017-05-19 15:53:20.0485077); DeviceManagementProvider | where env_time > TimeStart and env_time < TimeEnd | where ActivityId == "<deviceID>" Locate "Issuing Install" in the query results from step 3. To do this, click anywhere in the results, press CTRL+F, and then type "Issuing Install" (including the double quotation marks). This locates the apps that did not deploy. Known exceptionsProblemThe following exception is logged:Microsoft.Management.Services.Common.AccessDeniedException: The service androidenterprise has thrown an exception: Google.GoogleApiException: Google.Apis.Requests.RequestError Can't grant an entitlement for the app because the app permissions haven't been accepted. [403] Errors [ Message[Can't grant an entitlement for the app because the app permissions haven't been accepted.] Location[ - ] Reason[appPermissionsNotAccepted] Domain[androidenterprise] ]

When DEP device attempts to setup and enroll, it takes the following communication path:1. Power on device2. Select Language3. Select Country4. Setup Wifi a. At this point, based on User Affinity settings, you will be guided in one of two directions: i. Non-User Affinity 1. The device continues to setup the device and pulls down the Management Profile. ii. User affinity 1. The device requires the user credentials (Intune/CM licensed user account) a. No bypass in place: i. The SSO requires user authentication to allow access through the firewall ii. Since the device is in the setup phase, the there is no way for the user to enter their SSO credentials. iii. The device is still in the setup phase, so it fails to install the DEP enrollment policy b. Bypass (Exception) group is used: i. A User Bypass group (Exception Group) will allow the user through, so there is no request for the user to be requested authenticate to SSO claim rule. This will allow the user to completely bypass the SSO. ii. Allows the user to enter their Intune credentials and continue with the enrollment process.

Customer has created a shared mailbox (configured as a user account) and targeted it with a conditional access policy within Intune. Customer tries to configure access to the mailbox on the device but the account is listed in Exchange as quarantined.
rootCause: By design - Intune can only push an email profile for the directly enrolled user. There is currently no support for "Share mailbox's" or any email profile that is not directly tied to the enrolled user. And only one email profile is supported.
solution: You can only have one mailbox managed through Intune on a device. The shared mailbox account does not show as enrolled into Intune when you check View Point. If the customer is targeting the user with a CA policy for Exchange the device will not be permitted to access the mailbox; and it will be listed as quarantined in Exchange.

symptom: Communication path between the Intune service and the Apple DEP service. For public content on this see https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/device-enrollment-program-enroll-ios#sync-managed-devices 1. Intune calls Apple to do a full sync to fetch all the devices for that tenant every 7 days. 2. Also we call Apple within every 48 hours on behalf of the customer to fetch delta /newly added devices for that tenant since the last sync (note this is delta sync only fetching new records not the whole list) 3. For the Delta sync case, Intune uses a cursor or a skip token which Apple provides to us which is a hex string that represents the starting position for a request. This is used for retrieving the list of devices that have been added or removed since a previous request. 4. We store and use this Cursor and update it every time after Delta Sync to resume from where we left of, every 7 days however we do a full sync and discard the cursor.

This article documents recent changes to the Mobility POD EMEA callback request process. This process is to be used when EMEA customers are only available to work during regular EMEA time zone business hours (M-F). Effective date: 07/06/2017 Scope: Mobility POD customers located in EMEA This process does not apply in the following situations: 1. When customers are in Japan, India or Korea.2. Critsits that need follow the sun, those must transfer across regions.2. Cases that require work during the weekend.

This article documents recent changes to the Mobility POD APAC callback request process. This process is to be used when APAC customers are only available to work during regular APAC time zone business hours (M-F, 4:00pm PST to 2:00am PST). Effective date: 07/06/2017 Scope: Mobility POD customers located in APAC This process does not apply in the following situations: 1. When customers are in Japan, India or Korea. 2. Critsits that need follow the sun, those must transfer across regions. 2. Cases that require work during the weekend.

When using the RemoteWipe CSP to send a DoWipe command to a Windows 10 computer, the Windows computer shows no sign of wiping the device and an Event ID 400 error is generated in the Microsoft-Windows-DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider/Admin event log with "The request is not supported": Log Name: Microsoft-Windows-DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider/Admin Source: DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider Event ID: 400 Computer: DESKTOP PC User: System Details: MDM ConfigurationManager: Command failure status. Configuration Source ID: (9B5DC01F-D64E-488A-BB24-F0E9DA4FBF47), Enrollment Name: (MDMFull), Provider Name: (RemoteWipe), Command Type: (CmdType_Execute), CSP URI: (./Vendor/MSFT/RemoteWipe/doWipe), Result: (The request is not supported.). XML: - <Event xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/win/2004/08/events/event"> - <System> <Provider Name="Microsoft-Windows-DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider" Guid="{3DA494E4-0FE2-415C-B895-FB5265C5C83B}" /> <EventID>400</EventID> <Version>0</Version> <Level>2</Level> <Task>0</Task> <Opcode>0</Opcode> <Keywords>0x8000000000000000</Keywords> <TimeCreated SystemTime="2017-06-29T13:18:40.644619600Z" /> <EventRecordID>17327</EventRecordID> <Correlation /> <Execution ProcessID="2560" ThreadID="8612" /> <Channel>Microsoft-Windows-DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider/Admin</Channel> <Computer>DESKTOP PC</Computer> <Security UserID="S-1-5-18" /> </System> - <EventData> <Data Name="Message1">9B5DC01F-D64E-488A-BB24-F0E9DA4FBF47</Data> <Data Name="Message2">MDMFull</Data> <Data Name="Message3">RemoteWipe</Data> <Data Name="Message4">CmdType_Execute</Data> <Data Name="Message5">./Vendor/MSFT/RemoteWipe/doWipe</Data> <Data Name="HexInt1">0x80070032</Data> </EventData> </Event>
rootCause: This can occur if the Windows Recovery Environment (Windows RE) is disabled on the Windows 10 client computer. The RemoteWipe CSP requires Windows RE in order to function.
solution: First check the status of Windows RE. You can do this by running a Command Prompt as an administrator, then executing the following command: Reagentc /info The command should show a Windows RE status of Enabled, like this:If the status is Disabled, enable it by running the following command:Reagentc /enableIf the remote wipe continues to fail after enabling WinRE, or if you are unable to enable WinRE, please contact Microsoft Support for assistance.

symptom: Outlook has recently updated their mobile app to use a new API for log collection called PowerLift.Intune Support and Intune CXP has been granted access to logs which can be found at the following site: http://aka.ms/powerlift. The instructions for how to get these logs are below.

Customers may ask why we state in https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune-classic/deploy-use/use-remote-lock-and-passcode-reset-in-microsoft-intune that:Android - Supported on versions earlier than Android 7.0. Creates a temporary passcode.You can tell the customer: This is due to a security decision made by Google to prevent Ransomware Attacks on Android 7.0 and later.

Microsoft/ Zimperium Support Collaboration and Escalation Process: In the event an issue within Microsoft�s Support organization requires the attention of Zimperium Support, the Microsoft Support Engineer shall execute the following: 1) Request your customer open a Zimperium Support Case reporting the same issue you�re working. The specific guidance on how to open case that can be supplied to Intune Customer is below. 2)Obtain the Case ID from the customer if possible. If for some reason this cannot be done, execute step 3 anyway. 3) Generate an email addressed to support@zimperium.com with the following template completed: Microsoft Case/SR number: <Microsoft case number> Subject: <Short description of MSFT customer issue requiring Zimperium support Detailed description of the issue: <what is needed from Zimperium support?> Date case opened with MicrosoftCustomer Name Role: zConsole Administrator, End USerzConsole URL/Tenant Identification: Zimperium Console URL, tenant domain name where applicable (i.e., xxxxxxxxxx.onmicrosoft.com and or AAD Tenant GUIDzIPS Version Date Issue StartedDevice Details (where applicable): User ID, Device ID, OS, OS VersionExpected Behavior: <What does Customer expected to occur>Observed Behavior: <What is your assessment of the behavior> Error Message: <Any Error Message is the Customer Seeing> Reproduction Available? (Y/N): <is this an in-house repro or customer only> What are the repro steps? <list out exact steps to reproduce behavior if possible> This is what to expect from Zimperium Support: In the event an issue within the Zimperium Support organization requires the attention of Microsoft Support, the Zimperium Support Engineer shall execute the following: 1) Request your customer open a Microsoft Service Request reporting the same issue you�re working. Work cannot reasonably continue, and the situation requires immediate attention. Microsoft actions: Microsoft resources are required at the customer's site as soon as possible (except for Essential Support Standard). The issue is escalated to the product teams. Senior executives at Microsoft are notified, and the first call response is within one hour or less. Customer actions: Notification of the customer's senior executives is required. The customer must allocate appropriate resources to sustain continuous effort 24 hours a day, every day, until the issue is resolved. Only Technical Account Managers, Technical Account Specialists, and Critical Situation Managers can code cases as Severity 1. Agents code as Severity A. Time to Respond: 1 hour Premier contract only; 2 hours Broad Commercial Severity A � Critical Definition: Situations that involve a system, network, server, or critical program down situation that severely affects customer production or profitability. High-impact issues where production, operations, or development are proceeding but could be severely affected within several days. Microsoft actions: First call response is in one hour or less for Premier and Essential Support Standard SRs and one hour for Professional SRs. Microsoft will provide continuous effort to resolve the problem 24 hours a day. Senior managers at Microsoft are notified. Customer actions: The customer must allocate appropriate resources to sustain a continuous effort 24 hours a day. The customer's management must be notified. Time to Respond: 1 hour Premier contract only; 2 hours Broad Commercial Severity B � Urgent Definition: Signifies a moderate business impact. The customer's business has a moderate loss or degradation of services, but work can reasonably continue. The customer is not experiencing an immediate work stoppage. Microsoft actions: First call response will be two hours (business hours or actual hours, customer's choice) or less for Premier and Essential Support Standard. For Professional SRs, the callback time is four business hours. Customer actions: The customer must allocate appropriate resources during business hours (or 24x7 for Premier customers only if the customer opts for 24x7 troubleshooting) to sustain a continuous effort to resolve the issue. Time to Respond: 2 Hours Premier contracts only; 4 hours Broad Commercial Severity C � Important Definition: Indicates an issue with minimum business impact. The customer's business is substantially functioning and has minor or no impediment of services. Microsoft actions: First call response will be four business hours or less for Premier and Essential Support Standard. For Professional and Personal SRs, the callback response time is 24 business hours. Customer actions: The customer must provide accurate information to their agents working on the issue with Microsoft and must respond within 24 hours. Time to Respond: 4 Hours Premier contract only; 8 hours Broad Commercial ZIMPERIUM SUPPORT LEVEL SEVERITY DEFINITIONS Response and Resolution Times: Zimperium will maintain the following service levels with respect to providing support for the Subscription Software in Company�s live, production environment. These service levels apply only to use of the Subscription Software at the corporate level and not to Zimperium�s response time in evaluating individual incidents at the end user level: Problems Will be managed to resolution based upon the following prioritization Priority Zimperium Initial Response Time and Performance Criteria Update Response Times and Resolution Goal S1 - Critical Respond within 2 business hours of initial reported incident. Update every 4 business hours; target time to resolve issue: 1 business day Description Problems that arise when hardware, software, or network causes a complete disruption to Company�s normal business activity. An error that renders the Purchased Services inoperative. If the production system is down or does not function at all, and there is no workaround for the problem. When a reasonable workaround is the resolution, the problem will be reclassified to an S4-Low for software resolution. Priority Initial Response Time and Performance Criteria Update Response Times and Resolution Goal S2 - High Respond within 4 business hours of initial report incident Update every 8 business hours; target time to resolve issue: 3 to 4 business days Description Affects critical data or functionality with difficult work around. Major impact to productivity and efficiency. Also includes when a material component of the Purchased Services is not performing, creating a significant operational impact. When a reasonable workaround is the resolution, the problem will be reclassified to an S4-Low for software resolution. Priority Initial Response Time and Performance Criteria Update Response Times and Resolution Goal S3 � Medium Respond within 8 business hours of initial report incident Update every 48 business hours; target time to resolve issue: 5-10 business days Description Production is impeded in one or more non-core areas of the Purchased Services or minor issues with core areas where there is no reasonable work around. Includes when a component of the Purchased Services is not performing as documented; there are unexpected results; problems are avoidable; there is moderate or minor operational impact. When a reasonable workaround is the resolution, the problem will be reclassified to an S4-Low for software resolution. Workaround exists. These include minor problems that are not significant to operation (also suggestions, questions, or requests for new product features and enhancements). It is important that everyone is clear on when it is appropriate to close a case as a duplicate. As a general practice, the number of cases that are closed as duplicate should be a very low number. Please remember that closing cases as duplicate can be perceived as avoiding surveys as well as impacting our ability to effectively forecast for future volume. Below are the expectations on when to close cases as duplicate as well as some of the scenarios and how each should be handled. DUPLICATE CLOSURE SCENARIOS Cases should only be closed as duplicates if they meet the following criteria in these two scenarios. Scenario 1 (In this scenario, you must meet BOTH criteria in order to close the case as a duplicate) Less than 20 minutes of labor has been spent identifying that the issue is NOT within Intune�s scope of support. The issue requires as case be opened in a CRM other than MS Solve (i.e. CAP, RAVE, etc.) Scenario 2 (In this scenario, it can meet either or both criteria) A Premier customer has mistakenly opened a BC case and wants to work within their Premier contract. A TAM has informed you that the BC case for a Premier customer needs to transferred to a Premier case. Scenario 3 The customer has indicated that they opened more than one case for the exact same issue and has indicated that the other issues can be closed without handling. CASE HANDLING SCENARIOS For further clarification, below are some of the scenarios we discussed and how these should be handled. Scenario 1 (In this scenario, you must meet BOTH criteria in order to close the case as a duplicate) Less than 20 minutes have been spent identifying that the issue is NOT within Intune�s scope of support. The issue requires as case be opened in a CRM other than MS Solve (i.e. CAP, RAVE, etc.) Actions Document the steps that were taken to confirm that this is outside of Intune�s scope of support. Document the NEW SR that was created in the other CRM. In the event, the customer doesn�t want to open a new case in the other CRM, document their refusal. Update the support topic to reflect the correct issue. Close the case as a duplicate. Scenario 2 (In this scenario, it can be either or both criteria) A Premier customer has mistakenly opened a BC case and wants to work within their Premier contract. A TAM has informed you that the BC case for a Premier customer needs to transferred to a Premier case. Actions Document that the customer has mistakenly open the case as a BC case and/or the TAM is requesting a Premier case. Document the NEW SR that was created and copy over all previously documented steps as well as labor to the Premier case. Reference the old SR in the NEW SR as well. Close the BC case as a duplicate. Scenario 3 The customer has indicated that they opened more than one case for the exact same issue and has indicated that the other issues can be closed without handling. Actions Collect the SR numbers of the duplicate cases and take ownership. Document in each duplicate SR, the SR number that will remain own. Close only the duplicate cases as a duplicate. Work the remaining SR as normal. Scenario 4 The customer has multiple issues, one related to Intune and the other is NOT within Intune�s scope of support. The other issue requires as case be opened in a CRM other than MS Solve (i.e. CAP, RAVE, etc.) Actions Document and resolve the Intune related issue. Close the SR per normal resolution standards, NOT as duplicate. Explain to the customer how to create a case for the other issue. Scenario 5 The issue has been identified that is NOT within Intune�s scope of support. The issue needs to be transferred to another support team within MS Solve. Actions Document the steps that were taken to confirm that this is outside of Intune�s scope of support. Update the support topic to reflect the correct issue. Using MS Solve, auto route the case after changing the support topic. Follow up with the other support team to ensure they take ownership of the case. Scenario 6 The customer has multiple issues, one is related to Intune and the other is NOT within Intune�s scope of support The issue needs to be transferred to another support team in MS Solve. Actions Document and resolve the Intune related issue. Close the SR per normal resolution standards, NOT as duplicate. Explain to the customer that they will need to create a new case for the other issue and assist them as needed.
When attempting to enroll an iOS device in Microsoft Intune, the enrollment process fails with the following error: Profile Installation Failed A connection to the server could not be established
rootCause: This can occur if the device had been previously enrolled by another user and that other user�s account was disabled or removed without first retiring or un-enrolling the device. The problem occurs because the device is still in the Intune database.
solution: To resolve this problem, you first need to find the previous device ID assigned to the device and remove it from Intune: Search Kusto on server IOSEnrollmentService and userId per SDK 3153254 If you see an error "Attempting to re-enroll device {xxxx-xxxx-xxxx} previously enrolled with user {xxx-xxxxx-xxxx} in account {xxx-xxxx-xxxxx} with a different user" then the device is still present in the Admin Console To remove the device you need to find the Serial # (On device navigate to Settings>General>About Then search for the device in the Admin Console and remove it Login to https://devicemanagement.microsoft.com Click on Devices > All Devices In the Search box, search for the Serial # found in step 9ai Select device and Click Retire/Wipe > Selective Wipe Select device and Click Delete

symptom: In an Intune/Configuration Manager hybrid environment, deploying an Android for Work (AfW) app fails with a status of Host Platform Not Applicable.
rootCause: This can occur if the app is deployed as Available. This is by design.
solution: To resolve this problem, remove the existing deployment and re-deploy the app as Required.

Delve is not in the list of apps that support MAM policies yet users can still log into Delve and copy and paste data out of SharePoint.
rootCause: By design
solution: The following ICM bug has been filed: https://icm.ad.msft.net/imp/v3/incidents/details/41783456/home

Customer configures Azure AD Application Proxy to protect NDES when deploying SCEP certificates to iOS and Android devices. Customer installs the app proxy, and publishes an application with the NDES server name that will be accessible from outside of their network. Customer tries to test the external URL by adding "certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll" to the path of the existing URL but receives the following error: "Page cannot be displayed"
rootCause: Customer had this format listed as the external URL: https://FQDN.com/certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll
solution: Delete the existing application from Azure and create a new app with the correct FQDN name in the internal URL

symptom: An iOS device fails to enroll in Intune and generates the following error message: "Profile Installation Failed. A Network Error Has Occurred"
rootCause: This can occur if there is an unspecified issue with iOS on the device.
solution: To resolve the issue, put the devices into DFU mode (Device Firmware Update mode) and restore iOS. Once this is done, the device should be able to enroll successfully.
