Customer after tenant migration can no longer install Microsoft Store based Apps from the Company Portal to iOS and Android devices. When attempting to install there is no error and the App just stays in "Pending Sync" state and never completes.
rootCause: As the applications were present in the Intune Classic (Silverlight) portal, prior or during the migration of the tenant to the Azure Graph API portal, there is a cross over of process. The Graph API side of the tenant cannot process the Microsoft App classic MAM policy.
solution:
The resolution to this issue, is to remove\clean up the Microsoft Apps from the Intune Classic (Silverlight) portal and then either use the default versions of the Microsoft Apps that come with the Azure Graph API Intune Portal or re-create the Microsoft Apps in the Azure Graph API Intune Portal from scratch. The MAM policy is replaced with the use of Application Protection (MAM-WE) policies as well and can be deployed (assignments) separate of the Microsoft Apps.(Example steps for a customer's action plan)

[Clean up the Microsoft Apps from the Intune Classic (Silverlight) Portal]

1. Open the Intune
Classic Portal

2. Navigate to
Apps->Apps

3. Select a Microsoft App, document the security group deployed to and MAM Policy settings

4. Click Delete5. Repeat for each Microsoft App until all are cleaned up



[Create the Microsoft Apps in the Azure Graph API Intune
Portal]

1. Open the Azure
Intune Portal

2. Navigate to
Intune->Mobile Apps->Apps

3. Click Add

4. Select from the drop down to set the App
Type, example: iOS Store App

5. Click "Select App"
and enter in the "Search" "Microsoft"

6. Select the Microsoft App you wish to add and click OK

7. Click App
Information

8. Configure any customizations you may wish to make and click OK

9. Click Add

 

Note: The app will
now be in the list, you will need to assign it

 

10. Select the
Microsoft App you just added

11. Click
"Assignments" and then "Select groups"

12. Select the
groups and click Select

13. Set your "Type"
and set the "VPN" setting, then click Save



Note: Your app is
now corrected and you will be able to install again from the Company Portal,
test this with a device.

Note: If you want to
re-assign a MAM policy for this app, you will need to create an Application Protection Policy (MAM-WE) and can reference the article: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/app-protection-policy

symptom: When iOS Device is unassigned from DEP Portal but after DEP Sync, Delete action for device is greyed out in the Pre-Declared list and cannot be removed, event when showing "Not contacted" or "Unenrolled" as if the device is still enrolled.
rootCause: This is a code fault where if the table "MDMCorpOwnedDevices" has been updated with a contact time, the device cannot be deleted as when the device is unassigned and the DEP Sync occurs the trigger does not perform to clean up the table.BUG: SMS494132 has been opened and this is being worked on.
solution:
There are two options for a work around on this problem for the customer while they wait for the Code Fault to be corrected, they are listed below.(Option 1 Workaround - created by Senthilkumar Pandurangan)1. Un-enroll the iPad with the offending behavior (Delete Greyed Out) by performing a retire\wipe command 2. Set to Unassigned in the DEP Portal and allow a Sync to complete (this is where the bug occurs FYI in a table) 3. Re-enroll the same device as a BYOD device using Company Portal (this causes the origin to be updated from DEP Sync to NULL) 4. Delete the Pre-declared entry

(Option 2
Workaround - created by Charles Allen)

This option is SQL
Based and should be used as a last resort
because it is not idea or supported to modify the ConfigMgr DB directly.1. Open the SQL Management Studio with an account that has CM DB rights (Full rights)2. Select the CM DB3. Click "New Query"4. Enter the query either of the queries below, after updating the value required:Update dbo.MDMCorpOwnedDevicesSet EnrollmentStatus = NULL, DiscoverySources = 1Where DeviceName in ('IMEINumberHere')Note: Can also use the serial number as well instead of the IMEI number Update dbo.MDMCorpOwnedDevicesSet EnrollmentStatus = NULL, DiscoverySources = 1Where UniqueId in ('SerialNumberHere') (Options for the queries)DiscoverySources values:1 = Empty2-3 = Imported4 = Dep SyncEnrollmentStatus values:2 = EnrolledNULL = Not Contacted5. Execute the query6. Refresh the Pre-Declared list, select device, right click and select "Delete"(Examples of Option 2)Before SQL Query:After SQL Query:

symptom: When attempting to enroll a Windows 10 Device (1703) via MDM. The following error Message is shown:Error 810800a: This Device is Already EnrolledWhen going to All Settings -> Accounts -> Access Work or school, the device does show as being MDM enrolled
rootCause: The device was previously enrolled with an Azure AD account that has been disabled or deleted
solution:
1) On the impacted device open regedit.exe and
navigate to: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments
2) Search for the "manage.microsoft.com" and identify the KEY that contains the UPN that the device was MDM enrolled with:  3) Perform a backup of the registry Key and remove it4) Re-enroll the Windows 10 device

The Intune PC client
shows as installing in the system tray for an extended amount of time and never appears to complete the installation.Also, when looking at the Windows machine's properties in the Intune classic portal, there is no last
update information or any information listed under the hardware tab. The policy tab may also be blank. Additionally, no errors are displayed in the Intune classic console for the machine(s).
rootCause:
A cause of the reported behavior can be that the Windows machine(s) is configured to connect to an on-premises Windows Server Update Services (WSUS) server. This configuration can prevent some of the Intune agents from retrieving updates. 
The on-premises WSUS servers will not provide updates for Intune client agents.
solution:
Avoid forcing (commonly done via a Group Policy Objects) the Windows machine(s) to use an on-premises Windows Server Update Services (WSUS) server.https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune-classic/deploy-use/resolve-gpo-and-microsoft-intune-policy-conflicts

 

https://social.technet.microsoft.com/Forums/en-US/9c744561-a05a-4678-8094-1fe1816ca000/how-do-you-make-windows-intune-and-wsus-work-together?forum=microsoftintuneprod

symptom: WIP is not applying when updating from Office Pro Plus version 1706 or 1707 to version 1708.
rootCause:
Office executables
code signed by the wrong CA
solution: Office suite: Non-security updates must be performed for version 1707 and it fixes 1706 issues.This update fixes an issue with how some program files are signed, causing anti-virus programs to flag those files as well as problems protecting or accessing data under Windows Information Protection (WIP).

symptom: You are unable to enroll DEP devices when prompting for User Affinity and Conditional Access is not required for enrollment. When this opccurs, the user enters their Username and Password, then the spinner at the top will spin for a short time then stop, returning to the Username and Password prompt.
rootCause: We have found that, on some devices that arrive from Apple/DEP vendors, they simply do not work with User Affinity when logging in.
solution: To resolve this problem, check this VKB first: https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4014915/UnabletoEnrollDEPDevicesWhenPromptingforUserAffinity. This article is often applicable and should be verified before performing the next steps. If the resolution in the article does not work, please try the steps below. =================== A common fix to this is often coined as a "DFU Restore" - this stands for a Device Firmware Update.  This process completely wipes iOS off of the device, downloads a new copy of iOS, and installs it onto the device. This process removes the DEP profile from the device, and is like setting up a brand new device with a new OS installation. The steps to perform this can be found here: https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT201263.  NOTE: Apple does not call this process a 'DFU Restore' any longer, but the article is still applicable. It is also important to note - when the iPad is being setup, in the Out of Box Experience, they will be prompted to "Set up as a new iPad" (or iPhone, etc) or "Restore from backup". They need to select "Set up as a new iPad" for this process, in order to ensure everything is from scratch.

symptom: Q:Can you push a custom wallpaper via an Intune device configuration policy to an iOS or Android device?A:Currently, Intune does not support custom wallpapers pushed to iOS and Android devices via a device configuration policy. A device configuration policy can be used to push a desktop background picture URL to Windows 10 devices.
Unable to install apps from Public or Private Store - Getting error stating"Error code 0x8024500C can't install or update apps from store Windows 10""Something unexpected happened (0x80248014).
rootCause: GPO Policy Setting that needed to be changed... "Disable all apps from Windows Store", needed to be set to "Not Configured" or "Allowed."
solution: This setting can be accessed by following the steps below: 1) click START, then type GPEDIT.MSC and press the Enter key2) Expand USER CONFIGURATION> ADMINISTRATIVE TEMPLATES> WINDOWS COMPONENTS> STORE3) Locate the "Disable all apps from Windows Store" Policy Setting and ensure that it is set to "Not Configured"Once that GPO Setting has been verified, the customer can refer to the following article to manager the Store for Businesshttps://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-store/windows-store-for-business-overview#set-up

symptom:
Customer is using a PFX certificate to deploy a
WiFi policy to an Android device. The WiFi policy however is not being applied to
the devices; the following error is seen:  "0x87D1FDE8 Remediation failed"
rootCause:
Misconfigured certificate template (template created using
wrong Windows version)
solution:
Created a new
     certificate template for Android devices
 In the CA went to the Certificate Templates node, click
     Action-> New > Certificate Template to Issue
 Enrolled an Android
     device into Intune, waited for about 30 minutes and the profile was
     deployed to the device and the settings were visible

symptom: Questions from a customer and the answers provided to these questions by a PM on the product team.  Customer facing doc: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/client-management/mdm/bulk-enrollment-using-windows-provisioning-toolFAQ: When       doing the Bulk Enrollment Process which user account does the device get       registered to within Azure Active Directory?If you are using Provisioning Package (PP) to do  bulk enroll, then each PP will have a corresponding �surrogate user� with a  name like �package-GUID�.  The device will get registered with this  surrogate user account in AADObviously,        a local account with admin rights would need to be used to install/apply       the provisioning package that performs the join/enrollment.         After this process is complete can that local admin account then be       deleted after the provisioning package is installed?YesCan       using the same provisioning package, can we revoke the admin rights from       the current signed in user (for example, the user who installs the provisioning       package, or perhaps this is the last thing in the provisioning sequence?I do not believe so.  You will probably have to  write your own script to do thatIn       the document it states: �Azure AD users are standard users on these       devices and receive assigned Intune policies and required apps.       Self-service and Company Portal scenarios are not supported at this       time.�  Is there some guidance on how this is different from if a       customer, where they are revoking the admin rights from a user who       performs the AAD join and enrollment using a script pushed via Intune as       a required installation?  Because in the past we have seen that       self-service and the company portal doesn�t work.  So how is this       process different?The only difference is that in the past if they did  not use PP, then they have to do the AADJ and Intune enroll using a �REAL�   user, which has a limit (15 if normal user, 1,000 if DEM user).  With PP  you can set it to unlimited.I       have a customer who in their last and current POC setup, they have       successfully managed to perform AD Join + Enrolment, push required       installs and finally revoke admin rights from the user along with a       forced restart (this was fixed with 1704 deployment), Conditional Access       works but they are stating they are not able to use self-service or       company portal. The company portal app shows device as compliant and has       been so for last 2 months. However, they�re not able to push more apps       as required installs AFTER revoking admin rights from the user who       joined, enrolled. Does the process of using the Bulk Enrollment solve       this scenario?  If you don�t know I will have the customer open a       new case for this scenario. Yes it should solve this scenarioWhen       admin rights are revoked from joining/enrolling user the device would go       out of compliance. Do you know WHEN or HOW LONG before a device could        go out of compliance. (NOTE: The customer has a device for more       than 2.5 months and it�s still not out of compliance and continues to       show as compliant both in company portal app and in Intune, which leaves       them in a conundrum because we�re not sure if we can rely on the info       being displayed)The  device is not expected to be marked as noncompliant if the admin rights are  revoked from the user. Compliance settings are about the device meeting  security requirements, and there is no compliance setting that requires the  current logged in user to be an admin user.
Unable to deliver SCEP certificates to some devices.Error: <insert error message and log file>
rootCause: Old SCEP urls were not deleted from Intune when they were deleted from SCCM, and thus were pushed to devices via the cert profile.  Since the urls were invalid, the SCEP certs could not be issued. We are still investigating root cause.
solution: Solution:Complete the following steps in the SCCM database to sync the Ndes URLs between SCCM & Intune: Insert Ndes URLs currently present in Intune (we need to insert and then remove these to sync properly): INSERT INTO [dbo].[MDMNdesList] VALUES ('6abc4dc5-109a-4668-a49e-967ba55783d2', 'https://scepmobile01.bosch.com/certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll') INSERT INTO [dbo].[MDMNdesList] VALUES ('2998907e-7a0d-42c0-a610-186466bd8fbb', 'https://fe0vm1664.de.bosch.com/certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll') INSERT INTO [dbo].[MDMNdesList] VALUES ('b1ab6296-cff3-4d91-a0dd-fb3284fc3bea', 'https://scepmobile.bosch.com/certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll') Verify that there are now 4 records:   SELECT URLID, NdesUrl FROM [dbo].[MDMNdesList] Wait for 1 hour (preferably confirm that at least one sync has been performed after executing the above SQL statements. Consult with the dmpuploader log.)  Execute background stored procedure to update MDMNdesList based on Ndes Cert info (this will delete the records we previously inserted, as they do not have a Ndes Cert associated): EXECUTE [dbo].[spUpdateNdesList] Verify that only the valid Ndes URL record exists: SELECT URLID, NdesUrl FROM [dbo].[MDMNdesList] Wait for 1 hour (preferably confirm that at least one sync has been performed after executing the above SQL statements. Consult with the dmpuploader log.) Alternately, we can patch the Account entity in Intune to match SCCM values:  URLID                                                                            NdesUrlED3D14E4-15EB-4145-921A-F6C62BCEFE9C           https://scepmobile01.bosch.com/certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll

symptom: When the Pioneer Trade Show app is deployed to iOS devices, the Share button is unavailable.
rootCause: This can occur if the policy Viewing corporate documents in unmanaged apps is set to Block. This policy setting can be found in profiles configured under Azure Intune portal -> Device configuration -> Profiles. Look for any profiles where Platform=iOS and Profile Type=Device Restrictions.
solution: To resolve this issue, configure the policy Viewing corporate documents in unmanaged apps to Not Configured. Once the policy updates on the iOS devices the Share button will now be available.

symptom: When attempting to configure a Mobile Threat Defense connector for Check Point SandBlast in Microsoft Intune, the connection status shows Not set up and all configuration options are grayed out.
rootCause: This can occur if the sync between Check Point and the Microsoft Intune service has not yet completed.
solution: For this to work, Check Point SandBlast must first be properly configured to use the Intune service. Once this is done, the sync from Check Point to Intune can take a couple hours. If the customer has recently configured Check Point for Intune in their portal, allow the service an hour or two to sync. Once the sync between Check Point and the Microsoft Intune service completes, the connection status will change to Connected and all configuration options will become available.

symptom: When you switch your MDM authority from Intune to SCCM, device categories that were created based on device group mapping in Intune are not carried over when the devices populate in Configuration Manager.
rootCause: This is by design. When you change your MDM authority from Intune to SCCM, the devices will populate in Configuration Manager the next time they check in (the next time they sync), however the device category property does not.
solution:
Below is a
workaround that can be used to recreate device group mapping categories in
Configuration Manager: Create a Device Collection
in Configuration Manager for each device category that currently exists in
Intune. To make it easy, you can make the name of each Device Collection match the
name of a device category in Intune.In the Intune portal,
export the members of each device group (category) to their own dedicated CSV
files.For each CSV, take the first
column and paste it into its own text file. This will give you a TXT file with
a list of all the group members (devices) in each category.Recreate Device Group
Mapping (Device Categories) in SCCM for each respective collection. This will
address enrollment going forward once the MDM authority switchover is made. See
https://www.petervanderwoude.nl/post/categorizing-devices/
for more details on how to do this.Disable Device Group
Mapping in the Intune Portal.Once ready, perform the
MDM authority switch.As devices begin to appear
in Configuration Manager, run the following PowerShell script for each txt file you
created in step 3 above. Substitute groupmembers.txt
for the name of your txt file and SCCM Device Collection 1 for each Device Collection you created in SCCM. Run
the script for each txt file/collection. The script reads each txt file and places
the respective devices into the new collection based on the name of the device.$groupMembers = get-content
"groupmembers.txt"foreach ($groupMember in $groupMembers){    $collectionname
= "SCCM
Device Collection 1"    $collectionID
= get-cmdevicecollection
-name $collectionname    Add-CMDeviceCollectionDirectMembershipRule
-CollectionID $collectionID
-ResourceId $(Get-CMDevice
-Name $groupMember).ResourceID

}

symptom: One of the top requests from Intune administrators is for richer tools to manage licenses and control service enrollment. This is now available via a public preview of Azure AD group-based license management and details can be found below.

When scanning for updates with the Intune Agent, you receive error 0x8024001e, and you no longer are receiving any new policies, updates, or app deployments deployed to the Intune Agent.Additionally, the WindowsUpdate registry key may look similar to this:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\windows\WindowsUpdate AcceptTrustedPublisherCerts REG_DWORD 0x1 WUServer REG_SZ http://localhost:1550 WUStatusServer REG_SZ http://localhost:1550 TargetGroup REG_SZ Automatic Windows Update Policy TargetGroupEnabled REG_DWORD 0x1
rootCause: To verify the Kaspersky agent is the cause of the issue, attempt to delete the WindowsUpdate registry key (make sure to make appropriate registry backups before making any modifications).After a deletion attempt, you will likely see the WindowsUpdate key being automatically recreated with the incorrect values, almost immediately.To confirm that Kaspersky is the culprit in this scenario, you can run a Process Monitor on the machine during the Registry deletion and recreation. You will then be able to see the klnagent.exe process setting the registry key values to the incorrect values:
solution: Customer should contact Kaspersky for guidance on adding the Intune Agent/Windows Update registry key to an exclusion for their AV service.

symptom: When downloading OneNote for iOS deep link app deployed "As Available" it gets stuck in "Pending sync" then it fails to install.
rootCause: URL on app was set to Canada - https://itunes.apple.com/ca/app/microsoft-onenote/id410395246?mt=8 The URL must be set to US

symptom: When adding the 26th Device Enrollment Manager (DEM) account, an error "An error occurred promoting user to DEM a user. Request ID: xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
rootCause: There is a limit of 25 Device Enrollment Manager (DEM) accounts in Microsoft Intune.

symptom: When adding the 26th Device Enrollment Manager (DEM) account, an error "An error occurred promoting user to DEM a user. Request ID: xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
rootCause: This can occur if the account you're trying to add does not have a valid Intune license. Another cause is you added more than 25 Device Enrollment Manager (DEM) accounts in Microsoft Intune. There is a limit of 25 Device Enrollment Manager (DEM) accounts in Microsoft Intune. See https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4039843/an-error-occurred-promoting-user-to-dem-a-user-when-adding-more-than-2
solution: To resolve this error, first try adding an Intune license to a user, then remove an unneeded DEM accounts before adding another.

symptom: Customer has AirWatch as his MDM and is using MAM w/o enrollment (with the IntuneMAMUPN setting deployed in an AirWatch app config policy). Customer would like to be able to copy/paste from those managed apps into the native iOS email client; but some users are unable to copy data from Word, Excel, etc, to the native email client. They receive the following error: "Your organization's data cannot be pasted here."
rootCause: By Design
solution: Only files can be shared on a 3rd Party MDM vendor when the EMM UPN[IntuneMAMUPN] is deployed. Copy/Cut/Paste only works in MAM managed apps.

symptom: Title: Internal Process for Mindtree Technical Support for Consults/Escalations Purpose: The purpose of this process is to improve workflow between teams and the quality of escalations reaching the Intune Escalation Team. Process for Consultation: Frontline Engineers Frontline Engineers may utilize the Intune MT Tech Leads Queue for consultations for cases they own during any time of the case if they feel they have exhausted all channels for troubleshooting or feel that they have no path moving forward for case progression. Some examples of channels for troubleshooting/collaboration: � Asking questions in the INTUNE Case Discussion Microsoft Teams Channel � Checked all documentation VKB/TechNet � Consulted with their local peers Once it is determined that a consultation is needed the Frontline Team will create Tech Review Task in their case. The Tech Review Task needs to contain: � The Escalation Template Filled Out with a clear ask on what is needed during consultation. � The Task Title formatted to: <TL Alias> | Consult | <Brief Description of the Issue> Example: franknov | Consult | Microsoft Intune Full Client Failing to Install with Error 0x80103301 Note The Technical Lead added to the Task Title is the Technical Lead to whom the Frontline Support Engineer currently works with/assigned to. Once the Escalation Template is filled out and the Task Title is updated, the Frontline Team will transfer the Consultation Task to the Intune MT Tech Leads Queue within MSSolve. Technical Leads The Technical Lead listed in the Title of the Task is the primary reviewer of the task and will consult with the Frontline Support Engineer who is requesting assistance and will perform the following actions: � Take ownership of Consultation Task � Review Escalation Template, Case Notes, Communications, etc. � Provide Action Plan to Case Owner with next steps � Close Task once Action Plan is Delivered. Process for Escalation: Frontline team fills out the Escalation Template as documented as a new Tech Review Task Update the Task Title using the following format: <TL Alias> | Escalation | <Brief Description of the Issue> and transfers the task to the Intune MT Tech Leads Queue within MSSolve. Example: franknov | Escalation | Windows 10 PC�s running 1607 fail to enroll with Intune MDM The Technical Lead listed in the Title of the Task is the primary reviewer of the task and will perform the following functions: � Take Ownership of the Escalation Task � Review Escalation Template, Case Notes, Communications, etc. � If information for approved escalation is not contained in the Escalation Template, Update Task with guidance on what information is missing. Reject Case (transfer back to case owner) to gather information and once information is contained Case Owner can re-escalate task. � If information for approved escalation is contained in the Escalation Template the Technical Lead will: - Update the Task with Note that states: Template Reviewed and Signed Off for Escalation as the last entry in the task. - Transfer the task to the Microsoft Intune Escalation Queue within MSSolve. Note The Technical Lead added to the Task Title is the Technical Lead to whom the Frontline Support Engineer currently works with/assigned to. Requests for updates/changes should be directed to the Intune Technical Advisors DL - IntuneTA@Microsoft.com for review.

The Basics For policy failures the first step should be to check if the device is enrolled. This can be checked in the start menu>settings>accounts>Work Access Here we can see the user duser1 is enrolled to the tenant �Enterprise Mobility�. If this is does not have an enrollment and just say �Connect� then the device is not MDM enrolled and will not receive MDM policy. The health of the enrollment can be checked by manually syncing with the MDM server and checking info. Manual sync will force the client to communicate to the MDM server and deploy any policy the server dictates. Under Info we can see if the device has been able to successfully sync with the MDM server, if the device has never synced or the last sync is from a long time ago this points to a networking issue. If the device is correctly enrolled the event logs can be used if the device is updated to Threshold2. To view the Desktop MDM Admin event logs: Open eventvwr.msc Enable Debug logs: Navigate to: Applications and Services Logs->Microsoft->Windows->DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider->AdminIf exporting for remote analysis: Right click->Save Log As...->Enter name (Enter a name)->OK->Select "Display information for these languages:"->OkExport the �Debug� logs as well If a device is not receiving MDM policy, or receiving it but not behaving as expected, check the settings in the registry. There are 2 important locations, Current and Default. Current stores the current settings, default stores the default settings. By comparing the two we can understand if policies are being configured on the device. For example: Current: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\current Default: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\default Here we can see the value currently set for AllowActionCenterNotifications is equal to the default value of 1. Policies from Microsoft Intune If a device is enrolled to Intune but not receiving policy, we can view additional information coming from Intune cached at this location. HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Provisioning\NodeCache\CSP\MS DM Server\Nodes\ Under this path lies a list of settings pushed down from the MDM server, in the example below we can see an AppLocker policy. We see the NodeUri the policy was applied to, this corresponds to the OMA URI from the Intune console and the ExpectedValue, which corresponds to the Data field in the Intune console. Checking the Enrollment If the correct policies are not present or not up to date the enrollment status should be checked. This can be seen at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Provisioning\OMADM\Accounts\<GUID associated with MDM enrollment>\Protected\ConnInfo\ Here we can see that the server was able to successfully connect to the MDM server at 2015 08 31 11:14:22 AM. In other words, August 31st, 2015.

symptom:
When attempting to enroll an iOS device, the
enrollment process fails with the following error:



Device Cap Reached
rootCause:
This can occur if the user has attempted to enroll more devices
then the configured maximum allowed.
solution:
To resolve this problem, first verify how many devices the user has enrolled, then check the device
enrollment limit configured in Intune and make sure that the enrollment limit
has not been reached.The number of devices the user has already
enrolled can be determined in the Intune admin portal under Devices -> All
Devices.Also have the affected user login to the Intune user portal and check for any old or unused devices there. It is possible for old devices to appear in the Intune user portal and not show up in the Intune admin portal. Devices here will also count towards the users enrollment limit even if they do not appear in the Intune admin portal.To check the device enrollment limit, navigate
to the Intune admin portal and go to Admin -> Mobile
Device Management -> Enrollment Rules. By default, the
device enrollment limit is set to 15.If you experience this problem, remove any unneeded
devices or increase the device enrollment limit.

symptom: When using the RemoteWipe CSP to send a DoWipe command to a Windows 10 computer, the Windows computer shows no sign of wiping the device and an Event ID 400 error is generated in the Microsoft-Windows-DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider/Admin event log with the following error:Log Name: Microsoft-Windows-DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider/AdminSource: DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-ProviderEvent ID: 400Computer: DESKTOP PCUser: SystemDetails: MDM ConfigurationManager: Command failure status. Configuration Source ID: (9B5DC01F-D64E-488A-BB24-F0E9DA4FBF47), Enrollment Name: (MDMFull), Provider Name: (RemoteWipe), Command Type: (CmdType_Execute), CSP URI: (./Vendor/MSFT/RemoteWipe/doWipe), Result: (The request is not supported.).XML:- <Event xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/win/2004/08/events/event">- <System>  <Provider Name="Microsoft-Windows-DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider" Guid="{3DA494E4-0FE2-415C-B895-FB5265C5C83B}" />   <EventID>400</EventID>   <Version>0</Version>   <Level>2</Level>   <Task>0</Task>   <Opcode>0</Opcode>   <Keywords>0x8000000000000000</Keywords>   <TimeCreated SystemTime="2017-06-29T13:18:40.644619600Z" />   <EventRecordID>17327</EventRecordID>   <Correlation />   <Execution ProcessID="2560" ThreadID="8612" />   <Channel>Microsoft-Windows-DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider/Admin</Channel>   <Computer>DESKTOP PC</Computer>   <Security UserID="S-1-5-18" />   </System>- <EventData>  <Data Name="Message1">9B5DC01F-D64E-488A-BB24-F0E9DA4FBF47</Data>   <Data Name="Message2">MDMFull</Data>   <Data Name="Message3">RemoteWipe</Data>   <Data Name="Message4">CmdType_Execute</Data>   <Data Name="Message5">./Vendor/MSFT/RemoteWipe/doWipe</Data>   <Data Name="HexInt1">0x80070032</Data>   </EventData>  </Event>
rootCause: This can occur if AllowUserToResetPhone=0 is set per https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/client-management/mdm/policy-csp-system#system-allowusertoresetphone.
solution: To resolve this issue, configure AllowUserToResetPhone=1 or do not specify a value for AllowUserToResetPhone. The default value is 1 and will be used automatically unless you set AllowUserToResetPhone=0.Once this is done, the DoWipe command should complete successfully.

Microsoft Service
Level Severity Definitions: 

 

Severity
1 � Catastrophic or critical situation 

�       
Definition:
Situations that critically affect the customer's business, such as complete
loss of a core business process. Work cannot reasonably continue, and the
situation requires immediate attention. 

�       
Microsoft
actions: Microsoft resources are required at the customer's site as soon
as possible (except for Essential Support Standard). The issue is escalated to
the product teams. Senior executives at Microsoft are notified, and the first
call response is within one hour or less. 

�       
Customer
actions: Notification of the customer's senior executives is required.
The customer must allocate appropriate resources to sustain continuous effort
24 hours a day, every day, until the issue is resolved. 

Only Technical Account Managers, Technical Account Specialists,
and Critical Situation Managers can code cases as Severity 1.  Agents code
as Severity A.  Time to Respond: 1
hour Premier contract only; 2 hours Broad Commercial

Severity
A � Critical 

�       
Definition:
Situations that involve a system, network, server, or critical program down
situation that severely affects customer production or profitability.
High-impact issues where production, operations, or development are proceeding
but could be severely affected within several days.

Attempting to open an attached Word file
in the Outlook app on an Android device or an iOS device fails with the following error: This action is blocked by your
organization or We can't open the fileWe can't show the file because it's
corrupted. or Can't open file























Try saving the file on the device and
then opening it.
rootCause: This can occur if a device configuration policy for device encryption exists and Encrypt app data is also enabled in a MAM app protection policy for Outlook. Note that the Nine Work for Intune email client is also affected by this when attempting to open an attachment.
solution:
To resolve this issue, complete the following:

Open the Azure portal.Click More Services and search on Intune.Select Intune App Protection.Click App Policy then find the policy deployed
to the affected users.Open the policy and click Policy Settings.Find the setting Encrypt app data and set to No
or Use device settings.Save the
policy.Once
the policy is saved, do a sync (check compliance) on the mobile device having
the problem then try opening attachments again.

If
you still receive an error, it may be necessary to also configure the following
settings in the same policy:

Allow
app to transfer data to other apps = All appsAllow
app to receive data from other apps = All apps

symptom: When trying to install the Microsoft Intune Exchange Connector the following error is seen: The Microsoft Intune Exchange Connector experienced an error: The underlying connection was closed: An unexpected error occurred on a receive. Reviewing the connector logs: Error enrolling: System.Net.WebException: The underlying connection was closed - System.ComponentModel.Win32Exception: The client and server cannot communicate, because they do not possess a common algorithm
rootCause: The Exchange Connector was installed with only an older version of .NET 3.5 on the machine, which caused it to default to an unsupported protocol/cipher even after 4.5.2 was installed, causing the "do not possess a common algorithm" error when the Exchange Connector tries to reach Intune service.
solution: On the Exchange Connector machine: 1) Install .NET 4.5.2. 2) Add DWORD SchUseStrongCrypto under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework4.0.30319 and HKLM\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Microsoft\.NETFramework4.0.30319 to ensure TLSv1.2. 3) Install Windows security patch 2960358 to disable unsafe RC4 cipher in .NET TLS.

symptom: Intune users targeted by Exchange Online MAMCA with the requirement to only allow apps that support Intune app policies are allowed to access email via the native mail app/EAS on their devices, and are not being forced to use the Outlook app. These users are also targeted by Exchange Online Conditional Access in the Intune classic portal.
rootCause: You can combine device-based and app-based conditional policies (logical AND), or provide either option (logical OR). If conditional access policy requirements are to require a compliant device AND use the approved app, you should set your conditional access policy using the Azure Active Directory conditional access blade and the Intune App Protection blade.
solution: Intune admins should migrate their Intune Exchange Online Conditional Access policy to Azure Active Directory Conditional Access, and turn off the policy in the Intune classic portal. If the Intune classic policy is turned off, the access criteria will be adjusted to require a compliant device AND use the approved app. Users should now be prompted to use Outlook, and EAS access should be blocked.

symptom: Customer deploys a Microsoft Intune configuration policy to configure BitLocker End Point Protection on a Windows 10 device running 1703. When attempting to setup BitLocker Endpoint Protection it prompts to encrypt the device via a work account, verifies that the device is not already encrypted, and displays error: "Error something went wrong. Try again later". The device is never encrypted.
rootCause: Unknown
solution: Deploy a device restriction settings password policy to require encryption.

symptom: When attempting to join a Windows 10 PC to Azure Active Directory, the join fails with error: Something went wrong. Confirm you are using the correct sign-in information and that your organization uses this feature. You can try to do this again or contact your system administrator with the error code 80180026.
rootCause: This can occur if both of the following conditions exist: MDM auto-enrollment is enabled in Azure, and either the PC agent (Intune agent) or the Configuration Manager client agent is installed on the Windows 10 computer.
solution: Either disable MDM auto-enrollment in Azure, or uninstall the PC agent or SCCM client agent. Once this is done the Azure AD join operation should complete successfully.

symptom: After switching the MDM authority from Intune to SCCM, all devices show up in the Configuration Manager admin console like they should with the exception of any devices configured without user affinity. These same devices appear in the web portal and check in as expected. The only symptom is that the devices do not appear in the CM console.
rootCause: This is caused by a bug in the migration process. As of this writing, this will have to be manually mitigated on the backend with an ops procedure.
solution: To resolve this issue, escalate via an IET task. See KB 2716418 for details on how create an escalation.

symptom: Administrators received an informational alert regarding the Intune client software being updated and the previous version expiring soon.
rootCause: A new version of the Intune PC agent has been available and the previous version will no longer be valid.
solution: Windows machines that already have the Intune PC agent installed will have it updated automatically. The alert is to ensure administrators use the newer version for new enrollments.

symptom: Customer configures an Azure Intune Device Restrictions Profile with Kiosk Mode enabled. Google Chrome is added via app package name or deep link. Samsung KNOX device enrolled but Chrome never appears.
rootCause: By design
solution: Only managed apps can be added to Kiosk Mode via an Intune policy.

symptom: Apple DEP devices managed via SCCM in supervised mode with policy to block automatic app downloads. Device shows message about i.manage.microsoft.com updating an app from App Store.
rootCause: By design
solution: This setting does not affect app updates.

symptom: Onboarding Windows Defender ATP with Microsoft Intune - guide for OMA-URI settings, policy creation, and detection test.
Customer wants to make Intune web apps available when applying iOS configuration policy for Show or hide apps.
solution: Deploy apps to device, then in Device restrictions profile add web app using bundle ID com.app.webapp with URL.

symptom: Recent app purchases from Store for Education not showing up in Apps blade.
rootCause: Sync may take up to 12 hours for new apps, 24 hours for Private Store apps.
solution: Wait for sync to complete. Check last sync time on Apps blade.

symptom: Intune for Education Express Configuration walkthrough - groups, apps, and settings setup.
Intune for Education reporting is limited - Device Inventory, App Inventory, Settings Error reports available as CSV.
solution: Use Enterprise version of Intune for fuller reporting.

symptom: Intune for Education Tenant Settings - IT dept email, privacy URL, support URL, Windows Defender and Device Update settings guide.

Intune for Education: Take a Test Profile See https://technet.microsoft.com/edu/windows/take-tests-in-windows-10 for more info 1. Log into the Intune for Education Console (https://intuneeducation.portal.azure.com). The dashboard appears once an admin is logged in. 2. Click on Take a Test Profile (pencil icon) on the left navigation 3. Click on Add Test Profile. 4. Name the Profile 5. Add a URL for the testing location. This must have http:// or https:// before the address. a. PCs signed into with a testing account are locked down to only Edge and the test URL 6. Choose whether Screen Capture is allowed. Default is No. 7. Select a User account. Only users with Office 365 for Faculty licenses are visible. a. The recommendation is to create specific testing accounts in portal.office.com b. See You can use Take a Test to set up a test for a single PC or multiple PCs. Follow these links to learn how: i. Set up Take a Test on a single PC ii. Set up Take a Test on multiple PCs 8. Click Save. 9. Profile summary is shown with a warning that no groups have been assigned. 10. Click on the Groups tab 11. Click on Change Group Assignment. a. Assignments can be groups of Users or Devices 12. The list of available groups is on the right side of the screen 13. For this example, we are selecting Students of MobPod Academy 14. Click on Add Members and click OK. 15. Now the profile shows the assignment and the status

Intune for Education: Groups1. Log into the Intune for Education Console (https://intuneeducation.portal.azure.com). The dashboard appears once an admin is logged in.2. Click on Groups on the left navigation or the Manage user and device groups on the Manage user and device groups dashboard.Create a Group1. Click on the + Create Group button at the top left of the screen.2. Enter a Group Name3. Click on Create Group at the bottom of the screen.4. By default, you are taken to the properties page for the newly created group with options for actions highlighted in boxes NOTE: In this example, the group was named �Mobpod Academy� but the look is different from Contoso High School and Fabrikam High School, the schools setup with the sample data from O365-EDU-Tools GitHub site and School Data Sync. 5. Click on Create sub group and enter �Students of <name of your school>6. Now this is looking similar to �Fabrikam High School�.7. The new group is incorrectly labeled as �Student� instead of �Students�. This needs to be corrected.8. Highlight the sub-group Student of Mobpod Academy and click on Rename at the top of the screen.9. Correct the name and click OK.10. Click Ok.Creating a group in the Intune for Education Portal creates a Security Group in Azure Active Directory, which is visible in the Office portal and Azure Portal. Group Properties and SettingsEach group has a Devices, Users, Apps, and Settings tab. Important items to note� In the Settings UI, configurations applied to All Users do not show as configured for a selected group� Groups can have their own settings, however, with most restrictive setting wins.� Users cannot be modified in Intune. Use portal.office.com or Azure Active Directory to do so. Move a Group1. Create a group following the workflow above called �School Administration�2. Click on Move Group button3. Highlight the group for the desired destination4. Click OkGroup Settings1. Select the group from the left to apply settings2. Click on the Settings tab3. Click on the downward facing caret to expand options4. Select the parameter to change5. Click SaveGroup InheritanceWhen you use Intune for Education, you have the choice of having group settings flow down to member groups (default Intune behavior) or breaking inheritance so that a child group does not inherit ANY settings from its parent group. In the latter case when you break inheritance, we create a DC profile assigned directly to the child group and place an exclusion assignment for the child group on the DC profile assigned to the parent group. When inheritance is reset using the experience the customer describes, we delete any DC profiles assigned directly to the child group and remove the exclusion on the parent group DC profile (basically undoing what we did when the user broke inheritance). In this case since we detected a profile assigned directly to the child group, we show that inheritance is broken and offer the user the option to reset it. Based on some recent feedback we updated our logic here to ignore any profile types that are not exposed in Intune for Education (e.g. VPN profiles), but since we do expose iOS device restrictions in the console, we end up deleting the profile in this case. The current recommendation for customers seeking to configure profiles in big Intune that should not be changed by delegated admins in Intune for Education is to utilize scope tags so that the profiles created by central IT in big Intune are not visible to delegated admins using Intune for Education.

Intune for Education Enrollment Accounts 1. Log into the Intune for Education Console (https://intuneeducation.portal.azure.com). The dashboard appears once an admin is logged in. 2. Select Enrollment Accounts from the left navigation 3. Select + New Enrollment Account 4. Search for a name to apply the Enrollment Account role. No Students are shown in the list. Only users with a license for Office 365 for Faculty are shown. a. For this workflow a user NCDevice was created 5. Select the user to become a Device Enrollment account. 6. Click Save. 7. To remove Device Enrollment Privileges, click on the Demote button.
The table dbo.CertificateData in the ConfigMgr DB is showing for the Subscription Certificate the following columns as NULL:ThumbprintValidToEncryptionPublicKey
rootCause: Corruption of the Intune Subscription Certificate table due to upgrade of Configuration Manager or failed configuration of the Intune Subscription.
solution: You will need to escalate this service request to IET, once there they will create an IcM request.Once the IcM is requested, the CxP team can perform the command ImportCertificateToSCCM.exe /SiteCode=<SiteCode> with a internal tool that we have.

symptom: Occasionally when a .msi application is deployed to Windows 10 MDM devices the Intune service returns error "0x87D1041C"However the package actually installs successfully on the device.
rootCause: This behavior is occurring because the package has the ability to update itself from the internet during installation.Thus the Intune service detects a lower version after installation then what was deployed causing the error.
solution: Configure the MSI app to ignore the version check process as per: Add a Windows line-of-business app to Microsoft Intune | Microsoft Learn You can configure a known self-updating mobile MSI app to ignore the version check process. Some MSI installer-based apps are automatically updated by the app developer. For these automatically updated MSI apps, you can configure the Ignore app version setting in the App information pane. When you switch this setting to Yes, Microsoft Intune will not enforce the app version that's installed on the Windows client. This capability is useful to avoid getting into a race condition. For instance, a race condition can occur when the app is automatically updated by the app developer and is updated by Intune. Both might try to enforce a version of the app on a Windows client, which creates a conflict.

symptom: You experience one or more of the following:Unable to enroll mobile device"Profile Install Failed" errorAffected units keep cycling through "enrollment update available", but the update does not enable enrollment and just returns to the same prompt.The error "Company Portal Temporarily Unavailable" appears when trying to update enrollment on iOSDmpDownloader.log or Kusto shows the following error:...is not eligible to enroll a device of type 'WindowsPhone'. Reason 'DeviceCapReached'. SMS_DMP_DOWNLOADER 2014-01-27 12:59:57 9132 (0x23AC)
rootCause: This is by design. Users have a default enrollment limit of 15 devices.This can also be caused by an end user deleting and re-enrolling a device until the daily cap per user is reached. Additionally there is a weekly enrollment cap, a job runs every seven (7) days to clear device enrollment attempts.Another possible cause is due to a known issue with Windows Phone not having the correct date and time. It will keep registering new devices in Azure and cause other platforms to fail to enroll.
solution: By default end users are limited to enrolling 15 devices. If they have less than 15 devices enrolled then follow these steps to mitigate the device cap. First step is to login to the Company Portal at http://portal.manage.microsoft.com and remove extra device records. Next step is to login to the admin console at https://admin.manage.microsoft.com (or check the CM console) and remove extra device records. If customers are in a testing phase then they can avoid the device enrollment cap for their IT Pros by using Device Enrollment Managers, see Enroll devices using a device enrollment manager account - Microsoft Intune | Microsoft Learn To increase the device cap limit then login to https://admin.manage.microsoft.com, click Admin / Enrollment Rules and change the Device Enrollment Limit (max of 15)

symptom: After configuring an iOS device in DEP to be supervised and to allow Activation Lock Bypass, when the device is enrolled into a hybrid Intune/Configuration Manager tenant, the device does not appear as Supervised and "bypass activation lock" is grayed out (greyed out) in the SCCM admin console.Note that this mainly occurs when the device has been retired/wiped and then re-enrolled.
rootCause: This is a known issue per 117062093452694, 117021415317116, 117030315402861 and 117030994129937This bug is fixed in Intune 1801. See the following IcMs for details:3298805255237785
solution: To resolve this problem, upgrade your Configuration Manager environment to the latest release, then either unenroll and re-enroll any affected devices in Intune once Intune 1801 is available. All tenants should be on 1801 by the end of January 2018. Alternatively, you can run the following query using the Device ID of the affected device (found in the MDMClientIdentity table):INSERT INTO MDMDeviceProperty (DeviceID, PropertyName, PropertyValue) VALUES ('deviceID', 'IsSupervised', 'True')For example:INSERT INTO MDMDeviceProperty (DeviceID, PropertyName, PropertyValue) VALUES ('6191CA89-FBEB-47C3-BE01-ECFBA36084FA', 'IsSupervised', 'True')

symptom: If you have a pre-declared serial number for a DEP device in SCCM you may be unable to delete the entry in the console. This may cause the device to not to be able to re-enroll.
rootCause: A bug was filed but not yet fixed. The reason the problem occurs is a sql table in SCCM database is not getting set properly.
solution: SMS: 494132: [CDCR] Unable to delete the iOS device from the PreDeclared list after removing from DEP profileThe temporary fix is to alter the sql table but is not recommended.

symptom: When using the Intune Preview Graph API to update �emailAddress� and/or �managementAgent�, using /PATCH on a managedDevices described in the documentation (https://developer.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/docs/api-reference/beta/api/intune_devicefe_manageddevice_update), the API just returns:{ 'error': { 'code': 'InternalError', 'message': 'An error has occurred - Operation ID (for customer support): 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 - Activity ID: <activity Id> - Url: https://fef.amsub0102.manage.microsoft.com/DeviceFE/StatelessDeviceFEService/managedDevices<device id>?api-version=2017-05-18 - CustomApiErrorPhrase: ', 'innerError': { 'request-id': <request id>, 'date': <date> } }}
rootCause: The /PATCH method only supports changing two attributes.The only attributes of existing device object which can be "patched" are ownership and category
solution: https://icm.ad.msft.net/imp/v3/incidents/details/46138189/home

symptom: Intune : Device Inventory in Ibiza portal shows less inventory than in Silverlight. Once customer is migrated the Silverlight reporting is broken.
rootCause: This issue is considered to be DCR by development group and may be fixed in future release.
solution: https://icm.ad.msft.net/imp/v3/incidents/details/38804597/home

symptom: An end-user device is blocked by the Intune on-premises Exchange connector, however the conditional access/quarantine email is not received. Exchange connector logs show: ProcessConditionalAccessWithNotificationCommand failed - The remote certificate is invalid according to the validation procedure.
rootCause: The CAS that the Intune on-premises Exchange connector is pointing to has a certificate issue; likely an expired certificate.
solution: Navigate to the EWS URL of the CAS the Intune Exchange connector points to and verify certificate. Exchange administrator needs to fix the certificate issue. See Renew an Exchange Certificate steps.

symptom: When a device security or compliance policy change is made for Android devices in Microsoft Intune, Android users are prompted in the notification window that action is needed to comply with the new policy. There is no way to manually force the policy change from Intune itself.
OMA-URI ./Vendor/MSFT/Policy/Config/Start/HideAppList policy is not applying to MDM enrolled Windows 10 1703 enterprise or education computers. The policy has no effect on enrolled devices.
rootCause: This is a client side bug fixed in Windows 10 RS3.
solution: Upgrade to Windows 10 RS3. ICM 43249767.

symptom: When a user enrolls an iPad device into the Intune Service and is targeted by Conditional Access, an error occurs prompting the user to register and enroll the device.
solution: iPhone devices work, but with the iPad, the customer will need to download the Microsoft Authenticator application.

symptom: When opening the Intune Web Company Portal, the end user is not seeing any apps that were deployed as Available.
rootCause: IW Portal team made a behavior change to support mobile app install without enrollment. The portal no longer forces the user to identify the device when logging on.
solution: Open portal.manage.microsoft.com, sign in, click menu > My Devices, click on a listed device to select it, verify device shows with a checkmark.

symptom: Windows 10: UI option to enroll device into Intune is unavailable or greyed out. Possible causes: device already connected to organization, device already managed, user is not local admin, UAC set to highest level, ExternallyManaged registry key set to 1.
App Protection Policy for Adobe Reader for Intune created from Intune on Azure portal and deployed with no errors, but the policy does not reach the app.
rootCause: The Adobe Reader for Intune app does not yet incorporate the new MAM SDK needed for MAM deployments from Ibiza.
solution: Remove the App Protection Policy from Ibiza. Deploy the enlightened Adobe Reader for Intune app from legacy console and tie it to a legacy MAM policy.

symptom: Limited administrator with Intune Service Administrator role is unable to view or manage Azure Active Directory Enterprise applications, but can navigate and manage the Intune on Azure blade with no issues.
rootCause: This blade is limited to global administrators and limited administrators that have the Security administrator role.
solution: Grant the Security administrator role to the limited administrator: Login to portal.azure.com as global admin > Intune > Users > Find user > Directory role > Check Security administrator > Save.

symptom: Intune: Internal Trial Account Requests and Extensions - how to sign up for EMS trial, request trial extensions, and manage employee usage rights accounts.
Use the following method to determine real time spdrsactivation queue processing is working, and more information about the data that is in queue and being processed.
SCEP requests for mobile devices stop at "Sending request to certificate registration point." in the NDESplugin.log.
rootCause: NDES policy module is using a thumbprint from an expired client authentication certificate that was selected when the CM policy module or Standalone Certificate connector was initially installed.
solution: 1) In regedit go to HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MSCEP\Modules\NDESPolicy. Open the key named NDESCertThumbprint. 2) Goto Start -> Run and type "certlm.msc". Expand Personal and click on Certificates 3) Look for an expired certificate that has the Intended Purpose of Client Authentication and a Issued To or Common Name that matches the NDES sever name. 4) Double click the certificate and go to Details. Scroll down to thumbprint and confirm it matches the regkey in step 1. 5) Hit ok to go back and right click the certificate in the list then select All Tasks -> Request Certificate with New Key 6) Hit next on the Certificate Enrollment dialog that comes up then click the link that says "More information is required to enroll for this certificate. Click here to configure settings. 7) Under the Subject Tab in the first drop down under Subject name select "Common Name" as type and under value enter the NDES server's FQDN then hit Add. 8) On the second drop down box under Alternative name select "DNS" and enter in the server's FQDN again then hit Add. 9) Click ok then Enroll then click Finish once enrollment is successful. 10) Double click the new certificate and go to Details. Scroll down to Thumbprint, hightlight the thumbprint and Ctrl-C. 11) Open notepad and paste the thumbprint of the new certificate and remove all the spaces. 12) Save the thumbprint as a text file hitting OK on the warning message that comes up about Unicode format. 13) Reopen the text file and copy the thumbprint making sure to not copy the question mark at the beginning of the line. 14) Paste the new thumbprint into NDESCertThumbprint regkey from step 1. 15) Open an Administrator command prompt and type "IISreset"

symptom: deleted

There is a known issue in System Center Configuration Manager 1702 where a missing setting in Android for Work email profiles prevents conditional access from working properly on enrolled Android for Work (work profiles) devices. Instead of the devices having access to email based on their compliance to policy, devices that receive these profiles are prevented from accessing email regardless of policy compliance. This issue only impacts email profiles for Android for Work; Android email profiles are not affected.
rootCause: [Cause is included in summary]
solution: This issue can be resolved by installing System Center Configuration Manager 1706. There is no hotfix for email profiles created in earlier versions of Configuration Manager. If you are experiencing this issue and do not wish to update to System Center Configuration Manager 1706, you should contact Microsoft Support to add the missing setting into affected profiles in your environment.

When configuring the option "Use private store only" in the Intune for EDU portal the policy shows "Not Applicable" when deployed to students computers
rootCause: Targeted computers have Windows PRO Education which does not support the RequirePrivateStoreOnly CSP policy
solution: The RequirePrivateStoreOnly CSP policy is not supported on Windows 10 PRO Education because it is equivalent to the Windows 10 PRO SKU. It is only supported with Windows 10 Education because it aligns with the Windows 10 Enterprise SKUThe CSP reference documentation below outlines the applicability of various CSPs for the different editions of Windows:https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/client-management/mdm/configuration-service-provider-reference

symptom: Customer requesting deletion of VPP tokenMultiple VPP tokens in admin portal
rootCause: Update 12/19/17: The option to delete VPP tokens was added to the Ibiza UI with the 1712 service update. Customers may now delete their VPP token as follows: Login to portal.azure.com. On the Intune blade, go to Mobile apps/ Setup/ iOS VPP tokens. Highlight the VPP token that you would like to delete. Open the ... menu, hit Delete, then OK. If the steps above should fail, please engage IET as detailed below. <MS Internal Only>First, Send the following template to the customer to complete the required checklistSecond, Ask the customer if they have any VPP apps deployed. If they have apps deployed, then ask the below questions in the template for choice A or B. Otherwise the questions do not apply - because if the customer does not have any apps deployed then there are no licenses to be reclaimed or lost.Third, Update the Support Topic to <Intune Service Administration/Provisioning> and Set the wait state in MSSolve to <Solution Delivered � Pending Confirmation> once you�ve sent the template to the customer
solution: Template for Customer CommunicationMy name is <SE name> and I will be assisting you with your case. I understand you are requesting deletion of your existing VPP tokens. In order to proceed with the request, we need to confirm the following information: Tenant ID: <the domain used to login to service (IE intune.onmicrosoft.com)>Deletion of just 1 or all tokens: <If just 1 then provide the following><If the email of the associated Apple ID is unique then let us know which apple ID to remove><If the tokens use the same Apple ID then let us know the expiry date of the token to be removed><If the tokens are identical then sync the token you wish to keep and we will delete the oldest sync time>Confirmation of choice A or B You are OK to potentially lose the license data for the VPP Apps ( If we delete the VPP tokens, you will no longer be able to uninstall apps from Intune and recover the licenses automatically. You will then be required to contact Apple in order to reclaim any outstanding licenses.) OR If you are not OK to potentially lose the licenses, then, before we delete the VPP state, please trigger an uninstall action (create the uninstall deployment) from Intune and ensure that the apps have been uninstalled for the Intune users. Once you are able to confirm that the apps have been uninstalled, please let me know and then I can proceed with deleting the VPP deletion request Note: This a highly disruptive process that will require you to halt your VPP deployments and reconfigure them after it's complete.Next Steps:Once the checklist has been verified than the process for VPP token deletions can take up to 5 business days, but typically occur within 3 days. Do not attempt to configure VPP tokens until your support engineer has confirmed this request has been completed successfully. Doing this prematurely may cause corruption and/or impact your ability to use our service

symptom: If a WIHOT does not already exist, then this error below may qualify for an automatic escalation, so we can file a bug with our OPS team:"An error occurred" or the logs may show "An internal server error has occurred"or the logs may show "The service is unavailable"Questions to ask1. Gather all logs, account information, and environment information as well as information on the failure Is it portal wide? Is it a single workload or view? 2. Is there maintenance scheduled or currently happening Check on Teams to see if there is any known maintenanceLog in to the problem MSU If you have a repro account, start there Otherwise, you may create an account: Creating Test Tenants With Tenant Monkey 3. Determine the nature of the problem Is the account logging in successfully? Some problems can be solved by clearing browser cache (make sure you close all other browser instances) or using InPrivate Mode if it's a cache issue4. Things to document before escalating this issue:Confirm the customer has an active Intune subscription on the affected tenant. Confirm that the MDM authority is set to Intune or Office 365. (CM Hybrid customers should use the CM portal to change settings)Confirm that an Intune license is assigned to the GA affected (required for some sections of the Intune Azure portal)An example Global Administrator that is affectedSteps to reproduce the error. (More details are recommended)Obtain the followinga. Screenshot of the customers browser showing error messageb. Copy of Customer�s full URL when getting errorc. Copy of all Request and Correlation ID�s displayed Caveats:A TL may have additional questions or troubleshooting steps for the customer, before processing the escalation.This error can be generated if a GA access the WSFB, VPA, or AFW pages and they do not have an Intune or EMS license assigned. If assigning a license resolves the issue then there is no need to escalate the case.

First, Determine if customer is using User Licensing or Device Licensing iOS VPP user licensing can allow multiple devices to be registered with Apple using the same Intune user account. however iOS VPP user licensing only allows an end user to install an app on 5-10 devices.. That means the first few enrolled devices would get the VPP app installed using user licensing, and the other devices will not get the app. If you deploy user licensing to Intune device groups then we will check the adonization to the Intune user record that owns the device and claim the license against the first Apple ID used by that user with the service.If you target User Licensing against a device group and the devices do not have user affinity then each user must have their own Apple ID to perform the Apple ID to device assocationiOS VPP device licensing associates each VPP license to each unique device and does not require an Apple IDIs the VPP app deployed to a device group or user group?If you assign a volume-purchased app for a device enrolled through a Device Enrollment Profile or Apple Configurator that does not have user affinity, only apps that are targeted to device groups work. Collect Device ID and name of VPP AppHow to look up the Intune Device ID if no user is associated to Corporate enrolled deviceIn customer's Intune Azure portal navigate to Intune / Devices / All Devices / <Device> / HardwareThe Unique device ID will correspond to Intune Device ID used in all Kusto queries Check Kusto for VPP app status - Note: ActivityId in the below queries is usually the Intune Device IDDeviceManagementProvider | where env_time > ago(2d) | where ActivityId == "96592024-3508-400d-80b9-97dc966b157c" | where message contains "<partial name of app>" or message contains "<partialname of app>" | project env_time, EventMessage, message iOSPlugin[IsVppAppReadyForInstall]: iOS VPP app, iTunesStoreId = '333710667', AppName = 'Scanner Pro - PDF document scanner app with OCR' is not ready for install, since user 516c09c3-65ab-4500-9fe2-2670a53239e7 with vppApplicationTokenId 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 yet to accept VPP invite. Cause: User hasn't accepted VPP invite. If user did not get invite, Confirm that the Apple Store is not blocked (use hide app option instead)InternalErrorException: Received unknown VPP error code: 9632 with VPP error message: Too many recent calls to manage licenses with identical requests - Operation ID (for customer support): a28c8660-1e85-431d-bd42-a8c3239337f7Cause: Either too many devices for 1 user or there is too many Apple ID's making requests from the same company. Use device based licensing insteadVPP Error Code: 9616Cause:The issue here is that user is using an AppleId already being used with the sToken by some other user, so some apps for which the other user associated with the AppleId has licenses will be allowed install by AppleLicense assignment for this user from Intune will fail with 9616 (regUsersAlreadyAssigned) since Apple recorded Intune user using a pre-used AppleId when user first accepted the VPP agreement and shared AppleId with Apple.Resolution:Move to device licensing that does not involve user�s AppleIdCreate a brand new Intune user for this user and associate a brand new AppleId with itIf there is no specific error then take the application ID (highlighted) and search on thatDeviceManagementProvider | where env_time > ago(2d) | where ActivityId == "96592024-3508-400d-80b9-97dc966b157c" | where message contains "87034542-b9e2-4874-aa9a-89797ccc5569" | project env_time, messageInstall action pre-requisites for AppId:ScopeId_87034542-b9e2-4874-aa9a-89797ccc5569:Application_87034542-b9e2-4874-aa9a-89797ccc5569:1, DT-Id:ScopeId_87034542-b9e2-4874-aa9a-89797ccc5569:DeploymentType_87034542-b9e2-4874-aa9a-89797ccc5569:1,are not satisfied. Status is APP_CI_ENFORCEMENT_IN_PROGRESS. Pre-requisites will be checked in a future DT processing session. Returning without calling the install action. Cause: Device has seen the application install request but some pre-req is not met, run the following query to check pre-req details DeviceManagementProvider | where env_time > ago(2d) | where ActivityId == "96592024-3508-400d-80b9-97dc966b157c" | where message contains "87034542-b9e2-4874-aa9a-89797ccc5569" | where message contains "IsVppAppReadyForInstall"or message contains "VppLicense" or message contains "VPPService" | project env_time, message iOSPlugin[IsVppAppReadyForInstall]: VPP user licensing not supported for userless enrollment Cause: Check targeting of the app is set to device licensing and escalate if it is Did not find VppLicense with ApplicationProductId 1235115317 for Apple VPP user 7ca4bdd4-5849-4a4f-b535-adbd41ac9a4b and deviceId 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 for account 4bf3add6-2ea1-451f-a8bb-c7063e6694f0 using applicationTokenId 4bf3add6-2ea1-451f-a8bb-c7063e6694f0.Cause: VPP license was already claimed by same Apple ID on another device, use device based instead (check app ID in Itunes store to confirm this is correct app using highlighted number All App installs fails for a given user-> Intune says license already exists, apple says license could not be found-> We sync licenses for each user with Apple regularly, so data on Intune side would be consistent with Apple.Check In logs - DeviceManagementProvider| where env_time >= ago(1d)| where ActivityId == "<Intune Device ID>" | where message contains "scan" or EventMessage contains "scan" // "scan" = some part of the app name"or message contains "539809170" or EventMessage contains "539809170" // "539809170" = adamIdor message contains "68e23d82-cf4b-4315-acfc-b13e0823dd6d" or EventMessage contains "68e23d82-cf4b-4315-acfc-b13e0823dd6d" // "68e23d82-cf4b-4315-acfc-b13e0823dd6d" is Intune application Id| where message contains "LicenseNotFound" or EventMessage contains "LicenseNotFound"| project env_time, message, EventMessagePossible Cause: The issue here is that somewhere during the lifecycle of Intune, the user has updated his AppleId, the reason Apple rejects the install request with LicenseNotFound is that the AppleId recorded in Apple�s system against the Intune-user does not match the AppleId user is logged in with.(It could be that the Apple Id was updated on one of the devices, not all, but the effect would be the same)Resolution:Log back in with the AppleId used earlier, basically the AppleId with which the user accepted Apple�s VPP agreement the very first time.Move to device licensing that does not involve user�s AppleIdCreate a brand new Intune user for this user and associate a brand new AppleId with itCheck Intune service to see if VPP app is being synced from AppleLook up app in public store - https://itunes.apple.com/us/app/adobe-acrobat-reader-for-microsoft-intune/id1051980424VppFeatureTelemetry | where env_time > ago(2d) | where accountId == "4bf3add6-2ea1-451f-a8bb-c7063e6694f0" | where productId == "1051980424" | project env_time, userId, deviceId, TaskName, ActivityId, relatedActivityId2If you get results 'VppDeviceLicenseAssignmentFailedEvent' and user is getting message "Your organization has depleted its licenses for this app" then they have exhausted their User licensing for this app.They need to either change to device license or temporarily revoke deployment of the app to the user to reclaim the licenses.Apps are not updatingVPP auto update is not supported for devices with OS version < 11.3 (apps will not be updated even if tenant has auto-update set to on)

Right-click each of the relevant categories for your machine, Executable, Windows Installer, Script, Packaged app (Windows Store Apps). *NOTE Most machines will only need Executable Rules (for.exe) and Packaged App Rules (Store Apps, .appx apps, and Universal Apps). 5. Click Create Default Rules. *NOTE Always do this first, if the default rules are not in place Windows can get into a broken state where it is unusable and the machine will need to be re-formatted or reset. 6. Right-click on the type of policy you want to create (Executable, Packaged App, etc.) and click Create New Rule. 7. This screen allows us to select a user or group to interact with and allow or deny them use of an application. 8. The next page has options to block apps by Publisher, Path, or File Hash. Path and File Hash are best to block individual apps. Publisher is useful for wider control of a
