After using Intune to assign a PFX certificate profile to a group of iOS and Android devices, the certificate never gets to the devices and errors similar to the following appear in the NDES Connector log:IssuePfx - COMException: System.Runtime.InteropServices.COMException (0x800706BA): CCertRequest::Submit: The RPC server is unavailable. 0x800706ba (WIN32: 1722 RPC_S_SERVER_UNAVAILABLE) IssuePfx -Generic Exception: System.ArgumentException: CCertRequest::Submit: The parameter is incorrect. 0x80070057 (WIN32: 87 ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETER)IssuePfx - COMException: System.Runtime.InteropServices.COMException (0x80094800): The requested certificate template is not supported by this CA. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x80094800)
rootCause: This can occur if the CA certificate had been renewed but the CA has not been enabled to support certificate requests signed by the old certificate. When the CA certificate is renewed, the OCSP Response signing certificate used for validation of existing certificates must still be signed by the CA certificate that was used to issue the existing certificates and the new CA certificate, however by default this is not enabled.To verify this, check for Event ID 128 in the Application Event Log every time a certificate request comes in:Log Name: Application:Source: Microsoft-Windows-CertificationAuthorityEvent ID: 128Level: WarningDetails:An Authority Key Identifier was passed as part of the certificate request 2268. This feature has not been enabled. To enable specifying a CA key for certificate signing, run: "certutil -setreg ca\UseDefinedCACertInRequest 1" and then restart the service.
solution: To resolve this problem complete the following:1. From elevated Command Prompt (Run as Administrator), run certutil -setreg ca\UseDefinedCACertInRequest 1.2. Restart the Certificate Services service.Once the Certificate Services service is restarted, devices should start receiving certificates.

symptom: When you try to access secured site (https)  in Intune Managed Browser for Android with a MAM policy applied  you get the following message listed below. Access to this website has been blocked to protect you. There is problem with this website security certificate with a security certificate can indicate a attempt to intercept data you send to a website.
rootCause: This can be caused by a problem with the web site certificate.
solution: The Intune Managed Browser on Android device needs to trust the entire certificate chain.  If there is problem with the certificate web site certificate it will block the content.   To help determine what website is causing the problem with the Intune Managed Browser you can use a website like the one listed below.    https://whatsmychaincert.com/https://www.sslshopper.com/ssl-checker.htmlhttps://sslanalyzer.comodoca.com/

symptom: Attempting to sign-in to Office 365 applications with a user account other than the Device Enrollment Manager (DEM) user account that the iOS device was initially enrolled with, the following message is encountered: Your admin wants the apps on this device to be managed with the account '<first account>'. The app account you are using '<second account>' will be removed. To access your organizations data with the account '<second users account>' you must un-enroll your device from the company portal.
rootCause: This is currently expected behavior.
solution: As a workaround, you can use a DEP or Apple Configurator profile which doesn't require user affinity. If a DEM account type of enrollment is required, you can ask the users to use the web based access for the applications/resources.

symptom: Customer unable to add groups and is configured as a service administrator
rootCause: Customer does not have the correct role in Azure. Customer is listed as a billing administrator.
solution:
1.
    The account must be configured as a user admin. 
  
  
    a.  In the Azure classic portal, click Active Directory, and then click the name 
  
  
          of your organization�s directory.
  
  
      
    b.  On the Users page, click the display name of the user you want to edit.
  
  
      
    c.  In the Organizational Role list, select the administrator role that you
  
  
          want to assign to this user, or select User if you want to remove an 
  
  
           existing administrator role.
  
  
    d.  In the Alternate Email Address box, type an email address. This email
  
  
         address is used for important notifications, including password self-
  
  
         reset, so the user must be able to access the email account whether or 
  
  
         not the user can access Azure.
  
  
    e.  Select Allow or Block to specify whether to allow the user to sign in and                     
  
  
         access services.
  
  
    f.   Specify a location from the Usage Location drop-down list.
  
  
    g.  When you have finished, click Save.

symptom: When deploying a device restriction policy for MDM devices you are getting an errorUnder the column status details you see error -2016281112 for password policy values like 'Required Password Type' or 'Maximum minutes of inactivity until screen locks'l screen lThis is happening because the end user has not accepted the password change. The policy will show an error status until the user changes their password to meet the requirements.On the Android platform, the end user must accept the password change notification as described at:2993046 Intune: How device security policy changes are implemented for Androidhttps://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-us/help/2993046/IntuneHowdevicesecuritypolicychangesareimplementedforAndroidOn the Windows MDM Desktop platform the user must press CTRL-ALT-DEL, click Change Password, and the new password rules will be enforced

After enabling "Unknown Sources" on an Android device and sideloading apps, the device is compliant and passes conditional access checks for Exchange Online even though there is an Intune Device Compliance policy set to "Block apps from unknown sources".
rootCause: This is by design.
solution: The policy setting for "Block apps from unknown sources" simply checks the setting for �Unknown Sources� under Settings -> Security on an Android device, and evaluates conditional access policy based on the current state. It does not block the installation of sideloaded apps itself, nor will it remove sideloaded apps if they are already installed.

symptom: When switching MDM authority in Hybrid to Intune, if an admin excludes more than 20 users at a time from MDM collection, cloud usersync mail fail to sync the changes and would throw following error in cloudusersync.logERROR: RemoveLicensedUsers exception Microsoft.Management.Services.Common.ServiceTooBusyException: SendingODataRequest ---> System.TimeoutException: operation timed out for service: StatelessConnectorService,
rootCause: Product issue which will be fixed in a future release
solution: Version 8239 � SCCM 2012 SP2/R2 Sp1 onwards following registry key cold be created to reduce the batch size.DWORD: UserBatchUploadSize under HKLM\Software\Microsoft\SMS\Components\SMS_CLOUD_USERSYNCValue: 20 (Decimal)Restart SMS_Executive service or Cloudusersync thread

symptom: After enrolling an iOS device and assigning an app protection policy to Outlook, performing a �Remove Company Data� action on a user�s device from the Azure Intune blade -> Devices -> All Devices, it unenrolls the device, however no data is ever removed from Outlook and the profile remains.
rootCause: This can occur if the Outlook app has not been deployed to the user from Intune. This is a requirement for "Remove Company Data" to remove email on iOS.
solution: To resolve this problem, deploy the Outlook mobile app as a Required app to the iOS users. Once that is done, if you then go to the he Azure Intune blade -> Devices -> All Devices and issue a "Remove Company Data" action for a users device, the Outlook profile should be removed within a minute or two and the device will become unmanaged.

symptom: Intune is making a key change to how conditional access compliance state is evaluated for targeted devices. How a user/device targeted by Conditional access works today (Prior to 1803 Sprint Deployment):� targeted by conditional access rule requiring compliant device� not targeted by any compliance policy� marked as compliant� can access corporate resources protected by conditional access How a user/device targeted by Conditional access works after Secure-by-Default - as part of 1803 - End of March 2018:� targeted by conditional access rule requiring compliant device� not targeted by any compliance policy� marked as non-compliant Admin may change this, but this is the new default setting (If they have been using compliance policies) The setting can be found under Compliance policy > Compliance Policy Settings � blocked from accessing any corporate resources protected by conditional accessThe change is to make sure devices are �Secure By Default.� Currently, if a group is targeted for conditional access, but there is no compliance policy set by which to evaluate compliance state, the device is marked as Compliant.After Secure by Default, the admin will have the option as to whether these devices are Compliant or Non-Compliant when they don�t have policy to evaluate. This setting will ship as turned ON and these devices will be marked as �not compliant�.Given the nature of the change there is an anticipated impact to devices that may be in this grandfathered allowed state, and to maintain access to targeted services, the Admin will have to take action for these devices. This feature released March 2018. Customers should ensure that devices have at least one compliance policy assigned to them or take one of the other suggested actions.Customers will be notified via message center as each scale unit begins enforcing the compliance policy requirement. Blog post: https://blogs.technet.microsoft.com/intunesupport/2018/02/09/updated-upcoming-security-enhancements-in-the-intune-service/ Intune Readiness TeamEMM CxP - Support as a Feature

After deploying profiles that uses NDES and SCEP certificates to iOS and Android devices, the devices initially received the certificates, however beginning at a certain date, no further certificates are delivered.You will also notice the following:- The customer's NDES URL still provides the expected 403 Forbidden response.- In the Azure Intune portal under Device Configurations -> Certificate Authority, the status shows "Error" and the Last Connected timestamp is some time in the past (The connector should communicate and update "Last connected" every 5 minutes).- The IIS Logs (in C:\inetpub\Logs) will contain entries similar to the following:2018-03-22 17:23:11 fe80::b4d4:78b4:bce6:b69d%12 GET /certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll operation=GetCACert&message=SCEP%20Authority 443 - profiled/1.0+CFNetwork/894+Darwin/17.4.0 - 200 0 0 02018-03-22 17:23:11 fe80::b4d4:78b4:bce6:b69d%12 GET /certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll operation=GetCACaps&message=SCEP%20Authority 443 - profiled/1.0+CFNetwork/894+Darwin/17.4.0 - 200 0 0 152018-03-22 17:23:12 fe80::b4d4:78b4:bce6:b69d%12 POST /CertificateRegistrationSvc/Certificate/VerifyRequest - 443 - NDES_Plugin - 201 0 0 932018-03-22 17:23:12 fe80::b4d4:78b4:bce6:b69d%12 GET /certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll ... 443 - profiled/1.0+CFNetwork/894+Darwin/17.4.0 - 200 0 0 109Note that it stops at the "VerifyRequest" step, which is when IIS hands off the request to the NDES Policy Module.- The CRP log (in C:\Program Files\Microsoft Intune\NDESConnectorSvc\Logs\Logs\CertificateRegistrationPoint_<YYYY-MM-DD>.svclog) will contain an entry similar to the following:Cryptography Exception: System.Security.Cryptography.CryptographicException: Keyset does not exist- You will see an Event ID 36870 logged in the System Event Log on the NDES computer:Log Name: SystemSource: SchannelEvent ID: 36870Task Category: NoneLevel: ErrorUser: SYSTEMComputer: XXXXXXXXXXXXXDescription:A fatal error occurred when attempting to access the SSL client credential private key. The error code returned from the cryptographic module is 0x8009030D. The internal error state is 10003.- The NDES Connector Service logs (in C:\Program Files\Microsoft Intune\NDESConnectorSvc\Logs\Logs\NDESConnector_<YYYY-MM-DD_xxxxxxxx>.svclog) will contain entries similar to the following:Exception in download thread: System.ArgumentException: Location Service Error : https://manage.microsoft.com/RestUserAuthLocationService/RestUserAuthLocationService/Certificate/ServiceAddresses : CN=bb31a533-5a08-402e-aff3-718aab61f1b4 : System.Net.WebException: The request was aborted: Could not create SSL/TLS secure channel.and Exception in upload thread: System.ArgumentException: Location Service Error : https://manage.microsoft.com/RestUserAuthLocationService/RestUserAuthLocationService/Certificate/ServiceAddresses : CN=bb31a533-5a08-402e-aff3-718aab61f1b4 : System.Net.WebException: The request was aborted: Could not create SSL/TLS secure channel.
rootCause: This is caused by a bug in the NDES Connector. This issue was identified in LSI 64244569.
solution: To resolve this problem, uninstall the existing NDES Connector, then download, install and configure the latest version of the connector as you normally would per https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/certificates-scep-configure#download-install-and-configure-the-certificate-connector. This is a new version of the connector that�s been updated to make sure this issue will no longer happen, so when you reinstall the connector, proceeding all the way through the "Sign In" process, you should start seeing certificates hitting the devices again.

symptom: If you try to download any file from the Intune Azure portal while using the Microsoft Edge browser you notice the following You cannot save the file to folder. You cannot open the file. If you look in Downloads folder you see filename with partial extensionThis will happen with any file that you try to download using Edge.
rootCause: This because you are using the Edge Browser in "InPrivate" mode.
solution: To resolve the issue close out "InPrivate" browser and log in with non "InPrivate" Edge window.

symptom: When a user is assigned an app protection policy for Skype for Business and is using a device flagged as rooted, a wipe request can be targeted to that device which will complete successfully, however the user remains flagged with an error for having a rooted device in the UI under Flagged Users. This only occurs with the Skype for Business app. All other errors associated with other MAM protected apps for the same user/device are removed.
rootCause: Expected Behavior
solution: The product group states that this state occurs when clients attempt to sent an acknowledgement back to Intune stating the wipe was successfully for each app, however depending on the token availability, this acknowledgement will sometimes not make it back to the service. As a result, some flagged items can remain in the console, and this may not only occur with Skype for Business (SfB). Beginning with Intune 1803 (the March 2018 release), the Intune service will periodically remove these stale entries after not getting a heart beat every 90 days.

symptom: This article only apples if the Enrollment profile is set to "Select where users must authenticate = Setup Assistant (Legacy)". During ADE (Formerly DEP) enrollment, the user is unable to login with their credentials when prompted (During Setup Assistant authentication). Error message that appears is "Your credentials are either missing or wrong. Try Again"
rootCause: This has multiple causes: Username or password is actually wrong (typo) The user is targeted by a Conditional Access policy that blocks "All Cloud Apps". This includes an app called "Microsoft Intune Enrollment" For customers using ADFS or any other Identity federation (Okta as an example but there are more) and WS-Trust 1.3 is disabled or not configured. Also, validate the issue persist while inside the network, as if the issue only happens outside the network, there could be a problem with their Proxy servers or any network appliance in between. Customer's federation server denies the login request due to some claims processing rules that block Active login attempts from outside their network. ADFS certificate was installed in the wrong certificate store location, for instance, an Intermediate certificate is in the Root store or vice versa. (This could happen on new implementations or after renewing the ADFS certificate. (ADFS support Team should be engaged to validate or remediate this) MFA is enabled for the affected user via Azure MFA (or any third party Identity solution like OKTA or DUO), Conditional access policy, or Federation server policy. The Password used has some special characters like "�,�,�,�" (this is currently being investigated, and this article will be updated once more information is available)
solution: If using Azure AD MFA, the preferred solution would be D.2Solution A: Enter the correct information and try again Have user validate an azure AD sign in can be competed at https://myapps.microsoft.com Solution B: Edit the Conditional access policy to Exclude "Microsoft Intune Enrollment" In the "Exclude" tab of the conditional access policy, add "Microsoft Intune Enrollment" Solution C: Enable WS-Trust 13 If you're using ADFS and you're using Setup Assistant to authenticate, WS-Trust 1.3 Username/Mixed endpoint Learn more is required. Solution D.1. Disable MFA as this is not supported when the ADE profile is configured to "users must authenticate" with "Setup assistant". Solution D.2. If MFA cannot be disabled (whatever reason customer has) then the ADE profile needs to be updated to Select where users must authenticate to Setup Assistant with modern authentication, and the device needs to be reset completely again so it received the new ADE profile with the changes Important: Company portal authentication will be deprecated soon (there is no official date yet) but customer SHOULD move away from it ASAP, and that has been communicated since July 2021. Move to Setup Assistant with Modern Authentication for Automated Device Enrollment https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/intune-customer-success/move-to-setup-assistant-with-modern-authentication-for-automated/ba-p/2556536 Federation server claims rules need to be fully evaluated and vetted to confirm they are not the cause..... First validate the DEP enrollment completes without issue when using a Cloud Managed account (*.onmicrosoft.com) UPN, if this works but federated accounts fail, you have determined the cause to be the federation service for the customer. Ask them to review there federation service.... if needed, engage the Cloud Identity team for assistance.

symptom: Update: This content is no longer up to date. For the most up-to-date content, refer to the Intune CSS WIKI found here: How to Setup NDES ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- The intention of this article is to illustrate step by step how to configure an NDES server from the beginning. For more MICROSOFT documentation, please review the following link: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/protect/certificates-scep-configure ==============================STEPS================================ A. Configuring NDES Service account Permissions 1.- In the Domain Controller, Run > DSA.MSC (Active Directory Users and Computers) 2.- Create a Service account to handle the permissions (password never expire)(i.e. SVC-Intune-NDES)Service account has: Domain User privileges. 3.- Login into the NDES server 4.- Go into Computer Management > Local Users and groups > Groups and Select IIS_IUSRS group, and then add the Service account to this group. This account must have the following rights on the server that hosts NDES (this permission comes by default, but some organizations decide to restrict them): Logon Locally Also validate that the IIS_IUSRS is part of the "Impersonate a client after authentication" Local Policies (this also comes by default, but worth check on the customer).https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4078210 B. Configure Client Certificate Templates on the Certification Authority Connect to the Issuing Certificate Authority. 1. Open the Certificate Authority console (from Server Manager) or Run > mmc , and then click OK. 2. Click on File > add/Remove snap-in 3. On the Certification Authority page, select Local computer , click Finish, and then click OK. 4. Right-click on Certificate Templates, and then select Manage. 5. Scroll down, highlight, and right-click on User, and then select Duplicate Template. (Could be Computer template too) NOTE: You can use only one (1) template, as most people do; or create all 3 (if you do 3, it can be more complicated when its time for troubleshooting) In this guide we are going to do only one template (General Purpose). ***Note: To understand how to use more than one template, please review this link: https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4585907 6. On the Properties of New Template page click on the General tab and change the Template display name to NDES General Purpose NOTE: It is not recommended to have checked Publish certificate in Active Directory 7. Click on the Request Handling tab, make sure the Purpose is set to Signature and encryption (click Yes if you receive a prompt). 8. Click on the Subject Name tab, select Supply in the request option, and then click OK at the prompt. 9. Click on the Security tab and grant the following rights: Add the Service account (i.e. SVC-Intune-NDES), and then grant Read and Enroll rights. 10. Highlight Authenticated Users and make sure it has Read permissions. 11. Click on the Extensions tab and ensure that the Description of Application Policies includes Client Authentication. 12. Note : For iOS and Mac OS X certificate templates, on the Extensions tab, edit Key Usage and ensure Signature is proof of origin is not selected. 13. The Compatibility tab is not crucial in this template. 14. Click Apply, and then click OK. Review to ensure that the NDES General Purpose template is listed along with other templates. 15. Close the Certificate Templates Console (Manage). Do not close Certificate Authority console. C. Publish Certificate Template 1. Right click over Certificate template > New > Certificate Template to Issue. 2. Highlight the new template (NDES General Purpose) and then click OK. 3. Ensure that the new template is listed under Certificate Templates. Please review the article: https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4560484 to validate how to create the SSL certificate template and then issue a certificate for SCEP purposes. D. Configure Pre-Requisites for NDES Server *Pre-requisites for NDES (Network Device Enrollment Service) server: When NDES is added to the server, the wizard also installs IIS. Ensure IIS has the following configurations: Web Server > Security > Request Filtering Web Server > Application Development > ASP.NET 3.5. Installing ASP.NET 3.5 will install .NET Framework 3.5. When installing .NET Framework 3.5, install both the core .NET Framework 3.5 feature and HTTP Activation. Web Server > Application Development > ASP.NET 4.7. Installing ASP.NET 4.7 will install .NET Framework 4.7. When installing .NET Framework 4.7, install the core .NET Framework 4.7 feature, ASP.NET 4.7 , and the WCF Services > HTTP Activation feature. Web server (IIS) > Management Tools > IIS 6 Management Compatibility > IIS 6 Metabase Compatibility Web server (IIS) > Management Tools > IIS 6 Management Compatibility > IIS 6 WMI Compatibility You can also add the prerequisites using the following PowerShell script: **Note: This script installs all the prerequisites and the NDES Role. Add-WindowsFeature -Name @("Web-Server","Web-WebServer","Web-Common-Http","Web-Default-Doc","Web-Dir-Browsing","Web-Http-Errors","Web-Static-Content","Web-Http-Redirect","Web-Health","Web-Http-Logging","Web-Log-Libraries","Web-Request-Monitor","Web-Http-Tracing","Web-Performance","Web-Stat-Compression","Web-Security","Web-Filtering","Web-Windows-Auth","Web-App-Dev","Web-Net-Ext","Web-Net-Ext45","Web-Asp-Net","Web-Asp-Net45","Web-ISAPI-Ext","Web-ISAPI-Filter","Web-Mgmt-Tools","Web-Mgmt-Console","Web-Mgmt-Compat","Web-Metabase","Web-WMI","NET-Framework-Features","NET-Framework-Core","NET-HTTP-Activation","NET-Framework-45-Features","NET-Framework-45-Core","NET-Framework-45-ASPNET","NET-WCF-Services45","NET-WCF-HTTP-Activation45","NET-WCF-TCP-PortSharing45","WAS","WAS-Process-Model","WAS-NET-Environment","WAS-Config-APIs","ADCS-Device-Enrollment","RSAT-ADCS-Mgmt") --------------------Manual Process-------------------- If you do it manually, on the NDES server go to Server Manager > Add Roles and features. NOTE: To configure the role you must log on as an Enterprise Administrator. Install NDES Role * Server role: Active Directory Certificate Services and click on Add Features * AD CS > Role services : clear the Certification Authority check box, select the Network Device Enrollment Service check box On the Confirmation page, click Install. -------------------- o -------------------- 1. Depending on the version of the Windows Server you may or may not need to close the Add Roles and Feature Wizard Windows and reboot the server. On Windows server 2016 and above, please REBOOT the server before Continuing the rest of the configuration. Click on the hazard sign, on "Configure Active Directory Certificate Service" to start the NDES role configuration: 2. On the Credentials page, make sure those credentials have Enterprise admin permissions and then click Next. 3. On the Role Services page, select the Network Device Enrollment Service check box, and then click Next. 4. On the Service Account for NDES page, click the Select option and specify the service account: (example: SVC-Intune-NDES) 5. On the CA for NDES page, click Select, choose your CA, click OK and then click Next. (This will be the Server that issue the certificates) 6. On the RA Information page, you can leave the default values or add the information you require, then click Next. 7. On the Cryptography for NDES page, please accept the default values, and then click Next. 8. On the Confirmation page, click Configure and wait until the configuration is complete. Click Close > Close . DELEGATION for the NDES service account: 9. SetSPN reads, modifies, and deletes the Service Principal Names (SPN) directory property for an Active Directory service account; in this case the Service account we created and using to handle communication between your NDES server and your CA. Open CMD (As Admin) and run the following cmdlet in the NDES server to set the SPN of the NDES Service account: setspn -s http/<DNS name of NDES Server> <Domain name>\<NDES Service account name> i.e. setspn.exe -s http/FC-CM01.fourthcoffee.local fourthcoffee\SVC-Intune-NDES 10. Once the installation completes, edit the following registry keys on the NDES server to specify the certificate templates that the NDES server is going to use to issue SCEP certificates. Note: There should be no spaces on the name, when specifying the following values. HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MSCEP\ EncryptionTemplate: NDESGeneralPurpose GeneralPurposeTemplate: NDESGeneralPurpose SignatureTemplate: NDESGeneralPurpose *NOTE: As we are using only one template, place the same name on all three keys. (before) (After) *These values represent the template that the NDES server is going to use to issue a SCEP certificate, and this will have a correspondence in the Intune SCEP policy 11. After any changes in the Registry you need to perform an IISRESET. At the Command Prompt (Admin), run iisreset. 12. In the registry, open the registry editor. Browse to the following registry keys: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\HTTP\Parameters Confirm/ADD that MaxFieldLength and MaxRequestBytes are configured as 65534 values (DWORD -> decimal). If the values are different then change them to 65534 (decimal). 13. At the Command Prompt (Admin), run iisreset. Restart NDES server E. Testing NDES Functionality 1. On the NDES Server, open a browser and go to the following URL: http://FQDN/certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll *NOTE: This will be you Internal NDES URL The output should be similar to the following screenshot: NOTE: From outside the network this site is not discoverable. As we configure the maximum size query for the NDES URL request, we also need to set some values in IIS manager. 2. Go to Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager 3. Go to Sites > Default Web Site and double-click on Request Filtering. 4. On the Actions pane, click on the Edit Feature Settings option. 5. Confirm the following settings for the Edit Request Filtering Settings . Maximum allowed content length (Bytes): 30000000 Maximum URL length (Bytes): 65534 Maximum query string (Bytes): 65534 6. Now we need to configure the Secure connection over port 443 (HTTPS) In this part it is important to have generated already the SSL certificate. If you haven't done it; please review the article: https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4560484 on how to create an appropriate SSL certificate for NDES purposes. Go to Sites > Default Web Site and click on Bindings under the Actions pane to bind an SSL certificate to the NDES server: 7. Review to make sure HTTPS is specified. 8. Highlight HTTPS, click Edit , and make sure HTTPS is set to port 443 , as this is the only supported port for Microsoft Intune (standalone). Ensure that the SSL Certificate is specified. *The SSL certificate that is needed for binding needs to have the following properties: Subject name Common name: FQDN NDES Server Alternative name DNS: FQDN NDES Server and computer name if not using Azure AD app Proxy: add External server name. If using an internal CNAME for resolution add CNAME (along FQDN) 9. Now try connecting again with HTTPS. This time with: httpS://FQDN/certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll It should provide the similar NDES page; however, you will now have a padlock. 10. To verify the certificate that it is being used, click the padlock and view certificate. 11. Click on the Details tab, scroll down, and select Enhanced Key Usage . 12. Install and Configure Microsoft Intune NDES Connector Open the Intune console and go to Tenant Admin > Connectors and Tokens > Certificate Connectors > Add > and click on Download the certificate connector software. 14. Download IntuneCertificateConnector.exe to your NDES server. 15. On the NDES server, right-click on IntuneCertificateConnector.exe, and select Run as Administrator to start the setup process. 16. Click Configure Now. 17. Select the feature you want to install. You can have only SCEP or you can include the rest. Please make sure to ONLY select values you are using. You can come back to this page and modify/add values any time. ***Note: if you select Certificate revocation, please make sure the account that you are going to select next (System is the recommended) has "Issue and Manage Certificate" in the Certificate Authority. 18. Select the account. System is the recommended account. Remember that this only controls under what account runs the connector and the certificate revocation portion. The NDES role runs under Service account and this is the one that will interact with the CA and request certificates. 19. Click on the Sign in button and register using Global Admin (or Intune Admin) credentials WITH an Intune license. (Please validate this as many admins do not have Intune licenses assigned). *For GCCH, pick "Azure for Government" On a browser, type again the following: https://FQDN/certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll and it should give you HTTP ERROR 403.0 - Forbidden. This is expected!! and you are now ready to configure certificate profiles. Your NDES server is ready to work!!! Last step is to externally publish your NDES URL. For using the Azure AD Application Proxy, please reference the following article: https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/help/4560513 Once your environment is configured, you can proceed to create the policies in the Intune console. First Create a Trusted Certificate policy using the Root certificate exported from the CA. Recommendation: Create as many policies as certificates in your certificate chain. Note: Depending on the platform, you will only need the Root CA but for consistency is better to have all certs deployed to the devices. Here is an example of a SCEP USER policy: Hope this little guide helps. Cheers !!!

The various sync intervals in Intune for the different policy and platform types is documented at the following location:Manage settings and features on your devices with Microsoft Intune policiesHowever, there is a blog post that does a really good job making this information easy to digest. The information below is taken from that blog post which can be found here.

When a device restriction policy for Show or Hide Apps is configured to show certain apps and is applied to a group of users with supervised iOS devices, adding a web app (web link or webclip) to the list of visible apps does not allow that app to appear on the device.
rootCause: This is by design. The Hidden Apps list and the Visible Apps list only support apps with a bundle ID, meaning only apps packaged as IPA files are supported in these lists.
solution: There are two potential workarounds for this: 1. Use a bundle ID of com.apple.webapp. It has been reported by one customer that this worked, assuming Safari was also allowed since it is the default URL handler for iOS. This has not been officially tested so use at your own risk. App Store URL: Not configured App bundle ID: com.apple.webapp App name: <Exact Name of the Webclip/web link app as it is displayed in MEM Admcin center> Note: This will show all web clip / Web links apps installed on the device and there is no way to specify only a few of them. To work-around that, make sure to only deploy the necessary web clip apps to the targeted device. 2. Wrap the web app in an IPA using a 3rd party tool and distribute it that way. That would give us a bundle ID just like any other IPA, meaning we should be able to then add it to the list and use it with that policy. Note that wrapping the app in an IPA is not supported by Microsoft in any way. If the customer wishes to pursue this workaround then we cannot assist them with this. We can only help them add an app once the app has a valid bundle ID.

symptom: Customer configured a compliance policy with the "Minimum Required OS" set to beyond what is currently available. Under the actions for noncompliance, customer has created two actions: Send email to end user and Mark device noncompliant after 24 hours. When trying to enroll a device into Intune, the device reports out of compliance due to minimum OS version requirement and stays stuck in a loop.
rootCause: By design. The device will enroll and then check for compliance.
solution: Close out of the company portal and re-open it and the Intune portal will show the device as "In grace period" for the compliance state.

symptom: After creating and assigning an Approved Apps list, the Apps list shows a State of Failed in status of the policy for a user device.
rootCause: The user device is not compliant with policy, not because the policy did not apply.
solution: No resolution at this time. The Restricted Apps policy is just a reporting policy. To block certain apps, use Allow or Block Apps instead if KNOX devices are being used.

symptom: When attempting to upload an APK file in the Azure Intune portal, after selecting the file, the OK button does not light up and none of the package details are displayed. In Classic portal: Could not parse required information from Android manifest.
rootCause: This can occur if there is an issue with the manifest for the Android app in the APK file.
solution: Contact the publisher or developer of the app and have them update the app.

symptom: How to set an NDES server for Intune SCEP profiles. Configuring NDES Service account Permissions requires creating a Service account in DC, adding it to IIS_IUSRS group on the NDES server.

Enhanced jailbreak detection compliance setting causes iOS device to check-in with Intune more frequently using device location services. Device is non-compliant if Company Portal location services are disabled. End user is prompted to turn on location services.
Customer deleted the Lookout for Work MTD Connector and re-added it back and since then it shows as Unavailable in the Azure console.
solution: Recreate the connector on the Lookout side.

symptom: Customer has an App configuration policy to setup a bookmark in the Managed Browser but it is not applying the bookmark to the iOS device.
rootCause: Cisco ACLs was blocking communication.
solution: Customer was using a Managed Browser with a VPN profile and had applied Cisco ACLs to block the communication.

symptom: When attempting to enroll a MacOS device in Intune, the enrollment fails with error: Your Mac cannot be enrolled at this time. Company Portal logs show Keychain error -25244 (errSecInvalidOwnerEdit) with failed to delete workplace join items.
rootCause: This can occur if there are stale or corrupt Keychain entries related to Intune enrollment in the macOS keychain.
solution: Make sure the user is a local administrator. Open Keychain Access, search for Microsoft, delete specific keys (com.microsoft.CompanyPortal, enterpriseregistration.windows.net, etc.), delete related application passwords, certificates with MS-Organization-Access and SC_Online_Issuing entries. Uninstall Company Portal, reinstall and re-enroll.

symptom: If you have a device that is showing as Not Compliant in WebRave or Viewpoint, you can run the Kusto queries below to find the name of the policy that the device is not compliant with.See https://supportability.visualstudio.com/Intune/_wiki/wikis/Intune/185968/Kusto-Intune-Check-for-Intune-Device-Compliance
You are unable to query the Microsoft Graph API within https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/deviceManagement/ when using a non-GA account and receive the error �HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized�
rootCause: To access the Intune graph api from a non GA account requires the account to both be a member of a custom Intune account role and that graph api app access is allowed for the entire organization.
solution: a. Create the custom Intune roll Create a user group and add the service account you would like to have access to the graph API. Create a custom Intune roll with steps here: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/role-based-access-control#custom-roles Assign the group created in step one to the custom role. b. allow non GA users access to Graph. Launch Graph Explorer https://developer.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/graph-explorer Click �Sign in with Microsoft� Log in with your Global Admin account Click �Modify Permissions� Select all the Intune RW scopes (permissions) Click �access to your entire organization� Log in again with your Global Admin account Click Accept (you should see all those perms you granted) Choose �Close�

symptom: After configuring DEP enrollment in Intune and deploying new DEP devices to users. the devices never complete enrollment once powered on, and any user-based policies and profiles are not delivered to the device.
rootCause: This can occur if both of the following conditions exist:1. The user has not manually launched the Company Portal app on their device and logged in.2. The setting Authenticate with Company Portal instead of Apple Setup Assistant is configure to Yes in the enrollment profile, as shown below.This is by design.
solution: To resolve this problem, either configure Authenticate with Company Portal instead of Apple Setup Assistant to No in the enrollment profile, or instruct the end user to login to the Company Portal app with their Intune credentials.When Authenticate with Company Portal instead of Apple Setup Assistant is set to No in the enrollment profile, the user will be automatically prompted to login using their Intune credentials as part of the enrollment process.

symptom: Attempting to MDM enroll a Windows 10 computer fails with the following error:We couldn�t auto-discover a management endpoint matching the username entered. Please check your username and try again. If you know the URL to your management endpoint, please enter it.
rootCause: This can occur if the optional CNAME records were not created in DNS.
solution: To resolve this problem, do one of the following:- Create the appropriate CNAME records in DNS per https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/windows-enroll.or- Manually enter enrollment.manage.microsoft.com in the box for MDM Server URL.Once either of these are done, enrollment should now succeed.

symptom: When setting up the Android for Work binding, per the following article: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/android-enroll#enable-enrollment-of-android-for-work-devices On step 2..."Configure Android for Work binding" -> "Sign in to Google"They receive the error: "Enterprise is already enrolled with another EMM."
rootCause: This is by-design. This error happens when they sign in using a Google "G Suite" enterprise account.Per the above document, "The account you choose must not be associated with a G-Suite domain."
solution: The customer should create a "personal" Google account, that isn't associated with a domain managed by G Suite.When this is created, they can then use this account to bind to Android for Work.

symptom: Previously customers could not remove email profiles if there was not another deployment to replace it.This was corrected in the Intune 1811 release
rootCause: This is currently by-design.
solution: Currently, the Engineering team has suspended removal of email profiles due to some issues that were occurring. This should be re-enabled in the future, however, as of now it is not possible and is considered by-design.

symptom: After setting up the Intune NDES connector, NDESConnectorUI shows a status of Connected and the date, however it does not show as Connected in the Azure Intune Portal at the following path: Azure Intune portal -> Device Configuration -> Certification Authority. This might only affect a single server (if the customer has multiple NDES connectors) and there are no other obvious errors in any the logs.
rootCause: This can occur when the "Sign In" process cannot complete. For example:1. You click the "Sign In" button and an Internet Explorer process is opened. 2. You get taken to the Office 365 login screen. Make sure you login with a Global Admin user who also has an Intune license. 3. After logging in, the connector will request a client authentication certificate from the Intune service. This cert is used for communication between the connector and the Intune service. 4. The connector then installs this certificate to the local Personal certificate store, and uses this certificate for communication. In this scenario, the process fails after we enter the credentials. The page goes away without showing an error, and you are never given the message "Successfully enrolled" as shown below:
solution: This can occur if you have "Internet Explorer Enhanced Security Configuration" (commonly called IE ESC) turned on for the NDES server. This is a strict security setting that once enabled, severely restricts the permissions & capabilities that Internet Explorer can use. Since the NDES connector utilizes Internet Explorer, this setting can interfere with our process.To resolve this problem, disable IE ESC by following the steps below. These steps are the same on a Windows Server 2012 R2 or Windows Server 2016.1. Open Server Manager. This is commonly pinned to the Taskbar or on the Start Menu. 2. Navigate to "Local Server" on the left.3. Find the option on the right for IE ESC. Click on the "ON" or "OFF" button.4. Turn this setting to "Off" for Administrators. Note that it is believed that this can be re-enabled after enrollment, however this would need to be tested. Most customers turn this off and leave it off.5. Attempt signing into the NDES Connector UI (don't forget to run the EXE as an Admin!) and the problem should be resolved.

symptom: Intune: Devices or service connectors are unable to connect to the Intune service after certificate yearly renewal Many certificate expirations are scheduled to happen on April 21, 2018. We expect to get customer calls to enquire about the status of their devices certification renewals etc. Certificates that Intune issues to establish trust with MDM managed devices and connectors are renewed automatically upon connection to the Intune service. Sometimes, devices are in an unhealthy state or simply have not connected with the service due to battery issues, network issues and so on. When devices or service connectors are unable to connect to the Intune service, Intune cannot automatically push updated certificates to them. The steps to fix this can be found at: https://aka.ms/cert_renewal - Ensuring Certificate Renewal for Devices and Connectors in Intune IMP: Customers should upgrade to latest version (or at least 1802 or higher) of Company Portal Application on Android to ensure device certs are renewed successfully. IT Pro Am i expected to see less devices in console if some devices fail to renew cert? Devices may not be deleted soon after cert expires but as they become inactive, they will eventually be deleted. How can I find out the devices that need to renew cert? Use the script from TechNet gallery or TSQL. How can I find out devices that did not renew cert (post 21/04)? Use the script here to identify devices that did not renew certs. It will return all devices in a tenant which also includes inactive devices. Do I need to re-enrol devices that could not renew cert in time? Yes Would MS help me understand why all devices in my environment could not renew certs within 42 days? We can investigate a few sample devices but may not be able to investigate all devices that couldn�t renew the cert. Also, we can only investigate devices which checked in the last 29 days How can I check if a device has successfully renewed cert? Check expiry date of device certificate issued by SC_Online. If it is after 21/04/2018, certificate has been renewed successfully. Post 21/04 - Is there a way MS could temporary allow these devices to interact whilst we reach out to end users? Unfortunately, answer is no. Support Identify why a device could not renew cert Use Kusto query 1 Identify devices in a tenant that are due to expire Use Kusto query 2 & 4 What did MS do to inform customers? A blog post has been published here. What would happen on the device when cert expires or if it can�t renew the cert? Device would fail to sync and fetch new policies etc. When this happens, it would need to be re-enrolled. Are there any clients side logs that CXP/IET would need to diagnose why a device could not renew cert? If certificate wasn�t renewed in time i.e. before 21/04, device will need to be re-enrolled. How often a device would try to renew cert? Every 7 days Can I share this information with my current customers (open tickets)? Yes More information - https://aka.ms/CertRenewalBrownbag Kusto Query 1 DeviceLifecycle | where deviceId == "<DeviceId>" and EventId != 46828 | where TaskName == "EnrollmentRenewDeviceEvent" | project TaskName, env_time Kusto Query 2 To get a list of enrolled devices that have checked in this month and have certs expiring on 4/21 DeviceService_Device | where AccountId == "<Intune AccountID>" | where ScaleUnitName in ('AMSUA0101','AMSUA0201','AMSUA0401','AMSUA0501','AMSUA0502','AMSUA0601','AMSUA0602','AMSUA0701','AMSUB0101','AMSUB0102','AMSUB0201','AMSUB0301','AMSUB0501','AMSUC0101','AMSUC0201','AMSUC0301','AMSUC0501') | where IsManaged == true | where ClientRegistrationStatus == "2" and ManagementState == "0" | where ManagementAgents in (2,10,11,130) | where CertExpirationDate startswith "4/21/2018" | where LastContact startswith "4/" and LastContact !contains "2017" | project DeviceId, ClientRegistrationStatus, LastContact, CreatedDate, CertExpirationDate, SerialNumber Kusto Query 3 To get metadata including Cert expiration date for a single device DeviceService_Device | where DeviceId == "<DeviceId>" | project DeviceId, ClientRegistrationStatus, LastContact, CreatedDate, CertExpirationDate Kusto Query 4 To get metadata for all affected devices in a specified Intune tenant DeviceService_Device | where AccountId == "<AccountId>" | where ClientRegistrationStatus == "2" and ManagementState == "0" | where ScaleUnitName startswith "AMSU" | where CertExpirationDate startswith "4/21/2018" | project DeviceId, ClientRegistrationStatus, LastContact, CreatedDate, CertExpirationDate

After deploying a SCEP profile, devices do not receive a certificate and the NDESPlugin log will display the following: <![LOG[Failed to send http request /CertificateRegistrationSvc/Certificate/VerifyRequest. Error 12030]LOG]!>
rootCause: This can occur if the IP Address field in IIS is bound to an actual IP address instead of "All unassigned", like this:
solution: To resolve this problem, in IIS under Bindings, select HTTPS -> Edit and set IP address to "All unassigned":Once this is done, open a Command Prompt with admin privileges and run the following to reset IIS:iisreset

symptom: When trying to enroll a IOS device into Intune service you get the following error message Could not add device your device Contact your IT Admin with assistance with this issue This issue account not on boarded
rootCause: This caused by a problem with the Apple push notification certificate. If you look at the Apple Push notification in Viewpoint or Rave by running the Intune Platform Onboarding diagnostic. It may missing any of the following information in the list below. Thumbprint Cert Issue Time Cert Expire Time Account ID In my customer case It was missing all of the information listed above.
solution: To resolve the issue fix the issue with the Apple Push notification. In my customer case we removed the existing Apple Push notification and added a new one back in. If the customer had existing certificate and large number of users its is better to have the customer renew Apple APN certificate. For information about how to renew Apple APN certificate see the following article listed below. Intune: Apple Push Cert APN has to renewed before expiration https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-us/help/2878491/intune-apple-push-cert-apn-has-to-be-renewed-before-expirationNot removing adding the Apple APN from Intune service you will have unenroll any devices that were enrolled with old certificate. They will have reenroll with the new certificate.

(Y/N) Microsoft Service Level Severity Definitions: Severity 1 Catastrophic or critical situation Definition: Situations that critically affect the customer's business, such as complete loss of a core business process. Work cannot reasonably continue, and the situation requires immediate attention. Microsoft actions: Microsoft resources are required at the customer's site as soon as possible (except for Essential Support Standard). The issue is escalated to the product teams. Senior executives at Microsoft are notified, and the first call response is within one hour or less. Customer actions: Notification of the customer's senior executives is required. The customer must allocate appropriate resources to sustain continuous effort 24 hours a day, every day, until the issue is resolved. Only Technical Account Managers, Technical Account Specialists, and Critical Situation Managers can code cases as Severity 1. Agents code as Severity A. Time to Respond: 1 hour Premier contract only; 2 hours Broad Commercial Severity A Critical Definition: Situations that involve a system, network, server, or critical program down situation that severely affects customer production or profitability. High-impact issues where production, operations, or development are proceeding but could be severely affected within several days. Microsoft actions: First call response is in one hour or less for Premier and Essential Support Standard SRs and one hour for Professional SRs. Microsoft will provide continuous effort to resolve the problem 24 hours a day. Senior managers at Microsoft are notified. Customer actions: The customer must allocate appropriate resources to sustain a continuous effort 24 hours a day. The customer's management must be notified. Time to Respond: 1 hour Premier contract only; 2 hours Broad Commercial Severity B Urgent Definition: Signifies a moderate business impact. The customer's business has a moderate loss or degradation of services, but work can reasonably continue. The customer is not experiencing an immediate work stoppage. Microsoft actions: First call response will be two hours (business hours or actual hours, customer's choice) or less for Premier and Essential Support Standard. For Professional SRs, the callback time is four business hours. Customer actions: The customer must allocate appropriate resources during business hours (or 24x7 for Premier customers only if the customer opts for 24x7 troubleshooting) to sustain a continuous effort to resolve the issue. Time to Respond: 2 Hours Premier contracts only; 4 hours Broad Commercial Severity C Important Definition: Indicates an issue with minimum business impact. The customer's business is substantially functioning and has minor or no impediment of services. Microsoft actions: First call response will be four business hours or less for Premier and Essential Support Standard. For Professional and Personal SRs, the callback response time is 24 business hours. Customer actions: The customer must provide accurate information to their agents working on the issue with Microsoft and must respond within 24 hours. Time to Respond: 4 Hours Premier contract only; 8 hours Broad Commercial BlackBerry Service Level Severity Definitions: BlackBerry Technical Support Service eligible customers are able to request a support ticket by visiting my.blackberry.com. The response time in providing an answer depends on the complexity of the situation. The hereunder table mentions the level of priority, the description as well as the guaranteed response time for an incident signaled to the BlackBerry technical support team. This problem is critical to your organizations ability to conduct business, and may affect either the BlackBerry Software or a majority of the deployed smartphones. Note: BlackBerry support teams are paged twenty-four (24) hours a day, seven (7) days a week for critical problems; Customers must agree to be available for engagement 24x7 until relief has been provided. Customer would like create a SQL Link Server to Intune to be able to run SQL query statements to pull device information from Intune (i.e. device ID, phone number, and subscriber). Customer would like to know if this is possible and if so, how to accomplish this.The supported way is to use PowerBI to pull the information and import it into SQL. To configure go to the Intune blade -> Overview -> Other tasks -> Set up Intune Data Warehouse

When using Outlook iOS where devices are managed via MDM users are reporting they are unable to sync contacts and\or able to edit contacts. This isn�t an Intune specific issue � all MDMs are impacted by Apple�s change. This isn�t limited to Outlook mobile; EAS profiles are also impacted by Apple�s change (in that contacts synced via EAS to native contacts app won�t be accessible by unmanaged apps, like WhatsApp).
rootCause: Both the Intune and Outlook Product Teams are still investigating with Apple, but below is brief description of the cause.With iOS 11.3, Apple changed the way MDM works with the native contacts app. According to this third-party article and this Apple support thread, in iOS 11.3, Apple made MDM improvements and changed the way the native contacts app works by preventing contacts in managed accounts from being used in unmanaged apps/accounts (managed in this case isn�t the same term that Intune uses to describe Intune SDK enlightened apps). When Intune enforces this device restriction settings on enrolled devices its telling iOS to prevent �non-managed� apps (i.e., apps not managed through the OS MDM channel, I suspect) from being able to access the managed data. For example, WhatsApp can�t access contacts synced down from an EAS profile pushed by an MDM.With the upgrade to iOS 11.3 you are now seeing odd behavior with Outlook for iOS in that you can�t create and edit contacts in the Outlook mobile app. If you disable that device restriction setting or unenroll the device, contacts can be managed appropriately in Outlook. Within Outlook we are surfacing and calling the native iOS contact management controls to create/edit contacts inside of Outlook. It could be that the combination of using the native contact controls, coupled with new OS MDM restrictions in place in iOS 11.3 (which is controlled by the above device restriction setting), that is preventing the contact form from being saved and applying the changes in Outlook. We�re beginning to explore this now internally.
solution: As a possible workaround, have you considered disabling the �viewing corporate documents in unmanaged apps� and replacing it with the applicable Intune App Protection policy settings?This would ensure only corporate identities in the approved apps (either apps that support Intune App Protection policies or those you exempt) could access and manipulate corporate data. Refer customer to https://blogs.technet.microsoft.com/intunesupport/2018/04/17/support-tip-ios-11-3-and-native-contacts-app/ for more information and steps on the workaround.

symptom: When a user attempts to setup a DEP device and enroll in Intune, if the device is running iOS 11.3 the enrollment fails with the following error:Remote ManagementThe Configuration of your iPhone could not be downloaded from <companyName>Invalid ProfileThis appears when the user is prompted to install the management profile.Devices running iOS 11.2 or earlier seem to enroll successfully however it has not been confirmed that this issue is necessarily specific to iOS 11.3.
rootCause: This can occur if the device has not been assigned an enrollment profile. This could either be because the default DEP profile has failed to be applied, or if there is no default DEP profile, the device has not been manually assigned an enrollment profile.
solution: To resolve this issue, manually assign the device an enrollment profile. If this resolves the issue and a default DEP profile was being used, further troubleshooting will be required to determine why the default profile was not being assigned to devices.

symptom: Customer has configured an Intune app protection policy to "Allow app to receive data from other apps" set to "Policy managed apps". When opening a meeting via the Outlook mobile app and tapping a valid address in the Location field it has no result (i.e. the location is not mapped). Customer configured an exemption for the native iOS Maps but the result is the same; the location is not mapped. Customer would like to know if the native iOS Maps can be exempted from the app protection policy?No. The native iOS Maps app is not listed as one of the exempt apps as documented here: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/app-protection-policy-settings-ios#data-transfer-exemptions

rootCause: Customer made changes to firewall/proxy settings that prevented the enrollment from completing
solution: Customer disabled the proxy and the device was able to enroll.

symptom: Applications deployed to Windows 10 computers that are MDM enrolled as mobile devices get stuck in an Install Pending state and are never installed. You will also see events similar to the following logged on targeted computers: Log name: Microsoft-Windows-DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider/AdminSource: DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-ProviderEvent ID: 400Level: ErrorUser: SystemDetails:MDM ConfigurationManager: Command failure status. Configuration Source ID: (A3E36CB6-A73F-4313-B440-5F8DB2EB701D), Enrollment Name: (MDMFull), Provider Name: (EnterpriseModernAppManagement), Command Type: (CmdType_Execute), CSP URI: (./User/Vendor/MSFT/EnterpriseModernAppManagement/AppInstallation/Microsoft.CompanyPortal_8wekyb3d8bbwe/StoreInstall), Result: (Bad request (400).).-----Log name: Microsoft-Windows-DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider/AdminSource: DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-ProviderEvent ID: 450Level: ErrorUser: SystemDetails:MDM ConfigurationManager: Command failure status. Configuration Source ID: (A3E36CB6-A73F-4313-B440-5F8DB2EB701D), Enrollment Name: (MDMFull), Provider Name: (EnterpriseModernAppManagement), Command Type: (CmdType_Execute), CSP URI: (./User/Vendor/MSFT/EnterpriseModernAppManagement/AppInstallation/Microsoft.CompanyPortal_8wekyb3d8bbwe/StoreInstall), Result: (0x86000022).
rootCause: This can occur if there is a problem with the device registration records in Azure Active Directory. In these cases you may see the device listed multiple times in AAD.
solution: To resolve this problem, unenroll the device from Intune, then remove all records for the device from Azure AD. Once all device records are removed from AzureAD, re-enroll the computer in Intune and confirm that the enrollment and registration in AAD is successful. The app should install correctly now and the events above should no longer appear in the Event Log.

symptom: When attempting to deploy PKCS certificates, the certificates are not deployed and the following error is logged:IssuePfx - COMException: System.Runtime.InteropServices.COMException (0x80094015): An enrollment policy server cannot be located. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x80094015)
rootCause: This can occur if the NDES computer is unable to locate a certificate enrollment policy server.
solution: To resolve this issue, configure the name of the policy server manually on the NDES computer.

symptom: Attempting to enroll a Windows 10 PC fails with the following error:Something went wrong.  This user is not authorized to enroll. You can try to do this again or contact your system administrator with error code 80180003"
rootCause: Unknown
solution: To resolve this problem, complete the following:1. Login to the Intune Azure Portal with a Global Administrator account.2. Navigate to Device Enrollment -> Enrollment Restrictions and select the Default Device Type Restriction.3. Select Platforms, then for the Windows platform make sure Windows (MDM) is set to Allow.  IMPORTANT: If the current setting is Allow, change it to Block, save the setting, then change it back to Allow and save the setting again. This will reset the enrollment setting.Once done, wait for approximately 15 minutes then try to enroll the affected device again.

symptom: When attempting to enroll an Apple DEP device, the device is powered up, and when the DEP splash screen appears you get the following error:Installing configuration from <companyName>Profile installation failed. The SCEP server returned an invalid response.
rootCause: This is often caused by an issue with the device itself. If the customer experiences this error with only one device, or a limited subset of DEP devices, this is likely the case.
solution: You should exhaust all other troubleshooting first, but as a last resort, do a factory reset on the device and try enrolling again. In most cases this will resolve the problem.Check if non-DEP iOS enrollment works on the same WiFi network.Try connecting from a different WiFi networkTry connecting from a cellular network instead of WiFi.

symptom: Previously Intune could not deliver support for MFA email-profiles. This is now possible with iOS 12 by selecting Oauth in the email profileOffice 365 MDM email profiles still do not support MFA
