As per the following TechNet Article https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sccm/mdm/deploy-use/ios-device-enrollment-program-for-hybrid ADFS WS-trust 1.3 username/Mixed endpoint is required to be enabled. Customer can have ADFS as federation service or it could be third party as well but it should have WS-trust 1.3 username/Mixed endpoint enabledFrom Intune perspective we might need to Involve Identity team for advance troubleshooting on ADFS side but we can check at very basic level if the requirements are met.In the process of validating the requirement we need to first gather the endpoint URI. you can gather URI for a tenant from ViewpointFrom Viewpoint - under the Domain Federation Information we can copy the URI. This URI should be reachable over internet In case of Third Party Federation service, it would be something like this ( taking example of NetIQ ) From above information, pick the Exchange URI and check if you can reach it from internet. Once you can access the URI you should have XML ( in case of third party, you see list of endpoint and you need further click on the link for WsTrust )Once you have the XML, search for "UsernameWSTrustBinding" and you should see "usernamemixed" for the endpointWorking configuration should look like this============================================================================================================================================================================================

1.3:

 

<wsdl:port name="UserNameWSTrustBinding_IWSTrust13Async" binding="tns:UserNameWSTrustBinding_IWSTrust13Async"><soap12:address
location="https://sts.ABC.net/adfs/services/trust/13/usernamemixed"/><wsa10:EndpointReference><wsa10:Address>https://sts.ABC.net/adfs/services/trust/13/usernamemixed</wsa10:Address></wsa10:EndpointReference>

 

2005:

<wsdl:port name="UserNameWSTrustBinding_IWSTrustFeb2005Async" binding="tns:UserNameWSTrustBinding_IWSTrustFeb2005Async"><soap12:address
location="https://sts.ABC.net/adfs/services/trust/2005/usernamemixed"/><wsa10:EndpointReference><wsa10:Address>https://sts.ABC.net/adfs/services/trust/2005/usernamemixed</wsa10:Address></wsa10:EndpointReference>

==============================================================================================================================================================================Above is good enough to confirm the requirement is in place. However further you can check the ADAL communication as well using the attach script in more info section

When configuring the Cisco AnyConnect VPN client on Android phones, the following message is generated:External Control of AnyConnect is currently disabled.  Tap the Change Setting button to go to the Settings screen to enable this feature.  Enabling External Control reduces the overall security of AnyConnect.
rootCause: This is by design.
solution: This is expected behavior when the Cisco AnyConnect client is used on Android devices in conjunction with Microsoft Intune. This does not occur on iOS devices.Note that this is new behavior. Cisco updated their AnyConnect client to add this prompt sometime around January 2018. Prior versions would not generate this prompt.

symptom: When trying to setup the Intune on premise Exchange Connector you receive the following error messages below User Name  Not Recognized An error occurred while processing your request. The Microsoft Intune Exchange Connector cannot connect to Microsoft Intune Verify that you are connected to the Internet, check the Microsoft Intune Service Status and try to connect again. Microsoft Intune Service Status Error code: 0x0000006
rootCause: This error can be cause by user account that is being used to sign into Intune service has one or all of the following problems User account does not have Intune license assigned to it. User is not tenant  administrator or company administrator.
solution: To resolve the issue make sure the user account has the following: Intune License assigned to it. User is  tenant  administrator or company administrator role assigned.

symptom: This article describes the technical support boundaries for Windows Information Protection (WIP).Windows Information Protection is a feature integrated into the Windows 10 platform starting with Windows 10 version 1607 and Windows 10 Mobile version 1607. WIP is used to protect and control data access in an enterprise environment, and is configured via policies that are delivered by Mobile Device Management (MDM) providers. Microsoft Intune is an example of one such MDM provider, others include, AirWatch, MobileIron, MaaS360, et al.Please be aware that WIP is not a feature of the Intune product. It is a feature of the Windows product.

There was an issue in Intune regarding how files are shared in OneDrive. The reason for this is that OneDrive has changed how files are made available to FileAppFile sharing with Native Mail AppUsers cant share attachments from the native Mail app to OneDrive via FileApp in Mobile Device Management (MDM) managed devices when OneDrive is managed by Intune App Protection (also known as Mobile Application Management- MAM). Access to MAM managed accounts is currently blocked by OneDrive to avoid data leaks through FileApp. Were working on a solution for this, but it is dependent in part on an Apple bug fix for the Files app not supporting the managed Open-In policy.File access for MAM managed appsMAM-managed Office apps like Teams, Yammer that do not have OneDrive directly integrated cannot access files from MAM-managed OneDrive.
Android Devices get Missing Certificate when they try to enroll
rootCause: This usually happens when they use ADFS and the intermediate certificate is not install on the ADFS server and the ADFS proxy\wap

symptom: After configuring the Actions for noncompliance for device compliance in Intune, when a device is enrolled and it is marked as Not Compliant, the notification email is not sent.
rootCause: This can occur if the minimum OS version of the device compliance policy is set to an invalid value. For example, for the iOS platform where currently the latest version is 11.3, if you configure the minimum OS version to 12.2 then the email will never fire. This is by design.
solution: To resolve this problem, make sure that Minimum OS version and Maximum OS version (if used) are set to valid values.

symptom: After creating and assigning a Device Configuration profile that defines a custom VPN connection using OMA-URI settings, Windows 10 clients receive the profile and can connect to the VPN endpoint successfully, however the profile will report an error of -2016281112 (Remediation Failed) in the Intune admin portal.
rootCause: This occurs because in certain scenarios, the response sent by the Windows 10 client will include XML formatted differently than the XML sent via the Intune policy. If the policy is applied successfully, the XML in the response should be an exact match to the XML in the policy sent, which is how Intune confirms that the policy has been applied correctly. Because the XML does not match, it is interpreted as a remediation failure.This is a known problem in all current versions of Windows 10 up to and including Windows 10 Redstone 5 (RS5) and is scheduled to be addressed in a future Windows 10 release.
solution: You can ignore this error as the connection does work as expected. Alternatively, you can use the standard Intune VPN wizard to create their VPN device configuration profile instead of a custom profile with OMA-URI settings. If the standard wizard is used, the error above will not be generated.

symptom: When using an AD mobile account on a Mac the Company portal app fails to launch through the Self-Service portal as it is designed. If you use a Local account on the same Mac it works well and does register, but will not with a AD account using a mobile profile. It appears that the Company Portal Registration process does not work when logged into the machine with an AD mobile account.
rootCause: By design. The Company portal app does not support AD mobile accounts.
solution: Users must enroll into Jamf using a Local account on the Mac.

symptom: After installing or updating the NDES connector, you may notice that many files get stuck in the C:\Program Files\Microsoft Intune\CertificateRevocationStatus\Processing folder. Note that NDES continues to work fine and client devices receive certificates as expected, the only symptom is the backlog of files.
rootCause: This can occur if the Intune Connector Service is either stopped or no longer functioning correctly.orThe NDES computer account does not have Issue and Manage Certificates rights for each certificate template used by a certificate profile.
solution: To resolve this problem restart the Intune Connector Service and make sure it continues to run. The files should begin to process out. If this does not fix the problem, collect the Ndesconnector.svclog log file and look for errors.If that does not fix the problem, the NDES service account may not have Issue and Manage Certificates rights on the Certification Authority. To configure these permissions, complete the following:1. On the Certification Authority, go to the Start menu and choose All Programs -> Administrative Tools, then choose Certification Authority.2. In Certification Authority, right-click <CA server name>, then choose Properties.3. In the <CA server name> Properties dialog box, on the Security tab, add the computer account of the NDES computer, then in Permissions, for the Allow column, select the Issue and Manage Certificates box.

symptom: When you setup a new DEP Profile, using the New Interface, there is an option when you select "User Affinity" that lets you authenticate using the Company Portal. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/device-enrollment-program-enroll-ios-newuiWhen the setup the device, they are not prompted for a username & password (as expected) - however, they are prompted for an Apple ID to install the Company Portal. This is despite the customer previously deploying the Company Portal, to DEP devices, using VPP + Device Licensing (which has no prompt, and is a silent installation).
rootCause: This is currently expected behavior, and considered by-design.
solution: This is currently by-design.When you enroll, using the above method, we do not use VPP - we use a Deep Link, to the store, using a standard Configuration Profile.The Engineer team is aware of this, and is looking into developing a way to enable customers, who utilize VPP, to enable silent installation of the Company Portal application. There is no timeline as of now, 5/2/2018.

symptom: Many customers ask if/how they can block specific custom URLs using Endpoint Protection on Windows 10. The current status is below.

After enrolling a device in Intune, you may notice that no policies show up on the device, and when looking at the status of a Device Configuration profile under Microsoft Intune -> Devices -> All Devices -> affectedDevice -> policyName, some policy settings will show a State of Conflict.
rootCause: This can occur if rules in the Device Configuration policies are conflicting.
solution:
To determine which rules are conflicting, you can run the following Kusto query:IntuneEvent
| where env_time between (datetime(4/15/2021 12:09:54 AM)..datetime(4/16/2021 12:09:54 AM))
| where ApplicationName startswith 'DeviceCheckin_'
| where DeviceId == 'Intune Device Id'
| where Col5 != 'NotApplicable'
| where Col1 contains "conflict"
| project env_time, Col1, Col3, Col4, ScenarioInstanceIdJust be sure to substitute the actual Intune Device ID (can be found in Rave or MDMDiagnostic or ODC logs) of an affected device in the query above, and change the dates and time as appropriate.The output will look similar to the following:Col1 -> shows the number of conflicting rules, the setting name that is entering in conflict.Col3 -> The policy Id.Note: Replace underscore "_" characters in the policy Id with "-" dashes to look up the policy body in Rave.

symptom: This article describes the current processes to create Intune content:  Getting permissions to access ContentIdea How to create a new internal KB article How to update an existing KB article How to edit the Intune Supportability Wiki How to update Intune public docs Additional resources for using Github  Getting Permissions to Access ContentIdea 1. Go to http://contentidea (VPN required) or https://contentidea.microsoft.com/.  2. Click on the blue box labeled Request a new troubleshooting article:    3. Close all browser windows. The next time you use the links below you will have access.  IMPORTANT! If you still do not have access after completing the steps above, open a Service-Now ticket and they can configure your access manually. The steps are below. a. Got to Service-Now. b. Click on Create Support Ticket. c. Fill out the form and click Submit. An example is below.  How to create a new internal KB article   Tips:  Screen shots can be extremely helpful and are highly encouraged, however those screen shots need to be clear and meet standard guidelines for them to provide value. Please review and following the guidelines HERE any time you add screen shots to an article. Be sure to read the KB here before proceeding with the rest of this article.   1. Go to https://aka.ms/IntuneInternalKB. 2. In the Requested By field above, set the value to your name. 3. Give your article a title in the format of Intune: Symptoms of problem seen. For example: Intune: Error ABC123 when installing the transducer modulator app on iOS 4. Click the button for Primary Products and search for Intune. Add Azure -> Microsoft Intune and click OK. 5. Click the Content Idea Auto Publish tab, then for Article Type select either Break/Fix or Informational. The difference is that if you choose Break/Fix, the Symptoms, Cause, Resolution and More Information fields are all available. If you choose Informational then only the fields Summary and More Information are available.  6. Fill in the fields for Symptom, Cause, Resolution and More Information as appropriate.   7. Specify a peer reviewer for your article. Note that only individuals who have completed the security training can peer review your content.  8. On the Internal Technical Content tab, slide the Customer Data Compliance toggle to True, like this:  9. Click the Save button at the top. After a few minutes, you and your peer reviewer should get an email confirmation that your article was submitted. Once your peer reviewer approves your article, it should be published internally and will be indexed and discoverable in DfM Knowledge the same day. IMPORTANT! If your KB article does not get published at this point, please reach out to ask-intunecsswiki@microsoft.com for help.   How to update an existing KB article 1. From within the article, click in the Content Idea ID link in the header:  This will take you to the article in ContentIdea where you can make any needed edits.  2. Make the changes you want. 3. Specify a peer reviewer for the article. 4. On the Internal Technical Content tab, slide the Customer Data Compliance toggle to True, like this: 5. Click the Save button at the top.  After a few minutes, you and your peer reviewer should get an email confirmation that your edit was submitted. Once your peer reviewer approves the article, your updates will be viewable and searchable within DfM Knowledge in about 5-10 minutes. IMPORTANT! If your KB article does not get updated at this point, please reach out to ask-intunecsswiki@microsoft.com for help.  How to edit the Intune Supportability Wiki Complete steps to edit the Intune Supportability Wiki can be found here.    How to update our public docs (learn.microsoft.com) Recommended You can propose changes to docs articles without having to open a Content Idea request. Simply CLICK HERE to view a short guide on how to create and login with a GitHub account and create your own Pull Requests. Be sure to follow all steps precisely. Most Pull Requests (PRs) will be processed by a member of the docs team in about a week. Optional If you have a complex request that you're not comfortable making, contact ask-intunecsswiki@microsoft.com for further assistance.      Additional resources for using Github GitHub training Edit an article using the GitHub user interface Office authoring guide - Quickly update an article - Make special note of any sections marked IMPORTANT. FTEs should be making updates in the private repository, not public.  Learn Markdown reference PubOps publishing process

For AutoPilot to work you need to upload a CSV file to your Intune tenant with a following info 
    serial numbers, Windows product IDs, and hardware hashes of the devices you want to add.. There has been a PowerShell command that has been published to pull this info. 
  
  
     
  
  
    You can use the 
    
      Get-WindowsAutopilotInfo PowerShell script
    
    , which will generate a .csv file with the serial numbers, Windows product IDs, and hardware hashes of the devices you want to add.
  
  
     
  
  
     
  
  
    1.
    This first step may not be necessary but I had to install PowerShellGet 
  
  
    
      https://www.powershellgallery.com/packages/PowerShellGet/1.6.0
    
     
  
  
    For the path add a location on your local drive preferably within a folder, change the Path if you want it installed elsewhere.
  
  
    PS> Save-Module -Name PowerShellGet -Path C:\PS\
  
  
    PS> Install-Module -Name PowerShellGet -Force
  
  
    2.
    Run the following command to install the Get-WindowsAutoPilotInfo 
  
  
    
      https://www.powershellgallery.com/packages/Get-WindowsAutopilotInfo/1.3/DisplayScript
    
     
  
  
    For the path add a location on your local drive preferably within a folder, change the Path if you want it installed elsewhere, you will be using this path in the rest of the switches. I used C:\PS\
  
  
    PS> Save-Script -Name Get-WindowsAutoPilotInfo -Path C:\PS\
  
  
    PS> Install-Script -Name Get-WindowsAutoPilotInfo
  
  
     
  
  
    
      
    
  
  
     
  
  
    3.
    If you go to the location that you installed Get-WindowsAutoPilotInfo you can open the file and you will see the script along with the commands\switches that are used to run the script. I have added the commands below.
  
  
    .\Get-WindowsAutoPilotInfo.ps1 -ComputerName MYCOMPUTER -OutputFile .\MyComputer.csv
  
  
    .EXAMPLE
  
  
    .\Get-WindowsAutoPilotInfo.ps1 -ComputerName MYCOMPUTER -OutputFile .\MyComputer.csv -Append
  
  
    .EXAMPLE
  
  
    .\Get-WindowsAutoPilotInfo.ps1 -ComputerName MYCOMPUTER1,MYCOMPUTER2 -OutputFile .\MyComputers.csv
  
  
    .EXAMPLE
  
  
    Get-ADComputer -Filter * | .\GetWindowsAutoPilotInfo.ps1 -OutputFile .\MyComputers.csv
  
  
    EXAMPLE
  
  
    Get-CMCollectionMember -CollectionName "All Systems" | .\GetWindowsAutoPilotInfo.ps1 -OutputFile .\MyComputers.csv
  
  
    EXAMPLE
  
  
    .\Get-WindowsAutoPilotInfo.ps1 -ComputerName MYCOMPUTER1,MYCOMPUTER2 -OutputFile .\MyComputers.csv -Partner
  
  
     
  
  
    4.
    You may need to enable running scripts on the PC if they are disabled. You can find out by trying to run one of the commands above to see if it will run. If it does not you will see the following message.
  
  
    
      
    
  
  
    If you need to enable scripts you can run the following command.
  
  
    Set-executionpolicy unrestricted -scope currentuser -Force
  
  
    
      https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/microsoft.powershell.security/set-executionpolicy?view=powershell-6
    
     
  
  
  
    5.
    The command that I found to produce the info I was looking for was 
  
  
    .\Get-WindowsAutoPilotInfo.ps1 -ComputerName MYCOMPUTER1,MYCOMPUTER2 -OutputFile .\MyComputers.csv -Partner
  
  
     
  
  
    You cannot run it as it is because it will fail. I changed it to look like this. Keep in mind that I am running this from my C:\PS\ location yours will depend on the location that installed the Get-WindowsAutoPilotInfo.ps1 to in step 2.
  
  
     
  
  
    C:\PS\Get-WindowsAutoPilotInfo.ps1 -ComputerName desktop-xxxxxx -OutputFile C:\ps\MyComputers.csv 
  
  
     
  
  
    Running the above command produces the CSV file with the serial numbers, Windows product IDs, and hardware hash of the device into the location I specified C:\ps\MyComputers.csv the file will be named MyComputers.csv. This is the file that you will want to select to upload to Intune.

If a machine is joined to AD and enrolled in MDM some of the AD Group Policies may not apply to the machine.  This is due to a new CSP that is available in Windows 10 1803 via MDM.  The ControlPolicyConflict CSP can be pushed to the device that forces MDM Policy to win over Group Policy when there is a conflict.  That is called MDMWinsOverGP.  The default for this is Group Policy wins over MDM Policy.  If MDMWinsOverGP is set to 1 then MDM Policy will win.  

Once the MDMWinsOverGP flag is set, all MDM policies defined in Policy
CSP will win over Group Policy (GP) if corresponding GP is configured in the device.
 For GP that doesn�t have corresponding MDM policy pushed down, GP value
still apply.  This policy doesn�t support Delete command. This policy doesn�t support setting the value to be 0 again after it was previously set 1.

When you check on mobile device in the Intune console and under Exchange Access the Last EAS sync time is blank or the date and time stamp is extremely out of date.
rootCause: This can be caused by HasActiveSyncDevicePartnership is set to false on user that has Exchange ActiveSync device partnerships setup.   You can view this setting by running either of the following Exchange PowerShell commands. Get-Recipient user@smtpaddress.com | FL *Has* This issue can happen both Exchange on-premise and Exchange online environments. Get-CasMailbox user@smtpaddress | FL *Has*
solution: To resolve this issue run the following Exchange PowerShell command Get-CASMailbox user@smtpaddress.com -RecalculateHasActiveSyncDevice

symptom: An unexpected error has occurred when logging into Intune Classic Silverlight portalAn unexpected error has occurred when trying to renew APN for SCCM HybridNote: This article also applies to SCCM Hybrid as it still uses the Classic portal to request APN for iOS EnrollmentAn error occurred while processing your requestURL contains E=10 as per below screenshot
rootCause: Intune license is not assignedorLogin URL is not trusted
solution: Fist confirm if the user logging in is a Global Admin or Intune Service AdminMake the user a Global administrator (Company administrator) and is Intune licensed. If the customer doesn't want GA rights then assign the user the Service administrator role from within the classic portal.   login to the classic Intune portal with another UPN that is already a global admin go to Admin -> Administrator Management -> Service Administrators add the affected user's UPN as a Service AdministratorEnsure that the UPN is Intune licensed and  that MDM authority is not Office365Add the following URL to trusted sites - https://sts.manage.microsoft.comIf that resolves the issue the case can be closed as resolved.

symptom: Using Intune App Protection policies, you can configure settings to block end users from accessing a corporate app or account. These settings target data relocation and access requirements set by your organization for things like jail-broken devices and minimum OS versions. Starting with Intune 1806, you can explicitly choose to wipe your companys corporate data from the end users device as the action to take on non-compliance for these settings.

Managed apps on iOS device prompts users to configure a device PIN when only App Protection Policies are targeted. When the Encrypt App data policy is configured, even without the Require PIN for Access setting users may be required to setup and use a PIN with the message your organization has required you to first enable a device passcode to access this app.
rootCause: When the Encrypt App data policy is configured with any value other than Use device settings, even without the Require PIN for Access setting, users may be required to setup a PIN. If there is no device PIN and encryption is required, the apps will not open.
solution: Configure the Encrypt App data policy to Use device settings if the IT Pro does not want iOS users to be required to set a device PIN.

symptom: When configuring iOS Device Restrictions such as single app mode or Home screen layout under Intune, you need to enter the App Bundle ID for iOS Store Apps. There is no way to directly look up Bundle IDs in the Apple iOS App Store.
rootCause: Every iOS app requires a Bundle ID (e.g. com.microsoft.CompanyPortal). There is no direct lookup in the App Store.
solution: Find the app in iTunes store, copy the number after id in the URL, navigate to https://itunes.apple.com/lookup?id={number}, download the TXT file and search for bundleId. Alternatively rename .ipa to .zip, unzip, find softwareVersionBundleId in iTunesMetadata.plist.

symptom: Customer wants to use native Adobe Acrobat to open PDFs from Intune managed apps like Outlook/OneDrive. Customers often incorrectly use MAM Transfer Exceptions. This works for ENROLLED DEVICES ONLY.
solution: 1) Ensure device is fully enrolled into Intune MDM. 2) Deploy Adobe Acrobat Reader for Microsoft Intune app. 3) Add the app to MAM policy using correct Bundle ID. 4) When pushed via Intune, MAM policies will be applied.

symptom: Cannot apply Windows 10 Custom Setting to set IE DisableHomePageChange via OMA-URI. Result: (0x80004005) Unspecified error.
rootCause: Quotation marks in the policy value XML string are not in UTF-8 unicode (smart quotes instead of standard quotes).
solution: Edit the OMA-URI and modify its string value ensuring standard UTF-8 quotation marks are used in the XML.

symptom: Finding the App Bundle ID for iOS Apps. There is no way to directly look up bundle IDs in the Apple iOS App Store directly. To find the identifier, you will need to look at a file inside the app.

Sometimes a customer might find themselves wanting to utilize PDFs in a more efficient way than the built-in PDF reader in Intune managed applications. Customers often attempt to use MAM Transfer Exceptions to allow PDFs to be opened in Adobe Acrobat, which is not the right way. This scenario works for ENROLLED DEVICES ONLY.

After Hybrid Cloud Print is properly set up Intune is responsible for configuring the fields in the Intune Portal (shown below) and deploying them to Windows devices. Printer Discovery URL: <External Mopria Cloud Print URL here> Printer Access Authority URL: https://login.microsoftonline.com/<Azure AD Tenant ID here> Azure native client app GUID: <Native App ID here> Print service resource URI: http://MicrosoftEnterpriseCloudPrint/CloudPrint Maximum printers to query (mobile): <Up to customer, 10 is a safe default> Printer discovery service resource URI: http://MopriaDiscoveryService/CloudPrintExample screenshot of fields:  You can verify these values on targeted and synced Windows 10 MDM devices at the following registry location:  Computer\HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\current\SID VALUE\EnterpriseCloudPrintExample screenshot:

During
IOS enrollment using the Company Portal app, when it redirects to the Safari browser to install the
management profile, the device hangs at "Opening Settings".
rootCause: This can occur if JavaScript is turned off in Safari on the iOS device.This can be determined by opening Safari and typing portal.manage.microsoft.com in the search bar. You will see the following message:
solution:
To resolve this problem, turn
on JavaScript for the Safari browser on the iOS device. This is found under Settings -> Safari -> Advanced -> JavaScript.

symptom: After removing SCCM agent and enrolling into Intune, managed by is still showing as MDM/ConfigMgr.
After removing the Configuration Manager (SCCM) client agent and enrolling a Windows PC into Intune, the MDM authority shows to be SCCM/ConfigMgr.
rootCause: This is caused by a bug in the Configuration Manager 1710 RollUp 2 client. This is fixed in CM 1806.
solution: As a workaround, complete the following:1. Un-enroll the device from Intune.2. Delete the following Registry key: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\DeviceManageabilityCSP 3. Reboot the computer.4. Re-enroll the device in Intune.

symptom: When users are under Intune Read-Only operator role, they're not able to read anything in Intune, and receive the following error message in Azure Portal: { "error": { "code": "UnknownError", "message": ... Forbidden ... }
rootCause: The scope (groups) not targeting the group to which the user belongs to
solution: Include in the scope (groups) the group to which the user belongs.

symptom: When attempting to deploy Windows 10 devices in Kiosk mode automatically using AutoPilot, the deployment process fails at Setting up your device for work with the following error:Securing your hardware (Failed: 0x800705b4)Joining your organization's network (Previous step failed)Registering your device for mobile management (Previous step failed)
rootCause: This can occur if the targeted device does not meet both of the following prerequisites:- The device must have a physical TPM 2.0 chip. Note that devices with virtual TPMs (e.g. Hyper-V VMs) or those with TPM 1.2 chips won�t work with self-deploying mode.- The device must be running either:        - An Insider Preview build of Windows 10, build 17672 or later.        - Windows 10 1703 or later        - Windows 10 1809 or later (only if they're using a Hybrid Join profile)
solution: To resolve this problem, ensure that the targeted device meets both of these prerequisites.

symptom: We have released some new scripts on GitHub that will help validate various NDES/SCEP/PFX scenarios. Details on the scripts and the actions they perform are below.

You may get cases where a customer says that a remote password reset does not work for their Android devices, or that a work profile passcode reset on Android does not work. There can be some confusion surrounding what should work and what shouldn't, so hopefully the information below will help clarify the situation.

If a member of the field (TSP, PFE, etc) creates a Service Request and is asking for high-level Solution Provider support (example: My customer has 2500 licenses and wants to roll out Intune over the next few months, what's the best way to implement these 5 features in their environment?) please direct them to this process instead, as this type of support is out of our scope:

The requesting field engineer will need to:


 Fill
     out this form on behalf of the customer. This is Microsoft Internal information so do not send it to the customer.
 Email the form to emsiea@microsoft.com.

Example scenario, customer has configured a Device Restrictions Policy with the following setting: Policy expiration (days): 180 Customer is reporting that it has been significantly longer than 180 days since the user has reset their device password.
rootCause: *this is only currently confirmed for Android devices, if you are able to confirm this for iOS or Windows please reach out to mabutc@microsoft.com* Collect Company Portal Logs from the offending device and check the DiagnosticsInfo.log for these two values: Password Expiration Timeout: 15,552,000,000 Password Expiration Time On Device: 1,544,628,834,524   Password Expiration Timeout: 15,552,000,000 - This is a representation of the 180 day policy as configured by the policy in milliseconds Password Expiration Time On Device: 1,544,628,834,524 - This is a representation of the delta on the device since the timer started as a date Using a conversation calculator (https://www.timecalculator.net/milliseconds-to-date), 1,544,628,834,524 converts to Wed 12 December 2018 07:33:54, meaning that the current password on the device will expire on December 12th.
solution: The reason the user has not been prompted to change their password, is because the timer will reset every time you edit the policy - even if you don't touch the expiration value. If you create and assign a policy with this setting on January 1st to a device that is already enrolled, the timer will start and be expected to expire 180 days later on June 30th. However, if you modify the policy on June 29th, the timer will start over and now the new expiration time will be December 26th. The way to confirm this with the customer is to navigate to the policy in the Azure Portal and check the Last Modified field of the policy. If the device was enrolled prior to the Last Modified date, it should be 180 days after the date there. If the device was enrolled after the Last Modified date, it would expire 180 days after enrollment.

symptom: You can collect iOS Xcode/Console logs from a Windows PC. The link for the iOSLogInfo tool did not support iOS 11.3
rootCause: Old iOSLogInfo link did not support iOS 11.3, new link needed
solution: Use SDSiOSLogInfo from Blackberry.com. Connect iOS device via USB, open admin Command Prompt, run sdsiosloginfo.exe to capture console logs.

symptom: Automatic Enrollment fails - Info button does not show up for the work/school account when Configuration Manager client is installed. Windows 10 1703 or newer.
rootCause: ExternallyManaged registry key is set to 1
solution: Set ExternallyManaged to 0. On 1709+: HKLMSOFTWAREMicrosoftWindowsEnrollments. On 1703: HKLMSOFTWAREMicrosoftEnrollments.

symptom: MDM-enrolled devices become intermittently unenrolled. Device enrolling in MDM managed by on-prem AD with Windows RS3/RS4 certain updates.
rootCause: Bug in certain Windows RS3/RS4 updates causing unexpected MDM unenrollment
solution: Do not update until fix issued. Self-heal within 90 minutes. Workaround: run disable script before June Update, enable script after July Update.

symptom: Password expiration (days) setting for Android devices in Intune does not prompt user to change password after configured days.
rootCause: Timer resets every time the policy is edited, even without changing expiration value.
solution: Check policy Last Modified date in Azure Portal. Expiration is relative to Last Modified or enrollment date.

symptom: Moving Intune Exchange connector to new Exchange server fails with error: The Microsoft Intune Exchange Connector version is not supported.
rootCause: Existing Exchange connector is still installed.
solution: Remove the old connector first and then install the new connector.

symptom: Need to capture F12 Network Trace Logs (HAR traces) for investigating Endpoint Management portal issues.
solution: Press F12 in Edge, click Network tab, clear session, reproduce issue, save as HAR with content.

symptom: After installing Intune connector, devices never receive PKCS certificate. NDES connector log shows error 0x80070057 ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETER.
rootCause: Intune PKCS profile misconfigured - wrong Certification authority name or SAN configured for Email address with user lacking valid email.
solution: Correct PKCS profile configuration in Intune and target correct user group. Certificates will be received after policy refresh.

symptom: Configuring Intune On-Premise Exchange Connector fails with error: The Microsoft Intune Exchange Connector version that you are running is no longer supported. Error code 0x0000f. Log shows ConnectorVersionNotSupportedException.
rootCause: Caused by different/mismatched version of the Intune On-premise Exchange connector registered in the Intune service.
solution: Delete the existing connector from Intune Portal: Azure Portal > Intune > On-premises access > Exchange ActiveSync connectors > click three dots > Delete. Then reinstall/reconfigure the connector.

symptom: Custom packages for MacOS or PKG files must be wrapped or pre-processed with the Intune App Wrapping Tool for Mac (must be run on a MacOS workstation). This will wrap the file via the IntuneAppUtil with .intunemac file extension to be uploaded and deployed as Required or Available with enrollment. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/lob-apps-macos
rootCause: The package or .PKG file does not contain Increment package version and CFBundleVersion string in the packageinfo. The .pkg does not contain the correct install-path in the pkg-info file.
solution: Review the output of Detection.xml to extract the detection parameters and version for the created .Intunemac file. On a MacOS system with the .intunemac file run the command ./IntuneAppUtil -r <filename.intunemac> [-v] Example below for Skype For Business MacOS from https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=54108: ./IntuneAppUtil -r SkypeForBusinessMacOS.intunemacMicrosoft Intune Application Utility for Mac OS XVersion: 1.0.0.0Copyright 2018 Microsoft CorporationArchive: SkypeForBusinessMacOS.intunemac extracting: IntuneMacPackage/Metadata/Detection.xml <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?><PackageMetadata Version="1.0.0.0" TimeStamp="2018-05-18 20:38:20 +0000" MacOSX="10.13.4" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/Intune/2018/01/01/MACLOBAPP"><MacOSLobApp PackageType="pkg" PackageName="SkypeForBusinessInstaller-16.17.0.65.pkg" BundleId="com.microsoft.package.Microsoft_AutoUpdate.app" BuildNumber="3.8.16112200"><MD5HashChunkSize Size="10485760"/><MD5Hash>33a159eef753c90f522f54643b7055a6,f7becc593a7b4dcfbd41586789183211,aaa086b4eb85628356d2df1e7a81802b,445de9a759211d80eb548edaf081eb8e</MD5Hash><MacOSLobChildApp BundleId="com.microsoft.SkypeForBusiness" BuildNumber="16.17.65"/><MacOSLobChildApp BundleId="com.microsoft.autoupdate2" BuildNumber="3.8.1" VersionNumber="3.8.1"/></MacOSLobApp><InstallerParams VolumeInfo="/" RestartAction="None"/></PackageMetadata>If the custom package is missing the package version and/or CFBundleVersion string is not present then Intune won't be able to deploy the LOB successfully as they are required to correctly detect if the app is installed or not. You may also want to extract the pkg-info file check the install location of the application:Command line to extract a pkg file on Mac: xar -x -f <pkg file path> -C <output folder>install-location should be "/Applications" or its child folder <pkg-info identifier="com.microsoft.OneDrive" version="17.3.6760" install-location="/Applications"> <bundle path="./OneDrive.app" id="com.microsoft.OneDrive" CFBundleShortVersionString="17.3.6760" CFBundleVersion="6760.105"/>

symptom: When you enroll an iOS device via Apple's Device Enrollment Program with User Affinity, it will take 3 -12 hours for the following information to show in the portal "User Principal Name", "User Display Name", "Associated User".
rootCause: When the device is enrolled, there is no user information, so the device record created on DeviceProvider during enrollment session has empty Guid as EnrolledByUser and no UDA for the device at that time. When device is claimed by user, IWService will call action ClaimDevice on UserProvider to create the UDA, but the action wouldn�t update the EnrolledByUser of device record. In UDA BT, we will eventually update the EnrolledByUser of device record based on UDA, but it has delay range 0-12 hours. Why it was working before and not now: Previously, we join uda table in sql aggregation to get userid then use it to join user table to get those 3 attributes ("User Principal Name", "User Display Name", "Associated User") for get all devices. Since the UDA is created after device is claimed by user, it will get the correct value. Recently, to improve perf, we dropped uda table in our sql sproc and use EnrolledByUser property on DeviceProvider, which has 0-12 hours delay and surfaced as the issue customer is experiencing.
solution: Resolved: Code change to change\patch EnrolledByUser property on deviceProvider was released on 7/25/18

symptom: When a customer has setup a Compliance Policy, targeting Windows 10, that requires Secure Boot to be enabled, and it is showing as Non-Compliant, and the user cannot remediate this. The device, in the Admin Portal, might look like this: However, the device is fully enrolled, and running msinfo32 , the Secure Boot State is showing as On. However, when checking compliance, it fails and tells you that Secure Boot must be enabled.
rootCause: This is by-design.
solution: This is considered by-design. This feature uses something called Device Health Attestation. In a nutshell, DHA works like this: 1) The device enrolls, and Intune sends a DHA request to the device. 2) The device generates a security profile, encrypts it, and sends it to Intunes DHA service. 3) Intune evaluates the security profile, determines if what the device sends (settings such as Is Secure Boot Enabled?) and evaluates this against the compliance policy. 4) It will then mark a device as compliant/non-compliant based upon these findings. The limitation for this is on the Windows side, not Intune. Device Health Attestation requires a TPM 2.0 chip, and a UEFI 2.3.1 BIOS. If you only have a TPM 1.2 chip, for example, then you can enable Secure Boot but cannot report the status to the MDM provider via Device Health Attestation. o You can validate this by running tpm.msc o Eg; This is an Operating-System level requirement, and not something that Intune can control, sadly o https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/hardware-protection/tpm/trusted-platform-module-overview#device-health-attestation A good example is the HP EliteBook 840 G1 devices. These come with TPM 1.2 chipsets, which cannot report Secure Boot status. You can use the steps below to validate if a device is compatible. Run MSINFO as an Administrator (important for it to be elevated) Look under System Summary o The BIOS Mode needs to be UEFI , and PCR7 Configuration needs to be set to Bound . Run the PowerShell command (elevated): Confirm-SecureBootUEFI o o It needs to be True Check that the drive was successfully encrypted using PCR7 by running: manage-bde -protectors -get c: (or whichever drive is your OS) o If these values do not work, then your machine is not setup with Secure Boot using a TPM 2.0 chip. Its possible that your machine might have an option, in the BIOS, to enable TPM 2.0 if its using TPM 1.2 and is 2.0 compatible. Support should not evaluate this, its up to the customers & their device manufacturers. If this is the case (validated by using tpm.msc once upgraded), and its still not working, its possible a BIOS update might be needed to report the status back correctly if TPM 2.0 is available. Otherwise, as of now, this is not possible with a TPM 1.2 chip.

symptom: After configuring an App Protection Policy (MAM Policy) for Skype for Business and any other Office app, if you copy text from an Office app such as Outlook, Excel, Word or OneNote and paste it into Skype for Business, the text is pasted in with the markup tags\info added, like this:{\rtf1\ansi\ansicpg1252{\fonttbl\f0\fswiss\fcharset0 Helvetica;}{\colortbl;\red255\green255\bl �, by Charles Allen.{\rtf1\ansi\ansicpg1252{\fonttbl\f0\fswiss\fcharset0 Helvetica;}{\colortbl;\red255\green255\blue255;\red33\green33\blue33;}{\*\expandedcolortbl;;\cssrgb\c12941\c12941\c12941;}\deftab720\pard\pardeftab720\partightenfactor0\f0\fs29\fsmilli14667 \cf2 \expnd0\expndtw0\kerning0\outl0\strokewidth0 \strokec2 This is a line of text for copy paste.}
rootCause: This can occur if the App Protection Policy has the following setting:Restrict cut, copy, and paste with other apps = Policy Managed Apps with paste-in
solution: This issue was fixed in the 8.0.7 release of the Intune app SDK. Update Skype for Business to the latest version to resolve this issue

symptom: The following warning message is displayed when running "gpupdate/force" on a Hybrid AD joined device which is MDM enrolled already:Windows failed to apply the MDM Policy settings. MDM Policy settings might have its own log file. Please click on the "More information" link.First the "gpupdate/force" runs correctly with no error, then the above message is prompted:
rootCause: This can occur if an "AutoMDMEnrollment" GPO is deployed to the computer. In this scenario, when you run gpupdate/force it will try to enroll the Windows device. Since the device is already enrolled, this will fail. This is expected behavior.
solution: This message can be safely ignored. The error is expected considering that the device is already MDM enrolled.

symptom: As all Support Engineers know, finding a well-written solution in a KB article when searching in DfMK can make all the difference between a happy customer with a quick resolution, or a long, frustrating case with an unhappy customer. Properly capturing solutions in KB articles makes everyone�s job easier, so here are a few tips on how you should be documenting your solutions. IMPORTANT! Only create a new KB article after you search DfMK and determine that a similar article does not exist. Do not create duplicate articles or your article will be deleted.

The product team recently released an additional summary section to the Azure Active Directory portal that allows you to identify which conditional access policy is being applied to a particular user. This can be helpful in scenarios where a customer is unable to access a specific cloud resource such as Exchange or SharePoint Online, or is getting blocked by a conditional access policy to enroll their device with Intune.

Attempting to install ndesconnector.exe on a server using a V2 certificate fails with Microsoft Intune Connector Setup Wizard ended prematurely. SetupMsi.log shows: CustomAction ConfigureIISLimits returned actual error code 1168, WriteIIS7ConfigChanges Error 0x80070002: Site not found for create application, WriteIIS7ConfigChanges Failed, CustomAction WriteIIS7ConfigChanges returned actual error code 1603.
rootCause: This can occur if the Default Web Site name has been changed to something else, for example Default Web Site1.
solution: Change the site name back to the default which is Default Web Site.

symptom: When attempting to add a Windows 10 Line Of Business (LOB) app, you receive the error: File extension for fileName is not present in the allowed file extensions list - ips,apk,msi,xap,appx,appxbundle,intunemac. You may also be unable to select the download app package file in the UI.
rootCause: This can occur if the downloaded app package file does not have a valid extension.
solution: Give the download app package file a file extension of APPX, or whatever file extension is appropriate for your specific package, then try adding the LOB app again.

symptom: In August 2018, Google announced the release of Android P (Android 9 Pie). The Intune product team has been testing each preview, and currently all existing Intune MDM and Intune App Protection Policy (MAM policy) scenarios appear to be compatible.

After creating and assigning a device security policy where Required Password or Require alphanumeric Password or Minimum Password length is selected, a user device may not prompt for password creation. The devices may still show as compliant in the Intune company portal.
rootCause: Intune: This problem was fixed by removing the option to create this password type. Office 365 MDM: Expected to be addressed with the next version of Office 365 MDM.
solution: Office 365 MDM: Use Office 365 MDM powershell to set the Password Quality value.

symptom: Apple VPP token shows Assigned to external MDM or assignedToExternalMDM or ClientContextMismatchDetected in Microsoft Endpoint Manager admin center under Tenant administration > Connectors and tokens > Apple VPP tokens. Rave hostname value shows a non-Microsoft MDM service. Kusto VppFeatureTelemetry or IntuneEvent with EventUniqueName ClientContextMismatchDetected confirms the conflicting MDM.
rootCause: The same VPP location token was uploaded in two or more device management solutions. A location token can only be used with one device management solution at a time. The error may recur as long as the token exists in multiple MDMs due to sync cycles.
solution: Identify the conflicting MDM. If token needed in both MDMs: keep existing token in one MDM, delete from the other, create new Location in ABM/ASM for the other MDM, upload new VPP token. If only needed in Intune: remove token from other MDM, force VPP token sync in Intune.

symptom: With the deprecation of MAM CA policies in the Intune App Protection blade, users cannot access new apps added to the Approved Client apps list (e.g. Planner, To Do, Launcher) even though excluded from the CA policy in Azure, while older apps like Word, Excel, Outlook still work.
Customers will be impacted if they are using F5 Access VPN profiles targeted to iOS. These iOS devices will lose VPN access to F5 VPN when they upgrade to iOS 12
rootCause: F5 has announced that F5 Access 2.1 and earlier, also known as F5 Access Legacy, will not work on iOS 12. While F5 has released a new version, F5 Access 3.0 and higher (also known as F5 Access 2018) which does support iOS 12, we are not adding support for this version of F5 Access to hybrid mobile device management (MDM) as hybrid MDM has been deprecated . Support for this new iOS F5 Access VPN client is being added to Intune standalone and will be released soon. This impacts only iOS.
solution: Migrate their iOS devices to Intune on Azure as soon as possible so they can set up VPN profiles for the new VPN client. If they are unable to migrate their devices before iOS 12 is released (anticipated in mid-September), and they plan to continue to use F5 VPN with iOS 12 devices, there is a workaround available. Tyler Castaldo <tycast@microsoft.com> is the point of contact to help with the workaround.

symptom: Starting with the Intune Managed Browser version 1.2.8, diagnosing issues with all Intune App Protection Policy enrolled apps (also known as MAM-enrolled apps) on iOS is easier than ever. The Intune Diagnostic Console streamlines log collection and submission, and with the tap of a button it lets users send logs for any MAM-enrolled app to Microsoft Support as part of a helpdesk ticket so you can quickly identify issues and troubleshoot MAM policy enforcement. For details on how to use this feature see below.

Role Based Access Control (RBAC) offers much greater flexibility and control to ensure your IT administrators have the right permissions to perform their job, and no more. Below is a walk-through of some of the features of RBAC, plus information that will help you understand how Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) Directory Roles are supported by Intune.

In the Microsoft Intune on Azure administration console, there is a Discovered apps node available for each MDM enrolled device. There has been some confusion around what you can expect to see in here so the information below has been provided in an effort to promote clarity.

In this article we share details on service-impacting incident response and service health for Intune. While we always want the service to be 100% available, there are infrequent times when we experience a service incident. The goal of this article is to explain the experience. Below we walk through the following: Who is working on an incident How we detect a service-impacting incident Where you go to check on the status of service-impacting incidents Where we post on service changes By design, bugs, and other service non-incidents Other ways to get service notices

There is some confusion regarding the CNAME configuration required for Windows devices to automatically discover the MDM server for mobile device management (MDM). We have also had questions about the MDM server address users have to enter manually if prompted. This article hopes to help explain the requirements.

On Windows 10 devices, an administrator can create a block list for Microsoft Store apps and prevent the Store from updating these apps when updates are triggered. This is accomplished by using the DoNotUpdate setting under the AppStore node in the Enterprise App Management CSP.

Consider the following scenario:The NDES connector and server are running normally and the SCEP URL works as expected on the NDES server.A SCEP profile is setup with the correct parameters and is tied to a Trusted Root profile correctly.The client receives the profile correctly from Intune, but the SCEP certificate fails to install.You MAY see an error on the client but it will likely be vague and point to the SCEP certificate failed to install without a good reason.The HTTPS certificate appears to be valid on the CA and is properly configured in the IIS settings of the NDES server.No other errors are present on the NDES server.No IIS traffic is observed on the NDES server.The certificate chain for the SSL certificate looks valid with no issues.
rootCause: This can occur if the certificate uploaded to the Trusted Root (TR) profile in Intune that the SCEP profile was using is different than the trusted root certificate installed on the NDES server. Note that the issue is not with the SSL certificate, but rather that the client is unable to validate the certificate chain because the Trusted Root profile it pulled down from Intune was different than the NDES SSL certificate chain.Is this your issue?You can confirm this is your issue by going to https://<SCEPurl>/?operation=GetCACaps on the client device. If this is your issue you will see an SSL failure. Most browsers will initially say something about the SSL certificate not being trusted, and in in �More Details� you will see the error ERROR_WINHTTP_SECURE_FAILURE.
solution: To resolve this problem, upload the current Trusted Root certificate from the CA to the Trusted Root profile in Intune, target it to the appropriate users, and then make sure the SCEP profile is also pointing to that Trusted Root profile and is targeted to the same users. Once the users obtain the new, updated policy, the SCEP certificate should install successfully.

symptom: When deploying Intune managed iOS store apps with assignment types of Required, Available for enrolled devices or Available with or without enrollment, a Face ID/Touch ID authentication prompt appears, attempts authentication, then fails with the message �The <app name> could not be installed�.
rootCause: This can occur if the user is using iOS 11.3.x - 12.x, and the device has enabled �Use Touch ID for iTunes and App Store� in Settings. This is a known bug in iOS.
solution: This issue has been fixed by Apple in iOS 12 Beta 6. To resolve this problem, update the iOS device to iOS 12.x when released. As a work around, temporarily disable the setting 'Use Touch ID for iTunes and App Store' on the iOS device.

symptom: When sending a remote lock to MAC OS X devices enrolled into Intune, the expectation is that the device will lock. Once the lock happens, the Intune service will post a 6-digit PIN code into the Intune on Azure portal. This PIN is needed to unlock the device.You may notice that when a remote lock has been issued to a MAC OS X device, the Lock fails to complete and reports a status of Pending or Failed into the Intune on Azure portal. Also, the device gets stuck at the login screen asking for a PIN. However, there is no PIN code visible in the Intune on Azure portal and as a result the end user is effectively locked out from his device with no options of bypassing the prompt.
rootCause: This is a known issue with the current release of Intune.
solution:
To work around this issue, look up the affected
device ID in Rave or Graph, then retrieve the PIN code using F12 Developer
Tools. Steps to do this are below.

 

1. Launch Edge.

2. Login to https://portal.azure.com
with your global administrator account.

3. On the Intune blade, go to Devices -> All
Devices.

4. In the upper right, go to the Edge
Settings menu, then select Developer Tools from the
list. This will bring up the Dev Tools.

5. Click the Play icon
in the upper left.

6. Go to the Network tab.

7. Return to the Azure Intune portal
and select the impacted device.

8. Navigate from the Overview tab
to Properties, then go back to the Overview tab.
This will cause the Dev tools to pull in the remote action
details.

9. Return to Dev Tools.

10. On the left in the Name column,
find entries that start with manageddevices(�Actual Device ID. Below
is an example:

 



 

11. Highlight the first entry.

12. On the right, go to Body tab.

13. Under Response Body look
for a message that starts with the following:

 

 {"@odata.context":"https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/$metadata#deviceManagement/managedDevices( deviceActionResults...

 

If the message is different, move on
to the next entry that starts with manageddevices(�Actual Device
ID and continue the search.

 

14. Once you locate the entry, copy
the complete text and paste it into a text editor. It will resemble this:

15. Use this PIN to unlock the
device.

symptom: We often get questions relating to the Apple MDM push certificate � also known as the Apple Push Notification service (APNs) certificate - and how it plays a role in managing iOS devices. We have a lot of great documentation on this, for example our article here, but there are many other general questions and issues that don�t necessarily fall into any specific category. That�s where this KB article comes in. See the section below for a general Intune FAQ and some common solutions.

Recently, Intune released some features that will make understanding and troubleshooting Intune App installation issues much easier. For example, we now display installation issues per device, and will display an end-to-end history of the app right up to the point of the install failure. But that begs the questions - When we report a failure with a red cross, how do you solve the problem being reported? To help, here is a list of common error codes, some info on potential root causes, and the next steps in troubleshooting them. Keep in mind that all of the error codes below are client side errors (i.e., iOS/Android/Windows returning an error) so information can sometimes be limited. If you are aware of any other error codes and potential solutions not contained here, please let us know.

Using runbooks is an easy and efficient way to run the GetMessagesToTeamsAndOutlook PowerShell script shared in the TechNet Gallery. In this article we show how to use an Azure runbook to run this script on a timer.Before you run this script using runbooks, you�ll want to follow directions in the following KB articles to get values to put into the script above:4459968: This article outlines how to create an AzureAD application.4460075: Here you will find directions to get Webhook URIs for Teams and Outlook.After you�re done setting this up, you can set up an Azure Automation account, create an Azure runbook, and automate the process using the steps below.

Intune posts messages in the Message Center in Office 365, accessed from portal.office.com using your admin credentials. These messages contain important service information with links to more details about new features, updates made to the service, and upcoming changes that help customers prepare for any planned change we make. While you can read all of these messages in the Message Center, and even sign up to get weekly digests of the messages delivered as emails, this article describes an alternate way of accessing these messages using PowerShell. This process takes only a few minutes to set up and has two parts:Creating an Azure AD application to use O365 APIsRunning a PowerShell script that we�ve provided for your convenienceSee below for the complete list of steps.

In another KB article (4460074) we showed how you could pull in all your Intune messages into a PowerShell Window. Now as a follow up, we'll share how to get these messages into a Teams channel or delivered as emails into your Outlook inbox. The script also has the option to automate delivery of messages on a timer using an Azure function. We have an updated version of that script here: GetMessagesToTeamsAndOutlook.See below for the complete steps.

Microsoft Teams is a platform for chat based communication that includes a connector that allows for an easily pluggable extension for Microsoft Teams to integrate notifications in to discussions. This creates an opportunity to integrate datacenter and cloud operations practices with real time discussions. Among the available connectors in the list, you will find Incoming Webhook. This provides an easy solution to post notifications from any scripting language through JSON formatted web service call. See below for details.

We briefly outline where we communicate information about service changes in our product documentation and in KB 4459822. While it is helpful to deep dive into service health � both planned maintenance and unplanned downtime � it is equally interesting to demystify Intune service change � planned new features and changes to existing features. In this article we dive into Intune service change and share how you can best stay informed of Intune service change communications. We also include information on multiple ways you can get service change messages from the Office Message Center. See below for details.

Intune now supports Cisco AnyConnect 4.0.7 and higher for iOS. In this article we provide more information on the process of switching from Cisco Legacy AnyConnect (4.0.5 and earlier) to the new AnyConnect app and also document one known issue related to NAC. See below for details.

You may notice the following on MDM-managed iOS devices running version 11.3 and later:End users cannot create or edit contacts from within Outlook on an MDM managed device.Outlook's Save Contacts feature (exports contacts from Outlook to the native OS contacts app to facilitate caller-id, text messaging, etc.) no longer functions and existing contacts that had been previously exported prior to the upgrade to 11.3 are no longer accessible via unmanaged apps.
rootCause: This can occur if there is an iOS device restriction policy where either �Viewing corporate documents in unmanaged apps� or "Viewing non-corporate documents in corporate apps" set to Block. This causes contacts to be inaccessible by unmanaged apps.This is because with iOS 11.3, Apple introduced a new security feature that changed the way mobile device management (MDM) works with the native contacts app. Apple now prevents contacts in managed accounts from being used in unmanaged apps/accounts. This new security feature changes how MDM providers (not just Intune, EAS, or MDM for Office 365, but all MDM providers) integrate with the native contacts app in iOS 11.3.
solution: The following steps can be followed to configure policies in Intune to make this scenario work:Login with your Intune service credentials at https://portal.azure.com and head to the Intune blade.Select Device Configuration, then Managed Profiles.Find your iOS Device Restriction Profile.4. Select the policy and more information will open. Under Manage Properties check what settings are configured in the Device restrictions for iOS App Store, Doc Viewing, Gaming category.5. If either "Viewing corporate documents in unmanaged apps� or "Viewing non-corporate documents in corporate apps" is set to Block, this will prevent Contact Sync from working. Make sure both are set to Not Configured.Alternatively, if you still want to block unmanaged apps data transfer, you can still do so through an applicable Intune App Protection policy setting.To do this, head to Manage Mobile Apps.Select App Protection Policies and create a policy setting. Going this route would ensure that only corporate identities in the approved apps can access corporate data. You can add in apps that support Intune App protection policies or those you choose to exempt.Known Issue with App Protection Policies and Mobile Device ManagementWe do have one known issue that we�re currently working on a fix for, but the scenario is quite specific. For devices that received App Protection Policies settings that were configured in Intune Classic console (the Silverlight console) and deployed through Mobile Device Management, the existing workaround steps will leave Org data in an unprotected state. This will affect users that enrolled prior to migration to Intune in the Azure console.There are two options, for customers in this situation and please know we are actively working on other options:End users unenroll and reenroll with Microsoft Intune � this will reset the APP state pushed through MDM and allow the existing workaround steps to continue to protect the Org Data.Wait for a product update from Microsoft � this will take the form of an Intune APP SDK for iOS update and application updates to release with the updated SDK. The Intune APP SDK update is planned for release in Q2 2018. We will update this post when the SDK is released; just note it will take a bit longer then for the apps to adopt the new SDK. Additional InformationThis new feature has been discussed on Apple�s forum here: https://discussions.apple.com/thread/8338871.The specific release notes for this new feature are posted here: https://developer.apple.com/library/content/releasenotes/General/RN-iOS-11.3/index.html and also cut and paste below from Apple�s site for your awareness.Mobile Device ManagementAdded new configuration settings for device management. For details of the new settings, see the Configuration Profile Reference and the MDM Protocol Reference.Prevent unmanaged apps from accessing contacts in managed accounts.For more information on configuring Intune App protection head to the documentation here: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/app-protection-policyThis is documented publicly at  https://blogs.technet.microsoft.com/intunesupport/2018/04/17/support-tip-ios-11-3-and-native-contacts-app/

symptom: On an iOS device, when the screen is locked device configuration profiles may not be deployed to the iOS devices successfully. However, if you unlock the device you will see the profiles deployed successfully immediately. this occurs even though there is an Internet connection available when device is locked and �Background App Refesh� is turned on.When looking in the Azure Portal, iOS devices will show non-compliant with error "2016341112 -iOS device is currently busy"If you check in Kusto, you will see a response of NotNow from the device for the policy settings in question.
rootCause: This occurs because the iOS device is busy and unable to complete evaluation or enforcement of the policy. Typically this error is seen when a device has been powered on and connected to the internet, but has not yet been unlocked by the user.
solution: This is a function of the iOS platform. Once the device is unlocked the policy will be applied.

symptom: We thought it would be helpful to share with you some details about the flow for VPN profile installation on Android devices. Most clients like Cisco, SonicWall, Check Point, Citrix and F5 Networks have a setting that prevents external control by outside applications unless the end user explicitly enables it. This setting needs to be enabled in order to receive VPN profiles coming from Intune.

In many organizations, it�s very common to allow end users to use both Intune MDM managed devices (corporate owned devices for example) and unmanaged devices protected with only Intune App Protection Policies (BYO scenarios for example).As Intune App Protection Policies (aka MAM policies) are targeted to a user�s identity, the protection settings for a user traditionally apply to both enrolled (MDM managed) and non-enrolled devices (no MDM).In the March 2018 Intune update we added the ability to target an Intune App Protection Policy to either Intune enrolled or un-enrolled iOS and Android devices. This means you can have one protection policy for unmanaged devices in which strict App Protection controls are in place, and a separate protection policy for MDM managed devices where the App Protection controls may be a little more relaxed. This provides the best possible end-user experience based on the device enrollment state, while giving the IT Pro more control based on their business requirements.For details on how to configure these policies see below.

Starting with iOS 11, all iOS devices, no matter where they have been purchased, can be enrolled via DEP. This article describes the steps required to enroll these devices in Intune.Here are some of the requirements:An Apple DEP account, to create a new account, go to deploy.apple.com and create your program account. You will need to provide an email address associated with your business. If you have an existing DEP account, please skip this stepA macOS with Apple Configurator2 Version 2.5 or above installedAn iOS device with iOS 11 or aboveFor the complete list of steps, please see below.

We hear from a lot of customers that they want to automatically clean up devices that have not checked in with Intune during the past 90 days. Since Intune is built on top of Graph API, we can automate this pretty easily. See below for complete details.

Beginning in April 2018, Company Portal users will be able to upload Intune-related logs directly to Microsoft with the tap of a button, and view an incident ID that can be used to refer to the uploaded logs when contacting Microsoft Support. Intune-related logs will no longer be attached to emails when users request help from company support using iOS and Android Company Portal apps. The benefitsUsers blocked by Conditional Access for email can provide logs without configuring another email account.Users will no longer need to configure an email account for providing logs.You will no longer need to deploy an email app within Work Profile for providing logs.You only need to provide the 8-character incident ID to Microsoft Support to look up uploaded logs.Intune ensures that device logs are always contained within GDPR-compliant systems.Details of the end user experience are below.

In February 2018, Intune introduced a new experience to make it easier for customers to manage iOS device enrollment through Apple�s Device Enrollment Program (DEP), Apple School Manager (ASM), or Apple Configurator. With support for multi-token DEP, Intune aims to address scenarios where you would have multiple tokens, for example, when you are purchasing devices from several DEP resellers, have multiple DEP accounts or are migrating devices from other MDM vendors.This new release will provide a richer experience while basic functionality remains the same. You will be able to assign enrollment profiles, push configurations and enroll devices as before, while being able to manage devices in groups separated by tokens.As part of this new experience, to support modern authentication like Multi-factor authentication (MFA), admins will be given the option to authenticate with Company Portal instead of Apple Setup Assistant when enrolling devices with user affinity. With this option, end users will be asked to enter their credentials in the Company Portal app that will be automatically installed.NOTE Admins can provision Company Portal during DEP enrollment as a VPP app or an App Store app. AppleID is not required for enrolling devices with user affinity and authenticating with company portal when using VPP. To use VPP upgrade, indicate which VPP token should be used in enrollment profile and users will no longer be prompted for Apple ID. If the token expires or runs out of device licenses for the Company Portal app, Intune will install App Store Company Portal which will prompt for Apple ID.More information about enrolling iOS devices with Apple�s enrollment programs can be found at Enroll iOS devices in Intune. Click on the relevant link on that page to see screenshots of the current and new user interface (UI).More information on the user experience and Graph APIs can be found below.

Intune now supports Cisco AnyConnect 4.0.7 and higher for iOS. In this article we provide more information on the process of switching from Cisco Legacy AnyConnect (4.0.5 and earlier) to the new AnyConnect app and also document one known issue related to NAC.
You may notice the following on MDM-managed iOS devices running version 11.3 and later: End users cannot edit or save Outlook contacts.
rootCause: This can occur if there is an iOS device restriction policy where either Viewing corporate documents in unmanaged apps or Viewing non-corporate documents in corporate apps set to Block.
solution: Configure policies in Intune: Log in to Azure Portal > Intune > Device Configuration > Profiles. Set Viewing corporate documents in unmanaged apps to Not configured and Viewing non-corporate documents in corporate apps to Not configured.

symptom: On an iOS device, when the screen is locked device configuration profiles may not be deployed successfully. iOS devices will show non-compliant with error 2016341112 -iOS device is currently busy in the Azure Portal.
rootCause: This occurs because the iOS device is busy and unable to complete evaluation or enforcement of the policy. Typically this error is seen when a device has been powered on and connected to the internet, but has not yet been unlocked by the user.
solution: This is a function of the iOS platform. Once the device is unlocked the policy will be applied and the device will become compliant.

If you target the Skype for Business (SfB) app with an Intune App Protection Policy (MAM policy) the policies are not applied successfully.
rootCause: This can occur if "Hybrid Modern Authentication" for Hybrid and On-Premises Skype for Business has not been enabled in the customers environment. In May 2017, a Skype for Business Server 2015 Cumulative Update was released, enabling "Hybrid Modern Authentication" for Hybrid and On-Premises Skype for Business customers.Modern Authentication allows customers to enable many modern security features, such as Azure Active Directory Conditional Access or multi-factor authentication. It also enables the Intune App Protection features for the Skype for Business iOS and Android apps.If you target the Skype for Business app with App Protection policies and Modern Auth is not enabled, your App Protection policies will not apply successfully.
solution: To resolve this problem, enable hybrid modern authentication. To enable Hybrid Modern Auth, use the steps outlined in the following guide:https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/Skype-for-Business-Blog/Hybrid-Modern-Authentication-for-Skype-for-Business/ba-p/134751Once all prerequisites have been met and all steps have been completed, you can target the customer's Skype for Business policies.

symptom: If you are a customer who is managing Android Enterprise devices with a work profile (aka Android for Work, or AfW), there is a specific, one-time task that you need to perform in order to ensure that the Intune Company Portal app continues to receive automatic updates from the managed Google Play store. If this is not done, the Company Portal app may not receive updates. This could result in Company Portal falling out of date and potentially missing important bug fixes or feature updates. See below for details on how to do this.

Windows 10 MDM auto-enrollment into Intune via GPO fails and the following symptoms are observed:- The Task Scheduler generates an error on the \Microsoft\Windows\EnterpriseMgmt\Schedule
created by enrollment client for automatically enrolling in MDM from AAD task. The last run result is:Event 76 Auto MDM Enroll: Failed (Unknown Win32 Error code: 0x8018002b)- In the Event Viewer, under Applications and
Services Logs -> Microsoft -> Windows ->
DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider -> Admin, you may also see the
following event:

Log Name:
Microsoft-Windows-DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider/Admin

Source:
DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider

Event ID: 76

Level: Error

Description:
Auto MDM Enroll: Failed (Unknown
Win32 Error code: 0x80180002b)
rootCause: This can occur if the domain for the UPN is either unverified or unroutable. For example, it could be that the UPN is an internal (unresolvable) UPN. Some examples are below.Invalidcontoso\userxuserx@contoso.localValiduserx@contoso.com
solution: To resolve this problem, complete the following:1.	Open Active Directory Users and Computers2.	Select the user object for the affected user3.	Open Properties and select the Account tab.4.	Modify the UPN suffix, changing it to a valid value (e.g. from contoso.local. to contoso.com)Once complete, either wait for the next synchronization to occur, or force a Delta Sync from the Synchronization Server using the following steps:1. Launch an Administrative PowerShell.2. Run Import-Module ADSync3. Run Start-ADSyncSyncCycle -PolicyType DeltaThese commands will kick off a Delta Sync. Once the sync completes, the account should now reflect the updated domain within Azure.
